This is a list of some of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the shrines in the region of みなべ町. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ_Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-List of Kujūku Ōji (みなべ町)-qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ_Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-List of Kujūku Ōji (みなべ町)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:九十九王子 (みなべ町)]] ==Overview== This article discusses the '''{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}''' (九十九王子) located within {{ill|Minabe, Wakayama|ja|みなべ町}}, {{ill|Hidaka District, Wakayama|ja|日高郡 (和歌山県)}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}.
== What Are the Kujūku Ōji? == ''For more details, see {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}.''
The Kujūku Ōji (九十九王子) refers to a group of {{ill|Shinto shrines|ja|神社}} located along the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道}}, particularly on the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} and {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路}} routes. These shrines were rapidly organized primarily during the 12th to 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験}}, who served as guides for imperial family members and nobles on pilgrimages to Kumano, with prayers for the protection of pilgrims.
However, after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|ja|承久の乱}} in 1221, pilgrimages from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}} declined, leading to the deterioration and abandonment of the shrines along the Kiiji route. This trend accelerated after the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, as Kumano pilgrimages lost their prominence. Despite some restoration efforts during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩}}, the shrines did not regain their former status. Further changes during the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}} due to the {{ill|State Shinto|ja|国家神道}} reforms, shrine mergers, and urbanization led to the loss of original shrine sites, with some locations now unknown.
This article covers the Ōji shrines with identified sites in Minabe Town, Wakayama Prefecture.
== Kujūku Ōji in Minabe Town == There are three Ōji shrines within Minabe Town:
=== Iwashiro Ōji === [[File:Iwashiro-Oji Shrine.jpg|thumb|Iwashiro Ōji]] Located near the mouth of the Nishi-Iwashiro River facing the coast, the '''Iwashiro Ōji''' (岩代王子) shrine site is identified. Some historical sources such as the ''{{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja|和歌山県聖蹟}}'' place it here, while others like the ''{{ill|Kii Fudoki Continued|ja|紀伊続風土記}}'' identify a nearby Tenjin Shrine as the original site. There is a monument nearby related to {{ill|Prince Arima|ja|有間皇子}}. It is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 1558 Nishi-Iwashiro, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Senri Ōji === [[File:Senri-Oji01s2040.jpg|thumb|220px|Senri Ōji]] The '''Senri Ōji''' (千里王子) is located along the coast of {{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜}}. It has a monument commemorating {{ill|Emperor Kazan|ja|花山天皇}}’s poetry and is adjacent to Senri Kannon Temple. It was merged into {{ill|Suga Shrine (Minabe)|ja|須賀神社}} in 1909 but remains well-preserved. The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Minabe Ōji === [[File:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|Minabe Ōji]] The '''Minabe Ōji''' (三鍋王子) is located near {{ill|Minabe Station|ja|南部駅}}. Historical records mention it as a site for religious offerings during imperial pilgrimages. It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe)|ja|鹿島神社}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
== Surrounding Information == * '''{{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜}}''': A significant ritual site where pilgrims performed {{ill|Misogi|ja|禊}} purification rites. * '''Senri Kannon''': A Buddhist hall originally associated with Senri Ōji, now a separate temple after the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令}} during the Meiji era. * '''Ancient Pilgrimage Path (Senri Ōji to Minabe Ōji)''': A well-preserved ancient road, recently designated as a cultural property in 2022. * '''Nanbu Pass and Jizō Statue''': The pass was historically significant and features a {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} statue believed to protect against fractures.
<gallery> File:Senri Beach 02.jpg|Senri no Hama File:Kiiji - Senri Ōji.jpg|Senri Ōji with seashell offerings File:Senri Kannon.jpg|Senri Kannon File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 01.jpg|Stone Buddhas along the ancient path File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 03.jpg|Pilgrimage path through private plum orchards File:Nanbu Pass.jpg|Old Nanbu Pass File:Minabe-touge01s2048.jpg|Jizō statue at Nanbu Pass </gallery>

This is a list of some of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the shrines in the region of みなべ町. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ_Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-List of Kujūku Ōji (みなべ町)-qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ_Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-List of Kujūku Ōji (みなべ町)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:九十九王子 (みなべ町)]] ==Overview== This article discusses the '''{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}''' (九十九王子) located within {{ill|Minabe, Wakayama|ja|みなべ町}}, {{ill|Hidaka District, Wakayama|ja|日高郡 (和歌山県)}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}.
== What Are the Kujūku Ōji? == ''For more details, see {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}.''
The Kujūku Ōji (九十九王子) refers to a group of {{ill|Shinto shrines|ja|神社}} located along the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道}}, particularly on the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} and {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路}} routes. These shrines were rapidly organized primarily during the 12th to 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験}}, who served as guides for imperial family members and nobles on pilgrimages to Kumano, with prayers for the protection of pilgrims.
However, after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|ja|承久の乱}} in 1221, pilgrimages from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}} declined, leading to the deterioration and abandonment of the shrines along the Kiiji route. This trend accelerated after the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, as Kumano pilgrimages lost their prominence. Despite some restoration efforts during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩}}, the shrines did not regain their former status. Further changes during the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}} due to the {{ill|State Shinto|ja|国家神道}} reforms, shrine mergers, and urbanization led to the loss of original shrine sites, with some locations now unknown.
This article covers the Ōji shrines with identified sites in Minabe Town, Wakayama Prefecture.
== Kujūku Ōji in Minabe Town == There are three Ōji shrines within Minabe Town:
=== Iwashiro Ōji === [[File:Iwashiro-Oji Shrine.jpg|thumb|Iwashiro Ōji]] Located near the mouth of the Nishi-Iwashiro River facing the coast, the '''Iwashiro Ōji''' (岩代王子) shrine site is identified. Some historical sources such as the ''{{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja|和歌山県聖蹟}}'' place it here, while others like the ''{{ill|Kii Fudoki Continued|ja|紀伊続風土記}}'' identify a nearby Tenjin Shrine as the original site. There is a monument nearby related to {{ill|Prince Arima|ja|有間皇子}}. It is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 1558 Nishi-Iwashiro, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Senri Ōji === [[File:Senri-Oji01s2040.jpg|thumb|220px|Senri Ōji]] The '''Senri Ōji''' (千里王子) is located along the coast of {{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜}}. It has a monument commemorating {{ill|Emperor Kazan|ja|花山天皇}}’s poetry and is adjacent to Senri Kannon Temple. It was merged into {{ill|Suga Shrine (Minabe)|ja|須賀神社}} in 1909 but remains well-preserved. The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Minabe Ōji === [[File:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|Minabe Ōji]] The '''Minabe Ōji''' (三鍋王子) is located near {{ill|Minabe Station|ja|南部駅}}. Historical records mention it as a site for religious offerings during imperial pilgrimages. It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe)|ja|鹿島神社}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
== Surrounding Information == * '''{{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜}}''': A significant ritual site where pilgrims performed {{ill|Misogi|ja|禊}} purification rites. * '''Senri Kannon''': A Buddhist hall originally associated with Senri Ōji, now a separate temple after the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令}} during the Meiji era. * '''Ancient Pilgrimage Path (Senri Ōji to Minabe Ōji)''': A well-preserved ancient road, recently designated as a cultural property in 2022. * '''Nanbu Pass and Jizō Statue''': The pass was historically significant and features a {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} statue believed to protect against fractures.
<gallery> File:Senri Beach 02.jpg|Senri no Hama File:Kiiji - Senri Ōji.jpg|Senri Ōji with seashell offerings File:Senri Kannon.jpg|Senri Kannon File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 01.jpg|Stone Buddhas along the ancient path File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 03.jpg|Pilgrimage path through private plum orchards File:Nanbu Pass.jpg|Old Nanbu Pass File:Minabe-touge01s2048.jpg|Jizō statue at Nanbu Pass </gallery>

This is a list of some of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the shrines in the region of みなべ町. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ_Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-List of Kujūku Ōji (みなべ町)-qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ_Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-List of Kujūku Ōji (みなべ町)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:九十九王子 (みなべ町)]] ==Overview== This article discusses the '''{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}''' (九十九王子) located within {{ill|Minabe, Wakayama|ja|みなべ町}}, {{ill|Hidaka District, Wakayama|ja|日高郡 (和歌山県)}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}.
== What Are the Kujūku Ōji? == ''For more details, see {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}.''
The Kujūku Ōji (九十九王子) refers to a group of {{ill|Shinto shrines|ja|神社}} located along the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道}}, particularly on the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} and {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路}} routes. These shrines were rapidly organized primarily during the 12th to 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験}}, who served as guides for imperial family members and nobles on pilgrimages to Kumano, with prayers for the protection of pilgrims.
However, after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|ja|承久の乱}} in 1221, pilgrimages from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}} declined, leading to the deterioration and abandonment of the shrines along the Kiiji route. This trend accelerated after the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, as Kumano pilgrimages lost their prominence. Despite some restoration efforts during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩}}, the shrines did not regain their former status. Further changes during the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}} due to the {{ill|State Shinto|ja|国家神道}} reforms, shrine mergers, and urbanization led to the loss of original shrine sites, with some locations now unknown.
This article covers the Ōji shrines with identified sites in Minabe Town, Wakayama Prefecture.
== Kujūku Ōji in Minabe Town == There are three Ōji shrines within Minabe Town:
=== Iwashiro Ōji === [[File:Iwashiro-Oji Shrine.jpg|thumb|Iwashiro Ōji]] Located near the mouth of the Nishi-Iwashiro River facing the coast, the '''Iwashiro Ōji''' (岩代王子) shrine site is identified. Some historical sources such as the ''{{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja|和歌山県聖蹟}}'' place it here, while others like the ''{{ill|Kii Fudoki Continued|ja|紀伊続風土記}}'' identify a nearby Tenjin Shrine as the original site. There is a monument nearby related to {{ill|Prince Arima|ja|有間皇子}}. It is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 1558 Nishi-Iwashiro, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Senri Ōji === [[File:Senri-Oji01s2040.jpg|thumb|220px|Senri Ōji]] The '''Senri Ōji''' (千里王子) is located along the coast of {{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜}}. It has a monument commemorating {{ill|Emperor Kazan|ja|花山天皇}}’s poetry and is adjacent to Senri Kannon Temple. It was merged into {{ill|Suga Shrine (Minabe)|ja|須賀神社}} in 1909 but remains well-preserved. The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Minabe Ōji === [[File:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|Minabe Ōji]] The '''Minabe Ōji''' (三鍋王子) is located near {{ill|Minabe Station|ja|南部駅}}. Historical records mention it as a site for religious offerings during imperial pilgrimages. It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe)|ja|鹿島神社}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
== Surrounding Information == * '''{{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜}}''': A significant ritual site where pilgrims performed {{ill|Misogi|ja|禊}} purification rites. * '''Senri Kannon''': A Buddhist hall originally associated with Senri Ōji, now a separate temple after the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令}} during the Meiji era. * '''Ancient Pilgrimage Path (Senri Ōji to Minabe Ōji)''': A well-preserved ancient road, recently designated as a cultural property in 2022. * '''Nanbu Pass and Jizō Statue''': The pass was historically significant and features a {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} statue believed to protect against fractures.
<gallery> File:Senri Beach 02.jpg|Senri no Hama File:Kiiji - Senri Ōji.jpg|Senri Ōji with seashell offerings File:Senri Kannon.jpg|Senri Kannon File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 01.jpg|Stone Buddhas along the ancient path File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 03.jpg|Pilgrimage path through private plum orchards File:Nanbu Pass.jpg|Old Nanbu Pass File:Minabe-touge01s2048.jpg|Jizō statue at Nanbu Pass </gallery>

This is a list of some of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the shrines in the region of みなべ町. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ_Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-List of Kujūku Ōji (みなべ町)-qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ_Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-List of Kujūku Ōji (みなべ町)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:九十九王子 (みなべ町)]] ==Overview== This article discusses the '''{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}''' (九十九王子) located within {{ill|Minabe, Wakayama|ja|みなべ町}}, {{ill|Hidaka District, Wakayama|ja|日高郡 (和歌山県)}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}.
== What Are the Kujūku Ōji? == ''For more details, see {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}.''
The Kujūku Ōji (九十九王子) refers to a group of {{ill|Shinto shrines|ja|神社}} located along the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道}}, particularly on the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} and {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路}} routes. These shrines were rapidly organized primarily during the 12th to 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験}}, who served as guides for imperial family members and nobles on pilgrimages to Kumano, with prayers for the protection of pilgrims.
However, after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|ja|承久の乱}} in 1221, pilgrimages from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}} declined, leading to the deterioration and abandonment of the shrines along the Kiiji route. This trend accelerated after the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, as Kumano pilgrimages lost their prominence. Despite some restoration efforts during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩}}, the shrines did not regain their former status. Further changes during the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}} due to the {{ill|State Shinto|ja|国家神道}} reforms, shrine mergers, and urbanization led to the loss of original shrine sites, with some locations now unknown.
This article covers the Ōji shrines with identified sites in Minabe Town, Wakayama Prefecture.
== Kujūku Ōji in Minabe Town == There are three Ōji shrines within Minabe Town:
=== Iwashiro Ōji === [[File:Iwashiro-Oji Shrine.jpg|thumb|Iwashiro Ōji]] Located near the mouth of the Nishi-Iwashiro River facing the coast, the '''Iwashiro Ōji''' (岩代王子) shrine site is identified. Some historical sources such as the ''{{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja|和歌山県聖蹟}}'' place it here, while others like the ''{{ill|Kii Fudoki Continued|ja|紀伊続風土記}}'' identify a nearby Tenjin Shrine as the original site. There is a monument nearby related to {{ill|Prince Arima|ja|有間皇子}}. It is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 1558 Nishi-Iwashiro, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Senri Ōji === [[File:Senri-Oji01s2040.jpg|thumb|220px|Senri Ōji]] The '''Senri Ōji''' (千里王子) is located along the coast of {{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜}}. It has a monument commemorating {{ill|Emperor Kazan|ja|花山天皇}}’s poetry and is adjacent to Senri Kannon Temple. It was merged into {{ill|Suga Shrine (Minabe)|ja|須賀神社}} in 1909 but remains well-preserved. The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Minabe Ōji === [[File:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|Minabe Ōji]] The '''Minabe Ōji''' (三鍋王子) is located near {{ill|Minabe Station|ja|南部駅}}. Historical records mention it as a site for religious offerings during imperial pilgrimages. It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe)|ja|鹿島神社}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
== Surrounding Information == * '''{{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜}}''': A significant ritual site where pilgrims performed {{ill|Misogi|ja|禊}} purification rites. * '''Senri Kannon''': A Buddhist hall originally associated with Senri Ōji, now a separate temple after the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令}} during the Meiji era. * '''Ancient Pilgrimage Path (Senri Ōji to Minabe Ōji)''': A well-preserved ancient road, recently designated as a cultural property in 2022. * '''Nanbu Pass and Jizō Statue''': The pass was historically significant and features a {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} statue believed to protect against fractures.
<gallery> File:Senri Beach 02.jpg|Senri no Hama File:Kiiji - Senri Ōji.jpg|Senri Ōji with seashell offerings File:Senri Kannon.jpg|Senri Kannon File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 01.jpg|Stone Buddhas along the ancient path File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 03.jpg|Pilgrimage path through private plum orchards File:Nanbu Pass.jpg|Old Nanbu Pass File:Minabe-touge01s2048.jpg|Jizō statue at Nanbu Pass </gallery>

This is a list of some of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the shrines in the region of みなべ町. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ_Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-List of Kujūku Ōji (みなべ町)-qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ_Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-List of Kujūku Ōji (みなべ町)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:九十九王子 (みなべ町)]] ==Overview== This article discusses the '''{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}''' (九十九王子) located within {{ill|Minabe, Wakayama|ja|みなべ町}}, {{ill|Hidaka District, Wakayama|ja|日高郡 (和歌山県)}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}.
== What Are the Kujūku Ōji? == ''For more details, see {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}.''
The Kujūku Ōji (九十九王子) refers to a group of {{ill|Shinto shrines|ja|神社}} located along the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道}}, particularly on the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} and {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路}} routes. These shrines were rapidly organized primarily during the 12th to 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験}}, who served as guides for imperial family members and nobles on pilgrimages to Kumano, with prayers for the protection of pilgrims.
However, after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|ja|承久の乱}} in 1221, pilgrimages from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}} declined, leading to the deterioration and abandonment of the shrines along the Kiiji route. This trend accelerated after the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, as Kumano pilgrimages lost their prominence. Despite some restoration efforts during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩}}, the shrines did not regain their former status. Further changes during the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}} due to the {{ill|State Shinto|ja|国家神道}} reforms, shrine mergers, and urbanization led to the loss of original shrine sites, with some locations now unknown.
This article covers the Ōji shrines with identified sites in Minabe Town, Wakayama Prefecture.
== Kujūku Ōji in Minabe Town == There are three Ōji shrines within Minabe Town:
=== Iwashiro Ōji === [[File:Iwashiro-Oji Shrine.jpg|thumb|Iwashiro Ōji]] Located near the mouth of the Nishi-Iwashiro River facing the coast, the '''Iwashiro Ōji''' (岩代王子) shrine site is identified. Some historical sources such as the ''{{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja|和歌山県聖蹟}}'' place it here, while others like the ''{{ill|Kii Fudoki Continued|ja|紀伊続風土記}}'' identify a nearby Tenjin Shrine as the original site. There is a monument nearby related to {{ill|Prince Arima|ja|有間皇子}}. It is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 1558 Nishi-Iwashiro, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Senri Ōji === [[File:Senri-Oji01s2040.jpg|thumb|220px|Senri Ōji]] The '''Senri Ōji''' (千里王子) is located along the coast of {{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜}}. It has a monument commemorating {{ill|Emperor Kazan|ja|花山天皇}}’s poetry and is adjacent to Senri Kannon Temple. It was merged into {{ill|Suga Shrine (Minabe)|ja|須賀神社}} in 1909 but remains well-preserved. The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Minabe Ōji === [[File:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|Minabe Ōji]] The '''Minabe Ōji''' (三鍋王子) is located near {{ill|Minabe Station|ja|南部駅}}. Historical records mention it as a site for religious offerings during imperial pilgrimages. It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe)|ja|鹿島神社}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
== Surrounding Information == * '''{{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜}}''': A significant ritual site where pilgrims performed {{ill|Misogi|ja|禊}} purification rites. * '''Senri Kannon''': A Buddhist hall originally associated with Senri Ōji, now a separate temple after the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令}} during the Meiji era. * '''Ancient Pilgrimage Path (Senri Ōji to Minabe Ōji)''': A well-preserved ancient road, recently designated as a cultural property in 2022. * '''Nanbu Pass and Jizō Statue''': The pass was historically significant and features a {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} statue believed to protect against fractures.
<gallery> File:Senri Beach 02.jpg|Senri no Hama File:Kiiji - Senri Ōji.jpg|Senri Ōji with seashell offerings File:Senri Kannon.jpg|Senri Kannon File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 01.jpg|Stone Buddhas along the ancient path File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 03.jpg|Pilgrimage path through private plum orchards File:Nanbu Pass.jpg|Old Nanbu Pass File:Minabe-touge01s2048.jpg|Jizō statue at Nanbu Pass </gallery>

revision.comment: fixing some common errors in [[Category:Kujūku Ōji template forks]]
revision.text:
This is a list of some of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the shrines in the region of みなべ町. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ_Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-List of Kujūku Ōji (みなべ町)-qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ_Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-List of Kujūku Ōji (みなべ町)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:九十九王子 (みなべ町)]] ==Overview== This article discusses the '''{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}}''' (九十九王子) located within {{ill|Minabe, Wakayama|ja|みなべ町}}, {{ill|Hidaka District, Wakayama|ja|日高郡 (和歌山県)}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}.
== What Are the Kujūku Ōji? == ''For more details, see {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}}.''
The Kujūku Ōji (九十九王子) refers to a group of {{ill|Shinto shrines|ja|神社}} located along the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}}, particularly on the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} and {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路}} routes. These shrines were rapidly organized primarily during the 12th to 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験}}, who served as guides for imperial family members and nobles on pilgrimages to Kumano, with prayers for the protection of pilgrims.
However, after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|ja|承久の乱}} in 1221, pilgrimages from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}} declined, leading to the deterioration and abandonment of the shrines along the Kiiji route. This trend accelerated after the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, as Kumano pilgrimages lost their prominence. Despite some restoration efforts during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩}}, the shrines did not regain their former status. Further changes during the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}} due to the {{ill|State Shinto|ja|国家神道}} reforms, shrine mergers, and urbanization led to the loss of original shrine sites, with some locations now unknown.
This article covers the Ōji shrines with identified sites in Minabe Town, Wakayama Prefecture.
== Kujūku Ōji in Minabe Town == There are three Ōji shrines within Minabe Town:
=== Iwashiro Ōji === [[File:Iwashiro-Oji Shrine.jpg|thumb|Iwashiro Ōji]] Located near the mouth of the Nishi-Iwashiro River facing the coast, the '''Iwashiro Ōji''' (岩代王子) shrine site is identified. Some historical sources such as the ''{{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja|和歌山県聖蹟}}'' place it here, while others like the ''{{ill|Kii Fudoki Continued|ja|紀伊続風土記}}'' identify a nearby Tenjin Shrine as the original site. There is a monument nearby related to {{ill|Prince Arima|ja|有間皇子}}. It is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 1558 Nishi-Iwashiro, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Senri Ōji === [[File:Senri-Oji01s2040.jpg|thumb|220px|Senri Ōji]] The '''Senri Ōji''' (千里王子) is located along the coast of {{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜}}. It has a monument commemorating {{ill|Emperor Kazan|ja|花山天皇}}’s poetry and is adjacent to Senri Kannon Temple. It was merged into {{ill|Suga Shrine (Minabe)|ja|須賀神社}} in 1909 but remains well-preserved. The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Minabe Ōji === [[File:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|Minabe Ōji]] The '''Minabe Ōji''' (三鍋王子) is located near {{ill|Minabe Station|ja|南部駅}}. Historical records mention it as a site for religious offerings during imperial pilgrimages. It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe)|ja|鹿島神社}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
== Surrounding Information == * '''{{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜}}''': A significant ritual site where pilgrims performed {{ill|Misogi|ja|禊}} purification rites. * '''Senri Kannon''': A Buddhist hall originally associated with Senri Ōji, now a separate temple after the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令}} during the Meiji era. * '''Ancient Pilgrimage Path (Senri Ōji to Minabe Ōji)''': A well-preserved ancient road, recently designated as a cultural property in 2022. * '''Nanbu Pass and Jizō Statue''': The pass was historically significant and features a {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} statue believed to protect against fractures.
<gallery> File:Senri Beach 02.jpg|Senri no Hama File:Kiiji - Senri Ōji.jpg|Senri Ōji with seashell offerings File:Senri Kannon.jpg|Senri Kannon File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 01.jpg|Stone Buddhas along the ancient path File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 03.jpg|Pilgrimage path through private plum orchards File:Nanbu Pass.jpg|Old Nanbu Pass File:Minabe-touge01s2048.jpg|Jizō statue at Nanbu Pass </gallery>
== References == {{reflist|2}}
== Further Reading == * Hasegawa, Yasutaka (2007). *Kumano Ōji Pilgrimage Guidebook*. Shinpu Shobo. ISBN 9784882696292 * Nishi, Ritsu (1987). *Kumano Kodo Guide: Current Survey of the Kujūku Ōji Shrines*. Arao Seibundō. * Heibonsha (1983). *Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture*. Heibonsha.
== Related Topics == {{Commonscat|Kujuku-ojis|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}}
== External Links == * [http://www.aikis.or.jp/~minabe/kumano.html Kumano Kodo] - Minabe Town Tourism Association * [http://www.town.minabe.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkzai.html Minabe Town Cultural Properties]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[List of Kujūku Ōji (みなべ町)]] to [[List of Kujūku Ōji (Minabe Town)]]: Text replacement - "List of Kujūku Ōji (みなべ町)" to "List of Kujūku Ōji (Minabe Town)"
revision.text:
This is a list of some of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the shrines in the region of みなべ町. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ_Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-List of Kujūku Ōji (みなべ町)-qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ_Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-List of Kujūku Ōji (みなべ町)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:九十九王子 (みなべ町)]] ==Overview== This article discusses the '''{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}}''' (九十九王子) located within {{ill|Minabe, Wakayama|ja|みなべ町}}, {{ill|Hidaka District, Wakayama|ja|日高郡 (和歌山県)}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}.
== What Are the Kujūku Ōji? == ''For more details, see {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}}.''
The Kujūku Ōji (九十九王子) refers to a group of {{ill|Shinto shrines|ja|神社}} located along the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}}, particularly on the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} and {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路}} routes. These shrines were rapidly organized primarily during the 12th to 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験}}, who served as guides for imperial family members and nobles on pilgrimages to Kumano, with prayers for the protection of pilgrims.
However, after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|ja|承久の乱}} in 1221, pilgrimages from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}} declined, leading to the deterioration and abandonment of the shrines along the Kiiji route. This trend accelerated after the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, as Kumano pilgrimages lost their prominence. Despite some restoration efforts during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩}}, the shrines did not regain their former status. Further changes during the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}} due to the {{ill|State Shinto|ja|国家神道}} reforms, shrine mergers, and urbanization led to the loss of original shrine sites, with some locations now unknown.
This article covers the Ōji shrines with identified sites in Minabe Town, Wakayama Prefecture.
== Kujūku Ōji in Minabe Town == There are three Ōji shrines within Minabe Town:
=== Iwashiro Ōji === [[File:Iwashiro-Oji Shrine.jpg|thumb|Iwashiro Ōji]] Located near the mouth of the Nishi-Iwashiro River facing the coast, the '''Iwashiro Ōji''' (岩代王子) shrine site is identified. Some historical sources such as the ''{{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja|和歌山県聖蹟}}'' place it here, while others like the ''{{ill|Kii Fudoki Continued|ja|紀伊続風土記}}'' identify a nearby Tenjin Shrine as the original site. There is a monument nearby related to {{ill|Prince Arima|ja|有間皇子}}. It is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 1558 Nishi-Iwashiro, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Senri Ōji === [[File:Senri-Oji01s2040.jpg|thumb|220px|Senri Ōji]] The '''Senri Ōji''' (千里王子) is located along the coast of {{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜}}. It has a monument commemorating {{ill|Emperor Kazan|ja|花山天皇}}’s poetry and is adjacent to Senri Kannon Temple. It was merged into {{ill|Suga Shrine (Minabe)|ja|須賀神社}} in 1909 but remains well-preserved. The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Minabe Ōji === [[File:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|Minabe Ōji]] The '''Minabe Ōji''' (三鍋王子) is located near {{ill|Minabe Station|ja|南部駅}}. Historical records mention it as a site for religious offerings during imperial pilgrimages. It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe)|ja|鹿島神社}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
== Surrounding Information == * '''{{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜}}''': A significant ritual site where pilgrims performed {{ill|Misogi|ja|禊}} purification rites. * '''Senri Kannon''': A Buddhist hall originally associated with Senri Ōji, now a separate temple after the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令}} during the Meiji era. * '''Ancient Pilgrimage Path (Senri Ōji to Minabe Ōji)''': A well-preserved ancient road, recently designated as a cultural property in 2022. * '''Nanbu Pass and Jizō Statue''': The pass was historically significant and features a {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} statue believed to protect against fractures.
<gallery> File:Senri Beach 02.jpg|Senri no Hama File:Kiiji - Senri Ōji.jpg|Senri Ōji with seashell offerings File:Senri Kannon.jpg|Senri Kannon File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 01.jpg|Stone Buddhas along the ancient path File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 03.jpg|Pilgrimage path through private plum orchards File:Nanbu Pass.jpg|Old Nanbu Pass File:Minabe-touge01s2048.jpg|Jizō statue at Nanbu Pass </gallery>
== References == {{reflist|2}}
== Further Reading == * Hasegawa, Yasutaka (2007). *Kumano Ōji Pilgrimage Guidebook*. Shinpu Shobo. ISBN 9784882696292 * Nishi, Ritsu (1987). *Kumano Kodo Guide: Current Survey of the Kujūku Ōji Shrines*. Arao Seibundō. * Heibonsha (1983). *Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture*. Heibonsha.
== Related Topics == {{Commonscat|Kujuku-ojis|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}}

revision.text:
This is a list of some of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the ōji Shrines in the region of {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Minabe Town)|ja|みなべ町}} <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ_Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-List of Kujūku Ōji (みなべ町)-qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ_Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-List of Kujūku Ōji (みなべ町)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:九十九王子 (みなべ町)]] ==Overview== This article discusses the '''{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}}''' (九十九王子) located within {{ill|Minabe, Wakayama|ja|みなべ町}}, {{ill|Hidaka District, Wakayama|ja|日高郡 (和歌山県)}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}.
== What Are the Kujūku Ōji? == ''For more details, see {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}}.''
The Kujūku Ōji (九十九王子) refers to a group of {{ill|Shinto shrines|ja|神社}} located along the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}}, particularly on the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} and {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路}} routes. These shrines were rapidly organized primarily during the 12th to 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験}}, who served as guides for imperial family members and nobles on pilgrimages to Kumano, with prayers for the protection of pilgrims.
However, after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|ja|承久の乱}} in 1221, pilgrimages from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}} declined, leading to the deterioration and abandonment of the shrines along the Kiiji route. This trend accelerated after the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, as Kumano pilgrimages lost their prominence. Despite some restoration efforts during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩}}, the shrines did not regain their former status. Further changes during the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}} due to the {{ill|State Shinto|ja|国家神道}} reforms, shrine mergers, and urbanization led to the loss of original shrine sites, with some locations now unknown.
This article covers the Ōji shrines with identified sites in Minabe Town, Wakayama Prefecture.
== Kujūku Ōji in Minabe Town == There are three Ōji shrines within Minabe Town:
=== Iwashiro Ōji === [[File:Iwashiro-Oji Shrine.jpg|thumb|Iwashiro Ōji]] Located near the mouth of the Nishi-Iwashiro River facing the coast, the '''Iwashiro Ōji''' (岩代王子) shrine site is identified. Some historical sources such as the ''{{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja|和歌山県聖蹟}}'' place it here, while others like the ''{{ill|Kii Fudoki Continued|ja|紀伊続風土記}}'' identify a nearby Tenjin Shrine as the original site. There is a monument nearby related to {{ill|Prince Arima|ja|有間皇子}}. It is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 1558 Nishi-Iwashiro, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Senri Ōji === [[File:Senri-Oji01s2040.jpg|thumb|220px|Senri Ōji]] The '''Senri Ōji''' (千里王子) is located along the coast of {{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜}}. It has a monument commemorating {{ill|Emperor Kazan|ja|花山天皇}}’s poetry and is adjacent to Senri Kannon Temple. It was merged into {{ill|Suga Shrine (Minabe)|ja|須賀神社}} in 1909 but remains well-preserved. The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Minabe Ōji === [[File:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|Minabe Ōji]] The '''Minabe Ōji''' (三鍋王子) is located near {{ill|Minabe Station|ja|南部駅}}. Historical records mention it as a site for religious offerings during imperial pilgrimages. It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe)|ja|鹿島神社}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
== Surrounding Information == * '''{{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜}}''': A significant ritual site where pilgrims performed {{ill|Misogi|ja|禊}} purification rites. * '''Senri Kannon''': A Buddhist hall originally associated with Senri Ōji, now a separate temple after the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令}} during the Meiji era. * '''Ancient Pilgrimage Path (Senri Ōji to Minabe Ōji)''': A well-preserved ancient road, recently designated as a cultural property in 2022. * '''Nanbu Pass and Jizō Statue''': The pass was historically significant and features a {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} statue believed to protect against fractures.
<gallery> File:Senri Beach 02.jpg|Senri no Hama File:Kiiji - Senri Ōji.jpg|Senri Ōji with seashell offerings File:Senri Kannon.jpg|Senri Kannon File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 01.jpg|Stone Buddhas along the ancient path File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 03.jpg|Pilgrimage path through private plum orchards File:Nanbu Pass.jpg|Old Nanbu Pass File:Minabe-touge01s2048.jpg|Jizō statue at Nanbu Pass </gallery>
== References == {{reflist|2}}
== Further Reading == * Hasegawa, Yasutaka (2007). *Kumano Ōji Pilgrimage Guidebook*. Shinpu Shobo. ISBN 9784882696292 * Nishi, Ritsu (1987). *Kumano Kodo Guide: Current Survey of the Kujūku Ōji Shrines*. Arao Seibundō. * Heibonsha (1983). *Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture*. Heibonsha.
== Related Topics == {{Commonscat|Kujuku-ojis|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}}
== External Links == * [http://www.aikis.or.jp/~minabe/kumano.html Kumano Kodo] - Minabe Town Tourism Association * [http://www.town.minabe.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkzai.html Minabe Town Cultural Properties]

This is a list of some of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the ōji Shrines in the region of {{ill|Minabe Town|ja|みなべ町}} <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ_Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-みなべ町)-qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ_Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-みなべ町)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:九十九王子 (みなべ町)]] ==Overview== This article discusses the '''{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}}''' (九十九王子) located within {{ill|Minabe, Wakayama|ja|みなべ町}}, {{ill|Hidaka District, Wakayama|ja|日高郡 (和歌山県)}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}.
== What Are the Kujūku Ōji? == ''For more details, see {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}}.''
The Kujūku Ōji (九十九王子) refers to a group of {{ill|Shinto shrines|ja|神社}} located along the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}}, particularly on the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} and {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路}} routes. These shrines were rapidly organized primarily during the 12th to 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験}}, who served as guides for imperial family members and nobles on pilgrimages to Kumano, with prayers for the protection of pilgrims.
However, after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|ja|承久の乱}} in 1221, pilgrimages from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}} declined, leading to the deterioration and abandonment of the shrines along the Kiiji route. This trend accelerated after the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, as Kumano pilgrimages lost their prominence. Despite some restoration efforts during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩}}, the shrines did not regain their former status. Further changes during the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}} due to the {{ill|State Shinto|ja|国家神道}} reforms, shrine mergers, and urbanization led to the loss of original shrine sites, with some locations now unknown.
This article covers the Ōji shrines with identified sites in Minabe Town, Wakayama Prefecture.
== Kujūku Ōji in Minabe Town == There are three Ōji shrines within Minabe Town:
=== Iwashiro Ōji === [[File:Iwashiro-Oji Shrine.jpg|thumb|Iwashiro Ōji]] Located near the mouth of the Nishi-Iwashiro River facing the coast, the '''Iwashiro Ōji''' (岩代王子) shrine site is identified. Some historical sources such as the ''{{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja|和歌山県聖蹟}}'' place it here, while others like the ''{{ill|Kii Fudoki Continued|ja|紀伊続風土記}}'' identify a nearby Tenjin Shrine as the original site. There is a monument nearby related to {{ill|Prince Arima|ja|有間皇子}}. It is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 1558 Nishi-Iwashiro, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Senri Ōji === [[File:Senri-Oji01s2040.jpg|thumb|220px|Senri Ōji]] The '''Senri Ōji''' (千里王子) is located along the coast of {{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜}}. It has a monument commemorating {{ill|Emperor Kazan|ja|花山天皇}}’s poetry and is adjacent to Senri Kannon Temple. It was merged into {{ill|Suga Shrine (Minabe|ja|須賀神社}} in 1909 but remains well-preserved. The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Minabe Ōji === [[File:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|Minabe Ōji]] The '''Minabe Ōji''' (三鍋王子) is located near {{ill|Minabe Station|ja|南部駅}}. Historical records mention it as a site for religious offerings during imperial pilgrimages. It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe|ja|鹿島神社}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
== Surrounding Information == * '''{{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜}}''': A significant ritual site where pilgrims performed {{ill|Misogi|ja|禊}} purification rites. * '''Senri Kannon''': A Buddhist hall originally associated with Senri Ōji, now a separate temple after the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令}} during the Meiji era. * '''Ancient Pilgrimage Path (Senri Ōji to Minabe Ōji)''': A well-preserved ancient road, recently designated as a cultural property in 2022. * '''Nanbu Pass and Jizō Statue''': The pass was historically significant and features a {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} statue believed to protect against fractures.
<gallery> File:Senri Beach 02.jpg|Senri no Hama File:Kiiji - Senri Ōji.jpg|Senri Ōji with seashell offerings File:Senri Kannon.jpg|Senri Kannon File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 01.jpg|Stone Buddhas along the ancient path File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 03.jpg|Pilgrimage path through private plum orchards File:Nanbu Pass.jpg|Old Nanbu Pass File:Minabe-touge01s2048.jpg|Jizō statue at Nanbu Pass </gallery>

revision.comment: Text replacement - "== Related Topics ==" to "== See Also =="
revision.text:
This is a list of some of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the ōji Shrines in the region of {{ill|Minabe Town|ja|みなべ町}} <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ_Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-みなべ町)-qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ_Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-みなべ町)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:九十九王子 (みなべ町)]] ==Overview== This article discusses the '''{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}}''' (九十九王子) located within {{ill|Minabe, Wakayama|ja|みなべ町}}, {{ill|Hidaka District, Wakayama|ja|日高郡 (和歌山県)}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}.
== What Are the Kujūku Ōji? == ''For more details, see {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}}.''
The Kujūku Ōji (九十九王子) refers to a group of {{ill|Shinto shrines|ja|神社}} located along the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}}, particularly on the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} and {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路}} routes. These shrines were rapidly organized primarily during the 12th to 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験}}, who served as guides for imperial family members and nobles on pilgrimages to Kumano, with prayers for the protection of pilgrims.
However, after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|ja|承久の乱}} in 1221, pilgrimages from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}} declined, leading to the deterioration and abandonment of the shrines along the Kiiji route. This trend accelerated after the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, as Kumano pilgrimages lost their prominence. Despite some restoration efforts during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩}}, the shrines did not regain their former status. Further changes during the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}} due to the {{ill|State Shinto|ja|国家神道}} reforms, shrine mergers, and urbanization led to the loss of original shrine sites, with some locations now unknown.
This article covers the Ōji shrines with identified sites in Minabe Town, Wakayama Prefecture.
== Kujūku Ōji in Minabe Town == There are three Ōji shrines within Minabe Town:
=== Iwashiro Ōji === [[File:Iwashiro-Oji Shrine.jpg|thumb|Iwashiro Ōji]] Located near the mouth of the Nishi-Iwashiro River facing the coast, the '''Iwashiro Ōji''' (岩代王子) shrine site is identified. Some historical sources such as the ''{{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja|和歌山県聖蹟}}'' place it here, while others like the ''{{ill|Kii Fudoki Continued|ja|紀伊続風土記}}'' identify a nearby Tenjin Shrine as the original site. There is a monument nearby related to {{ill|Prince Arima|ja|有間皇子}}. It is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 1558 Nishi-Iwashiro, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Senri Ōji === [[File:Senri-Oji01s2040.jpg|thumb|220px|Senri Ōji]] The '''Senri Ōji''' (千里王子) is located along the coast of {{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜}}. It has a monument commemorating {{ill|Emperor Kazan|ja|花山天皇}}’s poetry and is adjacent to Senri Kannon Temple. It was merged into {{ill|Suga Shrine (Minabe|ja|須賀神社}} in 1909 but remains well-preserved. The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Minabe Ōji === [[File:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|Minabe Ōji]] The '''Minabe Ōji''' (三鍋王子) is located near {{ill|Minabe Station|ja|南部駅}}. Historical records mention it as a site for religious offerings during imperial pilgrimages. It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe|ja|鹿島神社}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
== Surrounding Information == * '''{{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜}}''': A significant ritual site where pilgrims performed {{ill|Misogi|ja|禊}} purification rites. * '''Senri Kannon''': A Buddhist hall originally associated with Senri Ōji, now a separate temple after the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令}} during the Meiji era. * '''Ancient Pilgrimage Path (Senri Ōji to Minabe Ōji)''': A well-preserved ancient road, recently designated as a cultural property in 2022. * '''Nanbu Pass and Jizō Statue''': The pass was historically significant and features a {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} statue believed to protect against fractures.
<gallery> File:Senri Beach 02.jpg|Senri no Hama File:Kiiji - Senri Ōji.jpg|Senri Ōji with seashell offerings File:Senri Kannon.jpg|Senri Kannon File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 01.jpg|Stone Buddhas along the ancient path File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 03.jpg|Pilgrimage path through private plum orchards File:Nanbu Pass.jpg|Old Nanbu Pass File:Minabe-touge01s2048.jpg|Jizō statue at Nanbu Pass </gallery>
== References == {{reflist|2}}
== Further Reading == * Hasegawa, Yasutaka (2007). *Kumano Ōji Pilgrimage Guidebook*. Shinpu Shobo. ISBN 9784882696292 * Nishi, Ritsu (1987). *Kumano Kodo Guide: Current Survey of the Kujūku Ōji Shrines*. Arao Seibundō. * Heibonsha (1983). *Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture*. Heibonsha.
== See Also == {{Commonscat|Kujuku-ojis|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}}
== External Links == * [http://www.aikis.or.jp/~minabe/kumano.html Kumano Kodo] - Minabe Town Tourism Association * [http://www.town.minabe.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkzai.html Minabe Town Cultural Properties]

This is a list of some of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the ōji Shrines in the region of {{ill|Minabe Town|ja|みなべ町}} <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-みなべ町)-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-みなべ町)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:九十九王子 (みなべ町)]] ==Overview== This article discusses the '''{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}}''' (九十九王子) located within {{ill|Minabe, Wakayama|ja|みなべ町}}, {{ill|Hidaka District, Wakayama|ja|日高郡 (和歌山県)}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}.
== What Are the Kujūku Ōji? == ''For more details, see {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}}.''
The Kujūku Ōji (九十九王子) refers to a group of {{ill|Shinto shrines|ja|神社}} located along the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}}, particularly on the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} and {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路}} routes. These shrines were rapidly organized primarily during the 12th to 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験}}, who served as guides for imperial family members and nobles on pilgrimages to Kumano, with prayers for the protection of pilgrims.
However, after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|ja|承久の乱}} in 1221, pilgrimages from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}} declined, leading to the deterioration and abandonment of the shrines along the Kiiji route. This trend accelerated after the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, as Kumano pilgrimages lost their prominence. Despite some restoration efforts during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩}}, the shrines did not regain their former status. Further changes during the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}} due to the {{ill|State Shinto|ja|国家神道}} reforms, shrine mergers, and urbanization led to the loss of original shrine sites, with some locations now unknown.
This article covers the Ōji shrines with identified sites in Minabe Town, Wakayama Prefecture.
== Kujūku Ōji in Minabe Town == There are three Ōji shrines within Minabe Town:
=== Iwashiro Ōji === [[File:Iwashiro-Oji Shrine.jpg|thumb|Iwashiro Ōji]] Located near the mouth of the Nishi-Iwashiro River facing the coast, the '''Iwashiro Ōji''' (岩代王子) shrine site is identified. Some historical sources such as the ''{{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja|和歌山県聖蹟}}'' place it here, while others like the ''{{ill|Kii Fudoki Continued|ja|紀伊続風土記}}'' identify a nearby Tenjin Shrine as the original site. There is a monument nearby related to {{ill|Prince Arima|ja|有間皇子}}. It is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 1558 Nishi-Iwashiro, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Senri Ōji === [[File:Senri-Oji01s2040.jpg|thumb|220px|Senri Ōji]] The '''Senri Ōji''' (千里王子) is located along the coast of {{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜}}. It has a monument commemorating {{ill|Emperor Kazan|ja|花山天皇}}’s poetry and is adjacent to Senri Kannon Temple. It was merged into {{ill|Suga Shrine (Minabe|ja|須賀神社}} in 1909 but remains well-preserved. The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Minabe Ōji === [[File:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|Minabe Ōji]] The '''Minabe Ōji''' (三鍋王子) is located near {{ill|Minabe Station|ja|南部駅}}. Historical records mention it as a site for religious offerings during imperial pilgrimages. It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe|ja|鹿島神社}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
== Surrounding Information == * '''{{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜}}''': A significant ritual site where pilgrims performed {{ill|Misogi|ja|禊}} purification rites. * '''Senri Kannon''': A Buddhist hall originally associated with Senri Ōji, now a separate temple after the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令}} during the Meiji era. * '''Ancient Pilgrimage Path (Senri Ōji to Minabe Ōji)''': A well-preserved ancient road, recently designated as a cultural property in 2022. * '''Nanbu Pass and Jizō Statue''': The pass was historically significant and features a {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} statue believed to protect against fractures.
<gallery> File:Senri Beach 02.jpg|Senri no Hama File:Kiiji - Senri Ōji.jpg|Senri Ōji with seashell offerings File:Senri Kannon.jpg|Senri Kannon File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 01.jpg|Stone Buddhas along the ancient path File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 03.jpg|Pilgrimage path through private plum orchards File:Nanbu Pass.jpg|Old Nanbu Pass File:Minabe-touge01s2048.jpg|Jizō statue at Nanbu Pass </gallery>

This is a list of some of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the ōji Shrines in the region of {{ill|Minabe Town|ja|みなべ町}} <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-みなべ町)-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-みなべ町)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:九十九王子 (みなべ町)]] ==Overview== This article discusses the '''{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}}''' (九十九王子) located within {{ill|Minabe, Wakayama|ja|みなべ町}}, {{ill|Hidaka District, Wakayama|ja|日高郡 (和歌山県)}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}.
== What Are the Kujūku Ōji? == ''For more details, see {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}}.''
The Kujūku Ōji (九十九王子) refers to a group of {{ill|Shinto shrines|ja|神社}} located along the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}}, particularly on the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} and {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路}} routes. These shrines were rapidly organized primarily during the 12th to 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験}}, who served as guides for imperial family members and nobles on pilgrimages to Kumano, with prayers for the protection of pilgrims.
However, after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|ja|承久の乱}} in 1221, pilgrimages from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}} declined, leading to the deterioration and abandonment of the shrines along the Kiiji route. This trend accelerated after the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, as Kumano pilgrimages lost their prominence. Despite some restoration efforts during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩}}, the shrines did not regain their former status. Further changes during the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}} due to the {{ill|State Shinto|ja|国家神道}} reforms, shrine mergers, and urbanization led to the loss of original shrine sites, with some locations now unknown.
This article covers the Ōji shrines with identified sites in Minabe Town, Wakayama Prefecture.
== Kujūku Ōji in Minabe Town == There are three Ōji shrines within Minabe Town:
=== Iwashiro Ōji === [[File:Iwashiro-Oji Shrine.jpg|thumb|Iwashiro Ōji]] Located near the mouth of the Nishi-Iwashiro River facing the coast, the '''Iwashiro Ōji''' (岩代王子) shrine site is identified. Some historical sources such as the ''{{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja|和歌山県聖蹟}}'' place it here, while others like the ''{{ill|Kii Fudoki Continued|ja|紀伊続風土記}}'' identify a nearby Tenjin Shrine as the original site. There is a monument nearby related to {{ill|Prince Arima|ja|有間皇子}}. It is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 1558 Nishi-Iwashiro, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Senri Ōji === [[File:Senri-Oji01s2040.jpg|thumb|220px|Senri Ōji]] The '''Senri Ōji''' (千里王子) is located along the coast of {{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜}}. It has a monument commemorating {{ill|Emperor Kazan|ja|花山天皇}}’s poetry and is adjacent to Senri Kannon Temple. It was merged into {{ill|Suga Shrine (Minabe|ja|須賀神社}} in 1909 but remains well-preserved. The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Minabe Ōji === [[File:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|Minabe Ōji]] The '''Minabe Ōji''' (三鍋王子) is located near {{ill|Minabe Station|ja|南部駅}}. Historical records mention it as a site for religious offerings during imperial pilgrimages. It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe|ja|鹿島神社}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
== Surrounding Information == * '''{{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜}}''': A significant ritual site where pilgrims performed {{ill|Misogi|ja|禊}} purification rites. * '''Senri Kannon''': A Buddhist hall originally associated with Senri Ōji, now a separate temple after the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令}} during the Meiji era. * '''Ancient Pilgrimage Path (Senri Ōji to Minabe Ōji)''': A well-preserved ancient road, recently designated as a cultural property in 2022. * '''Nanbu Pass and Jizō Statue''': The pass was historically significant and features a {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} statue believed to protect against fractures.
<gallery> File:Senri Beach 02.jpg|Senri no Hama File:Kiiji - Senri Ōji.jpg|Senri Ōji with seashell offerings File:Senri Kannon.jpg|Senri Kannon File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 01.jpg|Stone Buddhas along the ancient path File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 03.jpg|Pilgrimage path through private plum orchards File:Nanbu Pass.jpg|Old Nanbu Pass File:Minabe-touge01s2048.jpg|Jizō statue at Nanbu Pass </gallery>

== Further Reading == * Hasegawa, Yasutaka (2007). *Kumano Ōji Pilgrimage Guidebook*. Shinpu Shobo. ISBN 9784882696292 * Nishi, Ritsu (1987). *Kumano Kodo Guide: Current Survey of the Kujūku Ōji Shrines*. Arao Seibundō. * Heibonsha (1983). *Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture*. Heibonsha.
== See Also == {{Commonscat|Kujuku-ojis|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}}

This is a list of some of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the ōji Shrines in the region of {{ill|Minabe Town|ja|みなべ町}} <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-みなべ町)-qq]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-みなべ町)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:九十九王子 (みなべ町)]] ==Overview== This article discusses the '''{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}}''' (九十九王子) located within {{ill|Minabe, Wakayama|ja|みなべ町}}, {{ill|Hidaka District, Wakayama|ja|日高郡 (和歌山県)}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}.
== What Are the Kujūku Ōji? == ''For more details, see {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}}.''
The Kujūku Ōji (九十九王子) refers to a group of {{ill|Shinto shrines|ja|神社}} located along the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}}, particularly on the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} and {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路}} routes. These shrines were rapidly organized primarily during the 12th to 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験}}, who served as guides for imperial family members and nobles on pilgrimages to Kumano, with prayers for the protection of pilgrims.
However, after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|ja|承久の乱}} in 1221, pilgrimages from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}} declined, leading to the deterioration and abandonment of the shrines along the Kiiji route. This trend accelerated after the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, as Kumano pilgrimages lost their prominence. Despite some restoration efforts during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩}}, the shrines did not regain their former status. Further changes during the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}} due to the {{ill|State Shinto|ja|国家神道}} reforms, shrine mergers, and urbanization led to the loss of original shrine sites, with some locations now unknown.
This article covers the Ōji shrines with identified sites in Minabe Town, Wakayama Prefecture.
== Kujūku Ōji in Minabe Town == There are three Ōji shrines within Minabe Town:
=== Iwashiro Ōji === [[File:Iwashiro-Oji Shrine.jpg|thumb|Iwashiro Ōji]] Located near the mouth of the Nishi-Iwashiro River facing the coast, the '''Iwashiro Ōji''' (岩代王子) shrine site is identified. Some historical sources such as the ''{{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja|和歌山県聖蹟}}'' place it here, while others like the ''{{ill|Kii Fudoki Continued|ja|紀伊続風土記}}'' identify a nearby Tenjin Shrine as the original site. There is a monument nearby related to {{ill|Prince Arima|ja|有間皇子}}. It is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 1558 Nishi-Iwashiro, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Senri Ōji === [[File:Senri-Oji01s2040.jpg|thumb|220px|Senri Ōji]] The '''Senri Ōji''' (千里王子) is located along the coast of {{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜}}. It has a monument commemorating {{ill|Emperor Kazan|ja|花山天皇}}’s poetry and is adjacent to Senri Kannon Temple. It was merged into {{ill|Suga Shrine (Minabe|ja|須賀神社}} in 1909 but remains well-preserved. The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Minabe Ōji === [[File:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|Minabe Ōji]] The '''Minabe Ōji''' (三鍋王子) is located near {{ill|Minabe Station|ja|南部駅}}. Historical records mention it as a site for religious offerings during imperial pilgrimages. It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe|ja|鹿島神社}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
== Surrounding Information == * '''{{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜}}''': A significant ritual site where pilgrims performed {{ill|Misogi|ja|禊}} purification rites. * '''Senri Kannon''': A Buddhist hall originally associated with Senri Ōji, now a separate temple after the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令}} during the Meiji era. * '''Ancient Pilgrimage Path (Senri Ōji to Minabe Ōji)''': A well-preserved ancient road, recently designated as a cultural property in 2022. * '''Nanbu Pass and Jizō Statue''': The pass was historically significant and features a {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} statue believed to protect against fractures.
<gallery> File:Senri Beach 02.jpg|Senri no Hama File:Kiiji - Senri Ōji.jpg|Senri Ōji with seashell offerings File:Senri Kannon.jpg|Senri Kannon File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 01.jpg|Stone Buddhas along the ancient path File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 03.jpg|Pilgrimage path through private plum orchards File:Nanbu Pass.jpg|Old Nanbu Pass File:Minabe-touge01s2048.jpg|Jizō statue at Nanbu Pass </gallery>

This is a list of some of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the ōji Shrines in the region of {{ill|Minabe Town|ja|みなべ町}} <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-みなべ町)-qq]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-みなべ町)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:九十九王子 (みなべ町)]] ==Overview== This article discusses the '''{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}}''' (九十九王子) located within {{ill|Minabe, Wakayama|ja|みなべ町}}, {{ill|Hidaka District, Wakayama|ja|日高郡 (和歌山県)}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}.
== What Are the Kujūku Ōji? == ''For more details, see {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}}.''
The Kujūku Ōji (九十九王子) refers to a group of {{ill|Shinto shrines|ja|神社}} located along the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}}, particularly on the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} and {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路}} routes. These shrines were rapidly organized primarily during the 12th to 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験}}, who served as guides for imperial family members and nobles on pilgrimages to Kumano, with prayers for the protection of pilgrims.
However, after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|ja|承久の乱}} in 1221, pilgrimages from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}} declined, leading to the deterioration and abandonment of the shrines along the Kiiji route. This trend accelerated after the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, as Kumano pilgrimages lost their prominence. Despite some restoration efforts during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩}}, the shrines did not regain their former status. Further changes during the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}} due to the {{ill|State Shinto|ja|国家神道}} reforms, shrine mergers, and urbanization led to the loss of original shrine sites, with some locations now unknown.
This article covers the Ōji shrines with identified sites in Minabe Town, Wakayama Prefecture.
== Kujūku Ōji in Minabe Town == There are three Ōji shrines within Minabe Town:
=== Iwashiro Ōji === [[File:Iwashiro-Oji Shrine.jpg|thumb|Iwashiro Ōji]] Located near the mouth of the Nishi-Iwashiro River facing the coast, the '''Iwashiro Ōji''' (岩代王子) shrine site is identified. Some historical sources such as the ''{{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja|和歌山県聖蹟}}'' place it here, while others like the ''{{ill|Kii Fudoki Continued|ja|紀伊続風土記}}'' identify a nearby Tenjin Shrine as the original site. There is a monument nearby related to {{ill|Prince Arima|ja|有間皇子}}. It is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 1558 Nishi-Iwashiro, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Senri Ōji === [[File:Senri-Oji01s2040.jpg|thumb|220px|Senri Ōji]] The '''Senri Ōji''' (千里王子) is located along the coast of {{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜}}. It has a monument commemorating {{ill|Emperor Kazan|ja|花山天皇}}’s poetry and is adjacent to Senri Kannon Temple. It was merged into {{ill|Suga Shrine (Minabe|ja|須賀神社}} in 1909 but remains well-preserved. The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Minabe Ōji === [[File:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|Minabe Ōji]] The '''Minabe Ōji''' (三鍋王子) is located near {{ill|Minabe Station|ja|南部駅}}. Historical records mention it as a site for religious offerings during imperial pilgrimages. It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe|ja|鹿島神社}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
== Surrounding Information == * '''{{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜}}''': A significant ritual site where pilgrims performed {{ill|Misogi|ja|禊}} purification rites. * '''Senri Kannon''': A Buddhist hall originally associated with Senri Ōji, now a separate temple after the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令}} during the Meiji era. * '''Ancient Pilgrimage Path (Senri Ōji to Minabe Ōji)''': A well-preserved ancient road, recently designated as a cultural property in 2022. * '''Nanbu Pass and Jizō Statue''': The pass was historically significant and features a {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} statue believed to protect against fractures.

This is a list of some of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the ōji Shrines in the region of {{ill|Minabe Town|ja|みなべ町}} <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-みなべ町)-qq]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-みなべ町)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:九十九王子 (みなべ町)]] ==Overview== This article discusses the '''{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}}''' (九十九王子) located within {{ill|Minabe, Wakayama|ja|みなべ町}}, {{ill|Hidaka District, Wakayama|ja|日高郡 (和歌山県)}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}.
== What Are the Kujūku Ōji? == ''For more details, see {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}}.''
The Kujūku Ōji (九十九王子) refers to a group of {{ill|Shinto shrines|ja|神社}} located along the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}}, particularly on the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} and {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路}} routes. These shrines were rapidly organized primarily during the 12th to 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験}}, who served as guides for imperial family members and nobles on pilgrimages to Kumano, with prayers for the protection of pilgrims.
However, after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|ja|承久の乱}} in 1221, pilgrimages from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}} declined, leading to the deterioration and abandonment of the shrines along the Kiiji route. This trend accelerated after the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, as Kumano pilgrimages lost their prominence. Despite some restoration efforts during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩}}, the shrines did not regain their former status. Further changes during the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}} due to the {{ill|State Shinto|ja|国家神道}} reforms, shrine mergers, and urbanization led to the loss of original shrine sites, with some locations now unknown.
This article covers the Ōji shrines with identified sites in Minabe Town, Wakayama Prefecture.
== Kujūku Ōji in Minabe Town == There are three Ōji shrines within Minabe Town:
=== Iwashiro Ōji === [[File:Iwashiro-Oji Shrine.jpg|thumb|Iwashiro Ōji]] Located near the mouth of the Nishi-Iwashiro River facing the coast, the '''Iwashiro Ōji''' (岩代王子) shrine site is identified. Some historical sources such as the ''{{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja|和歌山県聖蹟}}'' place it here, while others like the ''{{ill|Kii Fudoki Continued|ja|紀伊続風土記}}'' identify a nearby Tenjin Shrine as the original site. There is a monument nearby related to {{ill|Prince Arima|ja|有間皇子}}. It is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 1558 Nishi-Iwashiro, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Senri Ōji === [[File:Senri-Oji01s2040.jpg|thumb|220px|Senri Ōji]] The '''Senri Ōji''' (千里王子) is located along the coast of {{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜}}. It has a monument commemorating {{ill|Emperor Kazan|ja|花山天皇}}’s poetry and is adjacent to Senri Kannon Temple. It was merged into {{ill|Suga Shrine (Minabe|ja|須賀神社}} in 1909 but remains well-preserved. The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Minabe Ōji === [[File:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|Minabe Ōji]] The '''Minabe Ōji''' (三鍋王子) is located near {{ill|Minabe Station|ja|南部駅}}. Historical records mention it as a site for religious offerings during imperial pilgrimages. It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe|ja|鹿島神社}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
== Surrounding Information == * '''{{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜}}''': A significant ritual site where pilgrims performed {{ill|Misogi|ja|禊}} purification rites. * '''Senri Kannon''': A Buddhist hall originally associated with Senri Ōji, now a separate temple after the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令}} during the Meiji era. * '''Ancient Pilgrimage Path (Senri Ōji to Minabe Ōji)''': A well-preserved ancient road, recently designated as a cultural property in 2022. * '''Nanbu Pass and Jizō Statue''': The pass was historically significant and features a {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} statue believed to protect against fractures.
