This is a list of some of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=draft}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the ōji Shrines in the region of {{ill|Minabe Town|ja|みなべ町|12=draft}} <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
<!--みなべ町)-->
<!--みなべ町)-->
==Overview== This article discusses the '''{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|12=draft}}''' (九十九王子) located within {{ill|Minabe, Wakayama|ja|みなべ町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hidaka District, Wakayama|ja|日高郡 (和歌山県)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|12=draft}}.
== What Are the Kujūku Ōji? == ''For more details, see {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|12=draft}}.''
The Kujūku Ōji (九十九王子) refers to a group of {{ill|Shinto shrines|ja|神社|12=draft}} located along the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō|12=draft}}, particularly on the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|12=draft}} routes. These shrines were rapidly organized primarily during the 12th to 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験|12=draft}}, who served as guides for imperial family members and nobles on pilgrimages to Kumano, with prayers for the protection of pilgrims.
However, after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|ja|承久の乱|12=draft}} in 1221, pilgrimages from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=draft}} declined, leading to the deterioration and abandonment of the shrines along the Kiiji route. This trend accelerated after the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|12=draft}}, as Kumano pilgrimages lost their prominence. Despite some restoration efforts during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}} by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩|12=draft}}, the shrines did not regain their former status. Further changes during the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代|12=draft}} due to the {{ill|State Shinto|ja|国家神道|12=draft}} reforms, shrine mergers, and urbanization led to the loss of original shrine sites, with some locations now unknown.
This article covers the Ōji shrines with identified sites in Minabe Town, Wakayama Prefecture.
== Kujūku Ōji in Minabe Town == There are three Ōji shrines within Minabe Town:
=== Iwashiro Ōji === [[File:岩代王子２.jpg|thumb|Iwashiro Ōji]] Located near the mouth of the Nishi-Iwashiro River facing the coast, the '''Iwashiro Ōji''' (岩代王子) shrine site is identified. Some historical sources such as the ''{{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja|和歌山県聖蹟|12=draft}}'' place it here, while others like the ''{{ill|Kii Fudoki Continued|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=draft}}'' identify a nearby Tenjin Shrine as the original site. There is a monument nearby related to {{ill|Prince Arima|ja|有間皇子|12=draft}}. It is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 1558 Nishi-Iwashiro, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Senri Ōji === [[File:Senri-Oji01s2040.jpg|thumb|220px|Senri Ōji]] The '''Senri Ōji''' (千里王子) is located along the coast of {{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|12=draft}}. It has a monument commemorating {{ill|Emperor Kazan|ja|花山天皇|12=draft}}’s poetry and is adjacent to Senri Kannon Temple. It was merged into {{ill|Suga Shrine (Minabe|ja|須賀神社|12=draft}} in 1909 but remains well-preserved. The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Minabe Ōji === [[File:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|Minabe Ōji]] The '''Minabe Ōji''' (三鍋王子) is located near {{ill|Minabe Station|ja|南部駅|12=draft}}. Historical records mention it as a site for religious offerings during imperial pilgrimages. It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe|ja|鹿島神社|12=draft}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
== Surrounding Information == * '''{{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|12=draft}}''': A significant ritual site where pilgrims performed {{ill|Misogi|ja|禊|12=draft}} purification rites. * '''Senri Kannon''': A Buddhist hall originally associated with Senri Ōji, now a separate temple after the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=draft}} during the Meiji era. * '''Ancient Pilgrimage Path (Senri Ōji to Minabe Ōji)''': A well-preserved ancient road, recently designated as a cultural property in 2022. * '''Nanbu Pass and Jizō Statue''': The pass was historically significant and features a {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩|12=draft}} statue believed to protect against fractures.
<gallery> File:千里海岸 02.jpg|Senri no Hama File:Kiiji - Senri Ōji.jpg|Senri Ōji with seashell offerings File:千里観音.jpg|Senri Kannon File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 01.jpg|Stone Buddhas along the ancient path File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 03.jpg|Pilgrimage path through private plum orchards File:南部峠.jpg|Old Nanbu Pass File:Minabe-touge01s2048.jpg|Jizō statue at Nanbu Pass </gallery>
== References == {{reflist|2}}
== Further Reading == * Hasegawa, Yasutaka (2007). *Kumano Ōji Pilgrimage Guidebook*. Shinpu Shobo. ISBN 9784882696292 * Nishi, Ritsu (1987). *Kumano Kodo Guide: Current Survey of the Kujūku Ōji Shrines*. Arao Seibundō. * Heibonsha (1983). *Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture*. Heibonsha.
== See Also == {{Commonscat|Kujuku-ojis|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō|12=draft}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|12=draft}}
== External Links == * [http://www.aikis.or.jp/~minabe/kumano.html Kumano Kodo] - Minabe Town Tourism Association * [http://www.town.minabe.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkzai.html Minabe Town Cultural Properties]

The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
== Surrounding Information == * '''{{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|12=draft}}''': A significant ritual site where pilgrims performed {{ill|Misogi|ja|禊|12=draft}} purification rites. * '''Senri Kannon''': A Buddhist hall originally associated with Senri Ōji, now a separate temple after the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=draft}} during the Meiji era. * '''Ancient Pilgrimage Path (Senri Ōji to Minabe Ōji)''': A well-preserved ancient road, recently designated as a cultural property in 2022. * '''Nanbu Pass and Jizō Statue''': The pass was historically significant and features a {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩|12=draft}} statue believed to protect against fractures.
<gallery> File:千里海岸 02.jpg|Senri no Hama File:Kiiji - Senri Ōji.jpg|Senri Ōji with seashell offerings File:千里観音.jpg|Senri Kannon File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 01.jpg|Stone Buddhas along the ancient path File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 03.jpg|Pilgrimage path through private plum orchards File:南部峠.jpg|Old Nanbu Pass File:Minabe-touge01s2048.jpg|Jizō statue at Nanbu Pass </gallery>
== References == {{reflist|2}}
== Further Reading == * Hasegawa, Yasutaka (2007). *Kumano Ōji Pilgrimage Guidebook*. Shinpu Shobo. ISBN 9784882696292 * Nishi, Ritsu (1987). *Kumano Kodo Guide: Current Survey of the Kujūku Ōji Shrines*. Arao Seibundō. * Heibonsha (1983). *Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture*. Heibonsha.
== See Also == {{Commonscat|Kujuku-ojis|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō|12=draft}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|12=draft}}

revision.text:
This is a list of some of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=draft}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the ōji Shrines in the region of {{ill|Minabe Town|ja|みなべ町|12=draft}}
==Overview== This article discusses the '''{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|12=draft}}''' (九十九王子) located within {{ill|Minabe, Wakayama|ja|みなべ町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hidaka District, Wakayama|ja|日高郡 (和歌山県)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|12=draft}}.
== What Are the Kujūku Ōji? == ''For more details, see {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|12=draft}}.''
The Kujūku Ōji (九十九王子) refers to a group of {{ill|Shinto shrines|ja|神社|12=draft}} located along the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō|12=draft}}, particularly on the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|12=draft}} routes. These shrines were rapidly organized primarily during the 12th to 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験|12=draft}}, who served as guides for imperial family members and nobles on pilgrimages to Kumano, with prayers for the protection of pilgrims.
However, after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|ja|承久の乱|12=draft}} in 1221, pilgrimages from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=draft}} declined, leading to the deterioration and abandonment of the shrines along the Kiiji route. This trend accelerated after the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|12=draft}}, as Kumano pilgrimages lost their prominence. Despite some restoration efforts during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}} by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩|12=draft}}, the shrines did not regain their former status. Further changes during the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代|12=draft}} due to the {{ill|State Shinto|ja|国家神道|12=draft}} reforms, shrine mergers, and urbanization led to the loss of original shrine sites, with some locations now unknown.
This article covers the Ōji shrines with identified sites in Minabe Town, Wakayama Prefecture.
== Kujūku Ōji in Minabe Town == There are three Ōji shrines within Minabe Town:
=== Iwashiro Ōji === [[File:岩代王子２.jpg|thumb|Iwashiro Ōji]] Located near the mouth of the Nishi-Iwashiro River facing the coast, the '''Iwashiro Ōji''' (岩代王子) shrine site is identified. Some historical sources such as the ''{{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja|和歌山県聖蹟|12=draft}}'' place it here, while others like the ''{{ill|Kii Fudoki Continued|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=draft}}'' identify a nearby Tenjin Shrine as the original site. There is a monument nearby related to {{ill|Prince Arima|ja|有間皇子|12=draft}}. It is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 1558 Nishi-Iwashiro, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Senri Ōji === [[File:Senri-Oji01s2040.jpg|thumb|220px|Senri Ōji]] The '''Senri Ōji''' (千里王子) is located along the coast of {{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|12=draft}}. It has a monument commemorating {{ill|Emperor Kazan|ja|花山天皇|12=draft}}’s poetry and is adjacent to Senri Kannon Temple. It was merged into {{ill|Suga Shrine (Minabe|ja|須賀神社|12=draft}} in 1909 but remains well-preserved. The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Minabe Ōji === [[File:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|Minabe Ōji]] The '''Minabe Ōji''' (三鍋王子) is located near {{ill|Minabe Station|ja|南部駅|12=draft}}. Historical records mention it as a site for religious offerings during imperial pilgrimages. It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe|ja|鹿島神社|12=draft}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
== Surrounding Information == * '''{{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|12=draft}}''': A significant ritual site where pilgrims performed {{ill|Misogi|ja|禊|12=draft}} purification rites. * '''Senri Kannon''': A Buddhist hall originally associated with Senri Ōji, now a separate temple after the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=draft}} during the Meiji era. * '''Ancient Pilgrimage Path (Senri Ōji to Minabe Ōji)''': A well-preserved ancient road, recently designated as a cultural property in 2022. * '''Nanbu Pass and Jizō Statue''': The pass was historically significant and features a {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩|12=draft}} statue believed to protect against fractures.
<gallery> File:千里海岸 02.jpg|Senri no Hama File:Kiiji - Senri Ōji.jpg|Senri Ōji with seashell offerings File:千里観音.jpg|Senri Kannon File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 01.jpg|Stone Buddhas along the ancient path File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 03.jpg|Pilgrimage path through private plum orchards File:南部峠.jpg|Old Nanbu Pass File:Minabe-touge01s2048.jpg|Jizō statue at Nanbu Pass </gallery>
== References == {{reflist|2}}
== Further Reading == * Hasegawa, Yasutaka (2007). *Kumano Ōji Pilgrimage Guidebook*. Shinpu Shobo. ISBN 9784882696292 * Nishi, Ritsu (1987). *Kumano Kodo Guide: Current Survey of the Kujūku Ōji Shrines*. Arao Seibundō. * Heibonsha (1983). *Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture*. Heibonsha.
== See Also == {{Commonscat|Kujuku-ojis|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō|12=draft}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|12=draft}}
== External Links == * [http://www.aikis.or.jp/~minabe/kumano.html Kumano Kodo] - Minabe Town Tourism Association * [http://www.town.minabe.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkzai.html Minabe Town Cultural Properties]

37 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:九十九王子 (みなべ町) up to rev 97865869
revision.text:
本項目では、[[和歌山県]][[日高郡 (和歌山県)|日高郡]][[みなべ町]]内に所在する'''[[九十九王子]]'''（くじゅうくおうじ）について述べる。
== 九十九王子とは == ''詳細は[[九十九王子]]を参照''
九十九王子（くじゅうくおうじ）とは、[[熊野古道]]、特に[[紀伊路|紀伊路・中辺路]]沿いに在する[[神社]]のうち、主に[[12世紀]]から [[13世紀]]にかけて、皇族・貴人の熊野詣に際して先達をつとめた熊野修験の手で急速に組織された一群の神社をいい、参詣者の守護が祈願された。
しかしながら、[[1221年]]（[[承久]]3年）の[[承久の乱]]以降、[[京都|京]]からの熊野詣が下火になり、そのルートであった紀伊路が衰退するとともに、荒廃と退転がすすんだ。[[室町時代]]以降、熊野詣がかつてのような卓越した地位を失うにつれ、この傾向はいっそう進み、[[江戸時代|近世]][[紀州藩]]の手による顕彰も行なわれたものの、勢いをとどめるまでには至らなかった。さらに、[[明治]]以降の[[神道]]の[[国家神道]]化とそれに伴う合祀、市街化による廃絶などにより、旧社地が失われたり、比定地が不明になったものも多い。
本記事では、これら九十九王子のうち、和歌山県みなべ町に比定地がある王子を扱う。
== みなべ町の九十九王子 == みなべ町内の九十九王子は3社。
=== 岩代王子 === [[ファイル:岩代王子２.jpg|thumb|岩代王子]] 西岩代川の河口近く、海岸に面して'''岩代王子'''（いわしろおうじ）の社地がある。『[[和歌山県聖蹟]]』は現在地を挙げているが、『[[紀伊続風土記]]』『[[紀伊名所図会]]』は東岩代川の南寄りの浜辺にある天神社を社址とする<ref name="nishi_1987_137">西[1987: 137]</ref><ref name="Heibonsha_1983_571">平凡社[1983: 571]</ref>。近隣には[[有間皇子]]の故事に因む歌碑がある。県指定史跡（[[1958年]]〈昭和33年〉4月1日指定）<ref name="Kinen">{{Cite web|和書 |publisher=和歌山県教育委員会 |url=http://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/500700/mokuroku/mokuroku/kenkinen.html |title=県指定文化財・記念物 |accessdate=2009-02-07 }}</ref>。
『中右記』の[[天仁]]2年（[[1109年]]）10月21日条、[[藤原定家]]の「熊野道之間愚記」（[[後鳥羽天皇|後鳥羽院]]参詣記、『[[明月記]]』所収）の[[建仁]]元年（[[1201年]]）10月12日条に記述が見られ、社殿に参詣の人々の名を書き連ねたり、和歌を奉納したりしていたことが分かる<ref name="Heibonsha_1983_571"/>。また、『[[平家物語]]』巻一〇には出家した[[平維盛]]が[[高野山]]から熊野に向かう途中に立ち寄ったとある<ref name="Heibonsha_1983_571"/>。
[[1909年]] （[[明治]]42年）に近隣の西岩代八幡神社（みなべ町西岩代）に合祀された。しかし、合祀に関係したものが病に倒れたため<ref name="nishi_1987_137"/><ref name="Hasegawa_2007_106">長谷川[2007: 106]</ref>（異説によれば海難や不漁の故だという<ref name="nishi_1987_137"/>）、御神体を旧社地に戻し遥拝所にしたと伝えられる。
* 所在地 - 日高郡みなべ町西岩代1558
=== 千里王子 === [[ファイル:Senri-Oji01s2040.jpg|thumb|220px|千里王子]] 岩代王子と千里王子を結ぶ参詣道はかつては海岸伝いであったと伝えられるが、今日では侵食のために通行不能である。[[国道42号]]線南側の住宅地の中を抜けて[[千里の浜]]に面した'''千里王子'''（せんりおうじ）にたどりつく。境内には[[花山天皇|花山院]]の歌碑があり、隣には千里観音寺がある。県指定史跡（1958年〈昭和33年〉4月1日指定）<ref name="Kinen"/>。
[[1909年]]（[[明治]]42年）に近隣の須賀神社（みなべ町村西本庄）に合祀されたが、社殿は良好な状態で遺されている。合祀された御神体24体も、1945年の春にすべて復旧したという<ref name="nishi_1987_138">西[1987: 138]</ref>。祭神不詳、本地仏として[[如意輪観音]]を祀る。
千里の浜は中世熊野御幸期には幅広い砂浜であったが、[[1331年]]（[[元弘]]元年）の地震で地形が変わってしまったと伝えられる（『太平記』）<ref name="Hasegawa_2007_108">長谷川[2007: 108]</ref>。県指定史跡（1964年〈昭和39年〉7月20日指定）<ref name="Kinen"/>。
* 所在地 - 日高郡みなべ町山内2519
=== 三鍋王子 === [[ファイル:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|三鍋王子]] 千里王子からJRの線路沿いに進み、高架をくぐって南部峠のゆるやかな上り坂を登りきると、南部峠の石仏が待っている。そこから峠を下り、南部川をわたって、[[南部駅]]のへ向かう街路の傍らに'''三鍋王子'''（みなべおうじ）がある。県指定史跡（1958年〈昭和33年〉4月1日指定）<ref name="Kinen"/>。
『中右記』10月12日条に南陪山の麓にある王子社で奉幣をしたとの記事があるほか、「熊野道之間愚記」に「三鍋王子」との記述が見られる。[[1877年]]（[[明治]]10年）に、千里王子と同じく須賀神社に合祀された。須賀神社はさらに 1909年に鹿島神社を合祀して鹿島神社に改称している。鹿島神社の社殿は王子社の旧社殿を移築したものであるという<ref>西 [1987: 139]</ref><ref>平凡社[1983: 573]</ref>。境内には[[小栗判官]]ゆかりの小栗井戸が遺されており<ref>{{Cite web|和書 |publisher=みなべ町役場教育学習課 |url=http://www.town.minabe.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkzai.html |title=町の文化財 |accessdate=2009-02-15 }}</ref>、また数多くある巨石の大半は板状卒塔婆で、三鍋王子の神宮寺であった安養寺から鎌倉時代以降に移されたものである。
鹿島神社は南部駅から南東約700mほどのところにある。そこから海岸に出ると、2kmほど沖合いに鹿島という島がある。この島の形が3つの鍋をひっくり返したように見えることから「みなべ」（三鍋、南部）の地名の由来になったと伝えられる<ref>長谷川[2007: 109]</ref>。
* 所在地 - 日高郡みなべ町北道60
== 周辺情報 == ; 千里の浜 : [[千里の浜]]（千里海岸）は紀伊路において[[海岸]]（[[砂浜]]）に下りて[[海水]]で[[禊]]をした潮[[垢離]]をした重要な場所とされた。千里王子は「貝の王子」という別称があるが、これは浜に打ち上げられた貝殻を王子に奉納したことに由来する。 ; 千里観音 : 明治の[[神仏分離令]]により独立した仏教施設（観音堂）となったが、元々は千里王子と合祀されており、現在も隣り合わせている。 ; 千里王子跡北東参詣道 : 千里王子を発ち三鍋王子へ向かう過程で状態の良好な古道が残されており、千里王子跡北東参詣道として[[2022年]]（[[令和]]4年）に文化財に指定する答申が行われた。 ; 南部峠と峠の地蔵 : 南部峠の鞍部に祭られた[[石仏]]。南部峠は明治初期までこの地域の交通の要衝で、その名は古くは藤原忠右の[[1109年]]（[[天仁]]2年）の参詣記（『[[中右記]]』所収）に南陪山の名で登場する。石仏の存在に関する初出は[[元禄]]年間に著された地誌『紀南郷導記』である。石仏は[[地蔵菩薩]]で骨折に霊験があるとされることから、骨つぎ地蔵とも称される。 <gallery> File:千里海岸 02.jpg|千里の浜 File:Kiiji - Senri Ōji.jpg|貝殻が並ぶ千里王子 File:千里観音.jpg|千里観音 File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 01.jpg|石仏が並ぶ古道 File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 03.jpg|私有地梅林の中を通る千里王子跡北東参詣道 File:南部峠.jpg|南部峠（旧道） ファイル:Minabe-touge01s2048.jpg|南部峠の地蔵 </gallery>
== 脚注 == {{reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == * 長谷川 靖高、2007、『熊野王子巡拝ガイドブック』、新風書房 ISBN 9784882696292 * 西 律、1987、『熊野古道みちしるべ - 熊野九十九王子現状踏査録』、荒尾成文堂（みなもと選書1） * 平凡社編、1983、『和歌山県の地名』、平凡社（日本歴史地名大系31）
== 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Kujuku-ojis|九十九王子}} * [[熊野信仰]] * [[熊野古道]] - [[紀伊路]] * [[九十九王子]] ** （[[九十九王子 (印南町)#切目中山王子|切目中山王子]]） - '''岩代王子''' - '''千里王子''' - '''三鍋王子''' - （[[九十九王子 (田辺市・上富田町)#芳養王子|芳養王子]]）
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.aikis.or.jp/~minabe/kumano.html 熊野古道] - みなべ町観光協会公式サイト内。町内の熊野古道および史跡めぐりのガイドマップ。 * [http://www.town.minabe.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkzai.html 町の文化財] - みなべ町役場教育学習課サイト内。 {{九十九王子}} {{神道 横}}

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[九十九王子 (みなべ町)]] to [[List of Kujūku Ōji (Minabe Town)]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
本項目では、[[和歌山県]][[日高郡 (和歌山県)|日高郡]][[みなべ町]]内に所在する'''[[九十九王子]]'''（くじゅうくおうじ）について述べる。
== 九十九王子とは == ''詳細は[[九十九王子]]を参照''
九十九王子（くじゅうくおうじ）とは、[[熊野古道]]、特に[[紀伊路|紀伊路・中辺路]]沿いに在する[[神社]]のうち、主に[[12世紀]]から [[13世紀]]にかけて、皇族・貴人の熊野詣に際して先達をつとめた熊野修験の手で急速に組織された一群の神社をいい、参詣者の守護が祈願された。
しかしながら、[[1221年]]（[[承久]]3年）の[[承久の乱]]以降、[[京都|京]]からの熊野詣が下火になり、そのルートであった紀伊路が衰退するとともに、荒廃と退転がすすんだ。[[室町時代]]以降、熊野詣がかつてのような卓越した地位を失うにつれ、この傾向はいっそう進み、[[江戸時代|近世]][[紀州藩]]の手による顕彰も行なわれたものの、勢いをとどめるまでには至らなかった。さらに、[[明治]]以降の[[神道]]の[[国家神道]]化とそれに伴う合祀、市街化による廃絶などにより、旧社地が失われたり、比定地が不明になったものも多い。
本記事では、これら九十九王子のうち、和歌山県みなべ町に比定地がある王子を扱う。
== みなべ町の九十九王子 == みなべ町内の九十九王子は3社。
=== 岩代王子 === [[ファイル:岩代王子２.jpg|thumb|岩代王子]] 西岩代川の河口近く、海岸に面して'''岩代王子'''（いわしろおうじ）の社地がある。『[[和歌山県聖蹟]]』は現在地を挙げているが、『[[紀伊続風土記]]』『[[紀伊名所図会]]』は東岩代川の南寄りの浜辺にある天神社を社址とする<ref name="nishi_1987_137">西[1987: 137]</ref><ref name="Heibonsha_1983_571">平凡社[1983: 571]</ref>。近隣には[[有間皇子]]の故事に因む歌碑がある。県指定史跡（[[1958年]]〈昭和33年〉4月1日指定）<ref name="Kinen">{{Cite web|和書 |publisher=和歌山県教育委員会 |url=http://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/500700/mokuroku/mokuroku/kenkinen.html |title=県指定文化財・記念物 |accessdate=2009-02-07 }}</ref>。
『中右記』の[[天仁]]2年（[[1109年]]）10月21日条、[[藤原定家]]の「熊野道之間愚記」（[[後鳥羽天皇|後鳥羽院]]参詣記、『[[明月記]]』所収）の[[建仁]]元年（[[1201年]]）10月12日条に記述が見られ、社殿に参詣の人々の名を書き連ねたり、和歌を奉納したりしていたことが分かる<ref name="Heibonsha_1983_571"/>。また、『[[平家物語]]』巻一〇には出家した[[平維盛]]が[[高野山]]から熊野に向かう途中に立ち寄ったとある<ref name="Heibonsha_1983_571"/>。
[[1909年]] （[[明治]]42年）に近隣の西岩代八幡神社（みなべ町西岩代）に合祀された。しかし、合祀に関係したものが病に倒れたため<ref name="nishi_1987_137"/><ref name="Hasegawa_2007_106">長谷川[2007: 106]</ref>（異説によれば海難や不漁の故だという<ref name="nishi_1987_137"/>）、御神体を旧社地に戻し遥拝所にしたと伝えられる。
* 所在地 - 日高郡みなべ町西岩代1558
=== 千里王子 === [[ファイル:Senri-Oji01s2040.jpg|thumb|220px|千里王子]] 岩代王子と千里王子を結ぶ参詣道はかつては海岸伝いであったと伝えられるが、今日では侵食のために通行不能である。[[国道42号]]線南側の住宅地の中を抜けて[[千里の浜]]に面した'''千里王子'''（せんりおうじ）にたどりつく。境内には[[花山天皇|花山院]]の歌碑があり、隣には千里観音寺がある。県指定史跡（1958年〈昭和33年〉4月1日指定）<ref name="Kinen"/>。
[[1909年]]（[[明治]]42年）に近隣の須賀神社（みなべ町村西本庄）に合祀されたが、社殿は良好な状態で遺されている。合祀された御神体24体も、1945年の春にすべて復旧したという<ref name="nishi_1987_138">西[1987: 138]</ref>。祭神不詳、本地仏として[[如意輪観音]]を祀る。
千里の浜は中世熊野御幸期には幅広い砂浜であったが、[[1331年]]（[[元弘]]元年）の地震で地形が変わってしまったと伝えられる（『太平記』）<ref name="Hasegawa_2007_108">長谷川[2007: 108]</ref>。県指定史跡（1964年〈昭和39年〉7月20日指定）<ref name="Kinen"/>。
* 所在地 - 日高郡みなべ町山内2519
=== 三鍋王子 === [[ファイル:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|三鍋王子]] 千里王子からJRの線路沿いに進み、高架をくぐって南部峠のゆるやかな上り坂を登りきると、南部峠の石仏が待っている。そこから峠を下り、南部川をわたって、[[南部駅]]のへ向かう街路の傍らに'''三鍋王子'''（みなべおうじ）がある。県指定史跡（1958年〈昭和33年〉4月1日指定）<ref name="Kinen"/>。
『中右記』10月12日条に南陪山の麓にある王子社で奉幣をしたとの記事があるほか、「熊野道之間愚記」に「三鍋王子」との記述が見られる。[[1877年]]（[[明治]]10年）に、千里王子と同じく須賀神社に合祀された。須賀神社はさらに 1909年に鹿島神社を合祀して鹿島神社に改称している。鹿島神社の社殿は王子社の旧社殿を移築したものであるという<ref>西 [1987: 139]</ref><ref>平凡社[1983: 573]</ref>。境内には[[小栗判官]]ゆかりの小栗井戸が遺されており<ref>{{Cite web|和書 |publisher=みなべ町役場教育学習課 |url=http://www.town.minabe.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkzai.html |title=町の文化財 |accessdate=2009-02-15 }}</ref>、また数多くある巨石の大半は板状卒塔婆で、三鍋王子の神宮寺であった安養寺から鎌倉時代以降に移されたものである。
鹿島神社は南部駅から南東約700mほどのところにある。そこから海岸に出ると、2kmほど沖合いに鹿島という島がある。この島の形が3つの鍋をひっくり返したように見えることから「みなべ」（三鍋、南部）の地名の由来になったと伝えられる<ref>長谷川[2007: 109]</ref>。
* 所在地 - 日高郡みなべ町北道60
== 周辺情報 == ; 千里の浜 : [[千里の浜]]（千里海岸）は紀伊路において[[海岸]]（[[砂浜]]）に下りて[[海水]]で[[禊]]をした潮[[垢離]]をした重要な場所とされた。千里王子は「貝の王子」という別称があるが、これは浜に打ち上げられた貝殻を王子に奉納したことに由来する。 ; 千里観音 : 明治の[[神仏分離令]]により独立した仏教施設（観音堂）となったが、元々は千里王子と合祀されており、現在も隣り合わせている。 ; 千里王子跡北東参詣道 : 千里王子を発ち三鍋王子へ向かう過程で状態の良好な古道が残されており、千里王子跡北東参詣道として[[2022年]]（[[令和]]4年）に文化財に指定する答申が行われた。 ; 南部峠と峠の地蔵 : 南部峠の鞍部に祭られた[[石仏]]。南部峠は明治初期までこの地域の交通の要衝で、その名は古くは藤原忠右の[[1109年]]（[[天仁]]2年）の参詣記（『[[中右記]]』所収）に南陪山の名で登場する。石仏の存在に関する初出は[[元禄]]年間に著された地誌『紀南郷導記』である。石仏は[[地蔵菩薩]]で骨折に霊験があるとされることから、骨つぎ地蔵とも称される。 <gallery> File:千里海岸 02.jpg|千里の浜 File:Kiiji - Senri Ōji.jpg|貝殻が並ぶ千里王子 File:千里観音.jpg|千里観音 File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 01.jpg|石仏が並ぶ古道 File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 03.jpg|私有地梅林の中を通る千里王子跡北東参詣道 File:南部峠.jpg|南部峠（旧道） ファイル:Minabe-touge01s2048.jpg|南部峠の地蔵 </gallery>

This is a list of some of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=draft}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the ōji Shrines in the region of {{ill|Minabe Town|ja|みなべ町|12=draft}}
==Overview== This article discusses the '''{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|12=draft}}''' (九十九王子) located within {{ill|Minabe, Wakayama|ja|みなべ町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hidaka District, Wakayama|ja|日高郡 (和歌山県)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|12=draft}}.
== What Are the Kujūku Ōji? == ''For more details, see {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|12=draft}}.''
The Kujūku Ōji (九十九王子) refers to a group of {{ill|Shinto shrines|ja|神社|12=draft}} located along the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō|12=draft}}, particularly on the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|12=draft}} routes. These shrines were rapidly organized primarily during the 12th to 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験|12=draft}}, who served as guides for imperial family members and nobles on pilgrimages to Kumano, with prayers for the protection of pilgrims.
However, after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|ja|承久の乱|12=draft}} in 1221, pilgrimages from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=draft}} declined, leading to the deterioration and abandonment of the shrines along the Kiiji route. This trend accelerated after the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|12=draft}}, as Kumano pilgrimages lost their prominence. Despite some restoration efforts during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}} by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩|12=draft}}, the shrines did not regain their former status. Further changes during the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代|12=draft}} due to the {{ill|State Shinto|ja|国家神道|12=draft}} reforms, shrine mergers, and urbanization led to the loss of original shrine sites, with some locations now unknown.
This article covers the Ōji shrines with identified sites in Minabe Town, Wakayama Prefecture.
== Kujūku Ōji in Minabe Town == There are three Ōji shrines within Minabe Town:
=== Iwashiro Ōji === [[File:岩代王子２.jpg|thumb|Iwashiro Ōji]] Located near the mouth of the Nishi-Iwashiro River facing the coast, the '''Iwashiro Ōji''' (岩代王子) shrine site is identified. Some historical sources such as the ''{{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja|和歌山県聖蹟|12=draft}}'' place it here, while others like the ''{{ill|Kii Fudoki Continued|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=draft}}'' identify a nearby Tenjin Shrine as the original site. There is a monument nearby related to {{ill|Prince Arima|ja|有間皇子|12=draft}}. It is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 1558 Nishi-Iwashiro, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Senri Ōji === [[File:Senri-Oji01s2040.jpg|thumb|220px|Senri Ōji]] The '''Senri Ōji''' (千里王子) is located along the coast of {{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|12=draft}}. It has a monument commemorating {{ill|Emperor Kazan|ja|花山天皇|12=draft}}’s poetry and is adjacent to Senri Kannon Temple. It was merged into {{ill|Suga Shrine (Minabe|ja|須賀神社|12=draft}} in 1909 but remains well-preserved. The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Minabe Ōji === [[File:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|Minabe Ōji]] The '''Minabe Ōji''' (三鍋王子) is located near {{ill|Minabe Station|ja|南部駅|12=draft}}. Historical records mention it as a site for religious offerings during imperial pilgrimages. It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe|ja|鹿島神社|12=draft}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
== Surrounding Information == * '''{{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|12=draft}}''': A significant ritual site where pilgrims performed {{ill|Misogi|ja|禊|12=draft}} purification rites. * '''Senri Kannon''': A Buddhist hall originally associated with Senri Ōji, now a separate temple after the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=draft}} during the Meiji era. * '''Ancient Pilgrimage Path (Senri Ōji to Minabe Ōji)''': A well-preserved ancient road, recently designated as a cultural property in 2022. * '''Nanbu Pass and Jizō Statue''': The pass was historically significant and features a {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩|12=draft}} statue believed to protect against fractures.
<gallery> File:千里海岸 02.jpg|Senri no Hama File:Kiiji - Senri Ōji.jpg|Senri Ōji with seashell offerings File:千里観音.jpg|Senri Kannon File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 01.jpg|Stone Buddhas along the ancient path File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 03.jpg|Pilgrimage path through private plum orchards File:南部峠.jpg|Old Nanbu Pass File:Minabe-touge01s2048.jpg|Jizō statue at Nanbu Pass </gallery>
== References == {{reflist|2}}

This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--><!--みなべ町)--><!--みなべ町)-->

This is a list of some of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the ōji Shrines in the region of {{ill|Minabe Town|ja|みなべ町|qq=draft}}
==Overview== This article discusses the '''{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|qq=draft}}''' (九十九王子) located within {{ill|Minabe, Wakayama|ja|みなべ町|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hidaka District, Wakayama|ja|日高郡 (和歌山県)|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|qq=draft}}.
== What Are the Kujūku Ōji? == ''For more details, see {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|qq=draft}}.''
The Kujūku Ōji (九十九王子) refers to a group of {{ill|Shinto shrines|ja|神社|qq=draft}} located along the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō|qq=draft}}, particularly on the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|qq=draft}} routes. These shrines were rapidly organized primarily during the 12th to 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験|qq=draft}}, who served as guides for imperial family members and nobles on pilgrimages to Kumano, with prayers for the protection of pilgrims.
However, after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|ja|承久の乱|qq=draft}} in 1221, pilgrimages from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft}} declined, leading to the deterioration and abandonment of the shrines along the Kiiji route. This trend accelerated after the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft}}, as Kumano pilgrimages lost their prominence. Despite some restoration efforts during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft}} by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩|qq=draft}}, the shrines did not regain their former status. Further changes during the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代|qq=draft}} due to the {{ill|State Shinto|ja|国家神道|qq=draft}} reforms, shrine mergers, and urbanization led to the loss of original shrine sites, with some locations now unknown.
This article covers the Ōji shrines with identified sites in Minabe Town, Wakayama Prefecture.
== Kujūku Ōji in Minabe Town == There are three Ōji shrines within Minabe Town:
=== Iwashiro Ōji === [[File:岩代王子２.jpg|thumb|Iwashiro Ōji]] Located near the mouth of the Nishi-Iwashiro River facing the coast, the '''Iwashiro Ōji''' (岩代王子) shrine site is identified. Some historical sources such as the ''{{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja|和歌山県聖蹟|qq=draft}}'' place it here, while others like the ''{{ill|Kii Fudoki Continued|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft}}'' identify a nearby Tenjin Shrine as the original site. There is a monument nearby related to {{ill|Prince Arima|ja|有間皇子|qq=draft}}. It is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 1558 Nishi-Iwashiro, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Senri Ōji === [[File:Senri-Oji01s2040.jpg|thumb|220px|Senri Ōji]] The '''Senri Ōji''' (千里王子) is located along the coast of {{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|qq=draft}}. It has a monument commemorating {{ill|Emperor Kazan|ja|花山天皇|qq=draft}}’s poetry and is adjacent to Senri Kannon Temple. It was merged into {{ill|Suga Shrine (Minabe|ja|須賀神社|qq=draft}} in 1909 but remains well-preserved. The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Minabe Ōji === [[File:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|Minabe Ōji]] The '''Minabe Ōji''' (三鍋王子) is located near {{ill|Minabe Station|ja|南部駅|qq=draft}}. Historical records mention it as a site for religious offerings during imperial pilgrimages. It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe|ja|鹿島神社|qq=draft}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
== Surrounding Information == * '''{{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|qq=draft}}''': A significant ritual site where pilgrims performed {{ill|Misogi|ja|禊|qq=draft}} purification rites. * '''Senri Kannon''': A Buddhist hall originally associated with Senri Ōji, now a separate temple after the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft}} during the Meiji era. * '''Ancient Pilgrimage Path (Senri Ōji to Minabe Ōji)''': A well-preserved ancient road, recently designated as a cultural property in 2022. * '''Nanbu Pass and Jizō Statue''': The pass was historically significant and features a {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩|qq=draft}} statue believed to protect against fractures.
<gallery> File:千里海岸 02.jpg|Senri no Hama File:Kiiji - Senri Ōji.jpg|Senri Ōji with seashell offerings File:千里観音.jpg|Senri Kannon File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 01.jpg|Stone Buddhas along the ancient path File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 03.jpg|Pilgrimage path through private plum orchards File:南部峠.jpg|Old Nanbu Pass File:Minabe-touge01s2048.jpg|Jizō statue at Nanbu Pass </gallery>

This article covers the ōji Shrines in the region of {{ill|Minabe Town|ja|みなべ町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minabe Town|qq=}}
==Overview== This article discusses the '''{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|qq=}}''' (九十九王子) located within {{ill|Minabe, Wakayama|ja|みなべ町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minabe, Wakayama|qq=}}, {{ill|Hidaka District, Wakayama|ja|日高郡 (和歌山県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hidaka District, Wakayama|qq=}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefecture|qq=}}.
== What Are the Kujūku Ōji? == ''For more details, see {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|qq=}}.''
The Kujūku Ōji (九十九王子) refers to a group of {{ill|Shinto shrines|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrines|qq=}} located along the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kodo|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kodō|qq=}}, particularly on the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahechi|qq=}} routes. These shrines were rapidly organized primarily during the 12th to 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shugendō|qq=}}, who served as guides for imperial family members and nobles on pilgrimages to Kumano, with prayers for the protection of pilgrims.
However, after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|ja|承久の乱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyū War|qq=}} in 1221, pilgrimages from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}} declined, leading to the deterioration and abandonment of the shrines along the Kiiji route. This trend accelerated after the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|qq=}}, as Kumano pilgrimages lost their prominence. Despite some restoration efforts during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}} by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishū Domain|qq=}}, the shrines did not regain their former status. Further changes during the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} due to the {{ill|State Shinto|ja|国家神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/State Shinto|qq=}} reforms, shrine mergers, and urbanization led to the loss of original shrine sites, with some locations now unknown.
This article covers the Ōji shrines with identified sites in Minabe Town, Wakayama Prefecture.
== Kujūku Ōji in Minabe Town == There are three Ōji shrines within Minabe Town:
=== Iwashiro Ōji === [[File:岩代王子２.jpg|thumb|Iwashiro Ōji]] Located near the mouth of the Nishi-Iwashiro River facing the coast, the '''Iwashiro Ōji''' (岩代王子) shrine site is identified. Some historical sources such as the ''{{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja|和歌山県聖蹟|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|qq=}}'' place it here, while others like the ''{{ill|Kii Fudoki Continued|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Fudoki Continued|qq=}}'' identify a nearby Tenjin Shrine as the original site. There is a monument nearby related to {{ill|Prince Arima|ja|有間皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Arima|qq=}}. It is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 1558 Nishi-Iwashiro, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Senri Ōji === [[File:Senri-Oji01s2040.jpg|thumb|220px|Senri Ōji]] The '''Senri Ōji''' (千里王子) is located along the coast of {{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|qq=}}. It has a monument commemorating {{ill|Emperor Kazan|ja|花山天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kazan|qq=}}’s poetry and is adjacent to Senri Kannon Temple. It was merged into {{ill|Suga Shrine (Minabe|ja|須賀神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine (Minabe|qq=}} in 1909 but remains well-preserved. The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Minabe Ōji === [[File:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|Minabe Ōji]] The '''Minabe Ōji''' (三鍋王子) is located near {{ill|Minabe Station|ja|南部駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minabe Station|qq=}}. Historical records mention it as a site for religious offerings during imperial pilgrimages. It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe|ja|鹿島神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Minabe|qq=}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
== Surrounding Information == * '''{{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|qq=}}''': A significant ritual site where pilgrims performed {{ill|Misogi|ja|禊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misogi|qq=}} purification rites. * '''Senri Kannon''': A Buddhist hall originally associated with Senri Ōji, now a separate temple after the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|qq=}} during the Meiji era. * '''Ancient Pilgrimage Path (Senri Ōji to Minabe Ōji)''': A well-preserved ancient road, recently designated as a cultural property in 2022. * '''Nanbu Pass and Jizō Statue''': The pass was historically significant and features a {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jizō Bosatsu|qq=}} statue believed to protect against fractures.
<gallery> File:千里海岸 02.jpg|Senri no Hama File:Kiiji - Senri Ōji.jpg|Senri Ōji with seashell offerings File:千里観音.jpg|Senri Kannon File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 01.jpg|Stone Buddhas along the ancient path File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 03.jpg|Pilgrimage path through private plum orchards File:南部峠.jpg|Old Nanbu Pass File:Minabe-touge01s2048.jpg|Jizō statue at Nanbu Pass </gallery>
== References == {{reflist|2}}
== Further Reading == * Hasegawa, Yasutaka (2007). *Kumano Ōji Pilgrimage Guidebook*. Shinpu Shobo. ISBN 9784882696292 * Nishi, Ritsu (1987). *Kumano Kodo Guide: Current Survey of the Kujūku Ōji Shrines*. Arao Seibundō. * Heibonsha (1983). *Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture*. Heibonsha.
== See Also == {{Commonscat|Kujuku-ojis|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kodo|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kodō|qq=}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.aikis.or.jp/~minabe/kumano.html Kumano Kodo] - Minabe Town Tourism Association * [http://www.town.minabe.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkzai.html Minabe Town Cultural Properties]

== What Are the Kujūku Ōji? == ''For more details, see {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|qq=}}.''
The Kujūku Ōji (九十九王子) refers to a group of {{ill|Shinto shrines|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrines|qq=}} located along the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kodo|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kodō|qq=}}, particularly on the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahechi|qq=}} routes. These shrines were rapidly organized primarily during the 12th to 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shugendō|qq=}}, who served as guides for imperial family members and nobles on pilgrimages to Kumano, with prayers for the protection of pilgrims.
However, after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|ja|承久の乱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyū War|qq=}} in 1221, pilgrimages from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}} declined, leading to the deterioration and abandonment of the shrines along the Kiiji route. This trend accelerated after the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|qq=}}, as Kumano pilgrimages lost their prominence. Despite some restoration efforts during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}} by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishū Domain|qq=}}, the shrines did not regain their former status. Further changes during the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|2=ja|3=明治|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} due to the {{ill|State Shinto|ja|国家神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/State Shinto|qq=}} reforms, shrine mergers, and urbanization led to the loss of original shrine sites, with some locations now unknown.
This article covers the Ōji shrines with identified sites in Minabe Town, Wakayama Prefecture.
== Kujūku Ōji in Minabe Town == There are three Ōji shrines within Minabe Town:
=== Iwashiro Ōji === [[File:岩代王子２.jpg|thumb|Iwashiro Ōji]] Located near the mouth of the Nishi-Iwashiro River facing the coast, the '''Iwashiro Ōji''' (岩代王子) shrine site is identified. Some historical sources such as the ''{{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja|和歌山県聖蹟|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|qq=}}'' place it here, while others like the ''{{ill|Kii Fudoki Continued|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Fudoki Continued|qq=}}'' identify a nearby Tenjin Shrine as the original site. There is a monument nearby related to {{ill|Prince Arima|ja|有間皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Arima|qq=}}. It is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 1558 Nishi-Iwashiro, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Senri Ōji === [[File:Senri-Oji01s2040.jpg|thumb|220px|Senri Ōji]] The '''Senri Ōji''' (千里王子) is located along the coast of {{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|qq=}}. It has a monument commemorating {{ill|Emperor Kazan|ja|花山天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kazan|qq=}}’s poetry and is adjacent to Senri Kannon Temple. It was merged into {{ill|Suga Shrine (Minabe|ja|須賀神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine (Minabe|qq=}} in 1909 but remains well-preserved. The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Minabe Ōji === [[File:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|Minabe Ōji]] The '''Minabe Ōji''' (三鍋王子) is located near {{ill|Minabe Station|ja|南部駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minabe Station|qq=}}. Historical records mention it as a site for religious offerings during imperial pilgrimages. It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe|ja|鹿島神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Minabe|qq=}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
== Surrounding Information == * '''{{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|qq=}}''': A significant ritual site where pilgrims performed {{ill|Misogi|ja|禊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misogi|qq=}} purification rites. * '''Senri Kannon''': A Buddhist hall originally associated with Senri Ōji, now a separate temple after the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|qq=}} during the Meiji era. * '''Ancient Pilgrimage Path (Senri Ōji to Minabe Ōji)''': A well-preserved ancient road, recently designated as a cultural property in 2022. * '''Nanbu Pass and Jizō Statue''': The pass was historically significant and features a {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jizō Bosatsu|qq=}} statue believed to protect against fractures.
<gallery> File:千里海岸 02.jpg|Senri no Hama File:Kiiji - Senri Ōji.jpg|Senri Ōji with seashell offerings File:千里観音.jpg|Senri Kannon File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 01.jpg|Stone Buddhas along the ancient path File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 03.jpg|Pilgrimage path through private plum orchards File:南部峠.jpg|Old Nanbu Pass File:Minabe-touge01s2048.jpg|Jizō statue at Nanbu Pass </gallery>
== References == {{reflist|2}}
== Further Reading == * Hasegawa, Yasutaka (2007). *Kumano Ōji Pilgrimage Guidebook*. Shinpu Shobo. ISBN 9784882696292 * Nishi, Ritsu (1987). *Kumano Kodo Guide: Current Survey of the Kujūku Ōji Shrines*. Arao Seibundō. * Heibonsha (1983). *Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture*. Heibonsha.
== See Also == {{Commonscat|Kujuku-ojis|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kodo|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kodō|qq=}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.aikis.or.jp/~minabe/kumano.html Kumano Kodo] - Minabe Town Tourism Association * [http://www.town.minabe.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkzai.html Minabe Town Cultural Properties]

This article covers the ōji Shrines in the region of [[:en:Minabe, Wakayama|Minabe Town]]
==Overview== This article discusses the '''{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|qq=}}''' (九十九王子) located within [[:en:Minabe, Wakayama|Minabe, Wakayama]], [[:en:Hidaka District, Wakayama|Hidaka District, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]].

The Kujūku Ōji (九十九王子) refers to a group of [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrines]] located along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]], particularly on the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahechi|qq=}} routes. These shrines were rapidly organized primarily during the 12th to 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shugendō|qq=}}, who served as guides for imperial family members and nobles on pilgrimages to Kumano, with prayers for the protection of pilgrims.
However, after the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū War]] in 1221, pilgrimages from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}} declined, leading to the deterioration and abandonment of the shrines along the Kiiji route. This trend accelerated after the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], as Kumano pilgrimages lost their prominence. Despite some restoration efforts during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] by the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], the shrines did not regain their former status. Further changes during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] due to the [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]] reforms, shrine mergers, and urbanization led to the loss of original shrine sites, with some locations now unknown.
This article covers the Ōji shrines with identified sites in Minabe Town, Wakayama Prefecture.
== Kujūku Ōji in Minabe Town == There are three Ōji shrines within Minabe Town:
=== Iwashiro Ōji === [[File:岩代王子２.jpg|thumb|Iwashiro Ōji]] Located near the mouth of the Nishi-Iwashiro River facing the coast, the '''Iwashiro Ōji''' (岩代王子) shrine site is identified. Some historical sources such as the ''{{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja|和歌山県聖蹟|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|qq=}}'' place it here, while others like the ''{{ill|Kii Fudoki Continued|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Fudoki Continued|qq=}}'' identify a nearby Tenjin Shrine as the original site. There is a monument nearby related to {{ill|Prince Arima|ja|有間皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Arima|qq=}}. It is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 1558 Nishi-Iwashiro, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Senri Ōji === [[File:Senri-Oji01s2040.jpg|thumb|220px|Senri Ōji]] The '''Senri Ōji''' (千里王子) is located along the coast of {{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|qq=}}. It has a monument commemorating [[:en:Emperor Kazan|Emperor Kazan]]’s poetry and is adjacent to Senri Kannon Temple. It was merged into {{ill|Suga Shrine (Minabe|ja|須賀神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine (Minabe|qq=}} in 1909 but remains well-preserved. The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Minabe Ōji === [[File:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|Minabe Ōji]] The '''Minabe Ōji''' (三鍋王子) is located near [[:en:Minabe Station|Minabe Station]]. Historical records mention it as a site for religious offerings during imperial pilgrimages. It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe|ja|鹿島神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Minabe|qq=}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
== Surrounding Information == * '''{{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|qq=}}''': A significant ritual site where pilgrims performed [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]] purification rites. * '''Senri Kannon''': A Buddhist hall originally associated with Senri Ōji, now a separate temple after the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|qq=}} during the Meiji era. * '''Ancient Pilgrimage Path (Senri Ōji to Minabe Ōji)''': A well-preserved ancient road, recently designated as a cultural property in 2022. * '''Nanbu Pass and Jizō Statue''': The pass was historically significant and features a [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]] statue believed to protect against fractures.
<gallery> File:千里海岸 02.jpg|Senri no Hama File:Kiiji - Senri Ōji.jpg|Senri Ōji with seashell offerings File:千里観音.jpg|Senri Kannon File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 01.jpg|Stone Buddhas along the ancient path File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 03.jpg|Pilgrimage path through private plum orchards File:南部峠.jpg|Old Nanbu Pass File:Minabe-touge01s2048.jpg|Jizō statue at Nanbu Pass </gallery>
== References == {{reflist|2}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "九十九王子|" to "九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|"
revision.text:
This is a list of some of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the ōji Shrines in the region of [[:en:Minabe, Wakayama|Minabe Town]]
==Overview== This article discusses the '''{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|qq=}}''' (九十九王子) located within [[:en:Minabe, Wakayama|Minabe, Wakayama]], [[:en:Hidaka District, Wakayama|Hidaka District, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]].
== What Are the Kujūku Ōji? == ''For more details, see {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|qq=}}.''
The Kujūku Ōji (九十九王子) refers to a group of [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrines]] located along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]], particularly on the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahechi|qq=}} routes. These shrines were rapidly organized primarily during the 12th to 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shugendō|qq=}}, who served as guides for imperial family members and nobles on pilgrimages to Kumano, with prayers for the protection of pilgrims.
However, after the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū War]] in 1221, pilgrimages from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}} declined, leading to the deterioration and abandonment of the shrines along the Kiiji route. This trend accelerated after the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], as Kumano pilgrimages lost their prominence. Despite some restoration efforts during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] by the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], the shrines did not regain their former status. Further changes during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] due to the [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]] reforms, shrine mergers, and urbanization led to the loss of original shrine sites, with some locations now unknown.
This article covers the Ōji shrines with identified sites in Minabe Town, Wakayama Prefecture.
== Kujūku Ōji in Minabe Town == There are three Ōji shrines within Minabe Town:
=== Iwashiro Ōji === [[File:岩代王子２.jpg|thumb|Iwashiro Ōji]] Located near the mouth of the Nishi-Iwashiro River facing the coast, the '''Iwashiro Ōji''' (岩代王子) shrine site is identified. Some historical sources such as the ''{{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja|和歌山県聖蹟|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|qq=}}'' place it here, while others like the ''{{ill|Kii Fudoki Continued|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Fudoki Continued|qq=}}'' identify a nearby Tenjin Shrine as the original site. There is a monument nearby related to {{ill|Prince Arima|ja|有間皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Arima|qq=}}. It is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 1558 Nishi-Iwashiro, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Senri Ōji === [[File:Senri-Oji01s2040.jpg|thumb|220px|Senri Ōji]] The '''Senri Ōji''' (千里王子) is located along the coast of {{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|qq=}}. It has a monument commemorating [[:en:Emperor Kazan|Emperor Kazan]]’s poetry and is adjacent to Senri Kannon Temple. It was merged into {{ill|Suga Shrine (Minabe|ja|須賀神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine (Minabe|qq=}} in 1909 but remains well-preserved. The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Minabe Ōji === [[File:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|Minabe Ōji]] The '''Minabe Ōji''' (三鍋王子) is located near [[:en:Minabe Station|Minabe Station]]. Historical records mention it as a site for religious offerings during imperial pilgrimages. It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe|ja|鹿島神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Minabe|qq=}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
== Surrounding Information == * '''{{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|qq=}}''': A significant ritual site where pilgrims performed [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]] purification rites. * '''Senri Kannon''': A Buddhist hall originally associated with Senri Ōji, now a separate temple after the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|qq=}} during the Meiji era. * '''Ancient Pilgrimage Path (Senri Ōji to Minabe Ōji)''': A well-preserved ancient road, recently designated as a cultural property in 2022. * '''Nanbu Pass and Jizō Statue''': The pass was historically significant and features a [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]] statue believed to protect against fractures.
<gallery> File:千里海岸 02.jpg|Senri no Hama File:Kiiji - Senri Ōji.jpg|Senri Ōji with seashell offerings File:千里観音.jpg|Senri Kannon File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 01.jpg|Stone Buddhas along the ancient path File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 03.jpg|Pilgrimage path through private plum orchards File:南部峠.jpg|Old Nanbu Pass File:Minabe-touge01s2048.jpg|Jizō statue at Nanbu Pass </gallery>
== References == {{reflist|2}}
== Further Reading == * Hasegawa, Yasutaka (2007). *Kumano Ōji Pilgrimage Guidebook*. Shinpu Shobo. ISBN 9784882696292 * Nishi, Ritsu (1987). *Kumano Kodo Guide: Current Survey of the Kujūku Ōji Shrines*. Arao Seibundō. * Heibonsha (1983). *Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture*. Heibonsha.

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:みなべ町の建築物
revision.text:
This is a list of some of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the ōji Shrines in the region of [[:en:Minabe, Wakayama|Minabe Town]]
==Overview== This article discusses the '''{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}''' (九十九王子) located within [[:en:Minabe, Wakayama|Minabe, Wakayama]], [[:en:Hidaka District, Wakayama|Hidaka District, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]].
== What Are the Kujūku Ōji? == ''For more details, see {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}.''
The Kujūku Ōji (九十九王子) refers to a group of [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrines]] located along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]], particularly on the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahechi|qq=}} routes. These shrines were rapidly organized primarily during the 12th to 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shugendō|qq=}}, who served as guides for imperial family members and nobles on pilgrimages to Kumano, with prayers for the protection of pilgrims.
However, after the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū War]] in 1221, pilgrimages from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}} declined, leading to the deterioration and abandonment of the shrines along the Kiiji route. This trend accelerated after the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], as Kumano pilgrimages lost their prominence. Despite some restoration efforts during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] by the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], the shrines did not regain their former status. Further changes during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] due to the [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]] reforms, shrine mergers, and urbanization led to the loss of original shrine sites, with some locations now unknown.
This article covers the Ōji shrines with identified sites in Minabe Town, Wakayama Prefecture.
== Kujūku Ōji in Minabe Town == There are three Ōji shrines within Minabe Town:
=== Iwashiro Ōji === [[File:岩代王子２.jpg|thumb|Iwashiro Ōji]] Located near the mouth of the Nishi-Iwashiro River facing the coast, the '''Iwashiro Ōji''' (岩代王子) shrine site is identified. Some historical sources such as the ''{{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja|和歌山県聖蹟|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|qq=}}'' place it here, while others like the ''{{ill|Kii Fudoki Continued|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Fudoki Continued|qq=}}'' identify a nearby Tenjin Shrine as the original site. There is a monument nearby related to {{ill|Prince Arima|ja|有間皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Arima|qq=}}. It is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 1558 Nishi-Iwashiro, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Senri Ōji === [[File:Senri-Oji01s2040.jpg|thumb|220px|Senri Ōji]] The '''Senri Ōji''' (千里王子) is located along the coast of {{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|qq=}}. It has a monument commemorating [[:en:Emperor Kazan|Emperor Kazan]]’s poetry and is adjacent to Senri Kannon Temple. It was merged into {{ill|Suga Shrine (Minabe|ja|須賀神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine (Minabe|qq=}} in 1909 but remains well-preserved. The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Minabe Ōji === [[File:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|Minabe Ōji]] The '''Minabe Ōji''' (三鍋王子) is located near [[:en:Minabe Station|Minabe Station]]. Historical records mention it as a site for religious offerings during imperial pilgrimages. It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe|ja|鹿島神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Minabe|qq=}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
== Surrounding Information == * '''{{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|qq=}}''': A significant ritual site where pilgrims performed [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]] purification rites. * '''Senri Kannon''': A Buddhist hall originally associated with Senri Ōji, now a separate temple after the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|qq=}} during the Meiji era. * '''Ancient Pilgrimage Path (Senri Ōji to Minabe Ōji)''': A well-preserved ancient road, recently designated as a cultural property in 2022. * '''Nanbu Pass and Jizō Statue''': The pass was historically significant and features a [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]] statue believed to protect against fractures.
<gallery> File:千里海岸 02.jpg|Senri no Hama File:Kiiji - Senri Ōji.jpg|Senri Ōji with seashell offerings File:千里観音.jpg|Senri Kannon File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 01.jpg|Stone Buddhas along the ancient path File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 03.jpg|Pilgrimage path through private plum orchards File:南部峠.jpg|Old Nanbu Pass File:Minabe-touge01s2048.jpg|Jizō statue at Nanbu Pass </gallery>
== References == {{reflist|2}}
== Further Reading == * Hasegawa, Yasutaka (2007). *Kumano Ōji Pilgrimage Guidebook*. Shinpu Shobo. ISBN 9784882696292 * Nishi, Ritsu (1987). *Kumano Kodo Guide: Current Survey of the Kujūku Ōji Shrines*. Arao Seibundō. * Heibonsha (1983). *Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture*. Heibonsha.
== See Also == {{Commonscat|Kujuku-ojis|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.aikis.or.jp/~minabe/kumano.html Kumano Kodo] - Minabe Town Tourism Association * [http://www.town.minabe.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkzai.html Minabe Town Cultural Properties]

revision.text:
This is a list of some of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the ōji Shrines in the region of [[:en:Minabe, Wakayama|Minabe Town]]
==Overview== This article discusses the '''{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}''' (九十九王子) located within [[:en:Minabe, Wakayama|Minabe, Wakayama]], [[:en:Hidaka District, Wakayama|Hidaka District, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]].
== What Are the Kujūku Ōji? == ''For more details, see {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}.''
The Kujūku Ōji (九十九王子) refers to a group of [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrines]] located along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]], particularly on the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahechi|qq=}} routes. These shrines were rapidly organized primarily during the 12th to 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shugendō|qq=}}, who served as guides for imperial family members and nobles on pilgrimages to Kumano, with prayers for the protection of pilgrims.
However, after the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū War]] in 1221, pilgrimages from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}} declined, leading to the deterioration and abandonment of the shrines along the Kiiji route. This trend accelerated after the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], as Kumano pilgrimages lost their prominence. Despite some restoration efforts during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] by the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], the shrines did not regain their former status. Further changes during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] due to the [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]] reforms, shrine mergers, and urbanization led to the loss of original shrine sites, with some locations now unknown.
This article covers the Ōji shrines with identified sites in Minabe Town, Wakayama Prefecture.
== Kujūku Ōji in Minabe Town == There are three Ōji shrines within Minabe Town:
=== Iwashiro Ōji === [[File:岩代王子２.jpg|thumb|Iwashiro Ōji]] Located near the mouth of the Nishi-Iwashiro River facing the coast, the '''Iwashiro Ōji''' (岩代王子) shrine site is identified. Some historical sources such as the ''{{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja|和歌山県聖蹟|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|qq=}}'' place it here, while others like the ''{{ill|Kii Fudoki Continued|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Fudoki Continued|qq=}}'' identify a nearby Tenjin Shrine as the original site. There is a monument nearby related to {{ill|Prince Arima|ja|有間皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Arima|qq=}}. It is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 1558 Nishi-Iwashiro, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Senri Ōji === [[File:Senri-Oji01s2040.jpg|thumb|220px|Senri Ōji]] The '''Senri Ōji''' (千里王子) is located along the coast of {{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|qq=}}. It has a monument commemorating [[:en:Emperor Kazan|Emperor Kazan]]’s poetry and is adjacent to Senri Kannon Temple. It was merged into {{ill|Suga Shrine (Minabe|ja|須賀神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine (Minabe|qq=}} in 1909 but remains well-preserved. The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Minabe Ōji === [[File:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|Minabe Ōji]] The '''Minabe Ōji''' (三鍋王子) is located near [[:en:Minabe Station|Minabe Station]]. Historical records mention it as a site for religious offerings during imperial pilgrimages. It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe|ja|鹿島神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Minabe|qq=}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
== Surrounding Information == * '''{{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|qq=}}''': A significant ritual site where pilgrims performed [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]] purification rites. * '''Senri Kannon''': A Buddhist hall originally associated with Senri Ōji, now a separate temple after the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|qq=}} during the Meiji era. * '''Ancient Pilgrimage Path (Senri Ōji to Minabe Ōji)''': A well-preserved ancient road, recently designated as a cultural property in 2022. * '''Nanbu Pass and Jizō Statue''': The pass was historically significant and features a [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]] statue believed to protect against fractures.
<gallery> File:千里海岸 02.jpg|Senri no Hama File:Kiiji - Senri Ōji.jpg|Senri Ōji with seashell offerings File:千里観音.jpg|Senri Kannon File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 01.jpg|Stone Buddhas along the ancient path File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 03.jpg|Pilgrimage path through private plum orchards File:南部峠.jpg|Old Nanbu Pass File:Minabe-touge01s2048.jpg|Jizō statue at Nanbu Pass </gallery>
== References == {{reflist|2}}
== Further Reading == * Hasegawa, Yasutaka (2007). *Kumano Ōji Pilgrimage Guidebook*. Shinpu Shobo. ISBN 9784882696292 * Nishi, Ritsu (1987). *Kumano Kodo Guide: Current Survey of the Kujūku Ōji Shrines*. Arao Seibundō. * Heibonsha (1983). *Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture*. Heibonsha.
== See Also == {{Commonscat|Kujuku-ojis|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.aikis.or.jp/~minabe/kumano.html Kumano Kodo] - Minabe Town Tourism Association * [http://www.town.minabe.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkzai.html Minabe Town Cultural Properties]
