This is a list of some of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the ōji Shrines in the region of [[:en:Minabe, Wakayama|Minabe Town]]
==Overview== This article discusses the '''{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}''' (九十九王子) located within [[:en:Minabe, Wakayama|Minabe, Wakayama]], [[:en:Hidaka District, Wakayama|Hidaka District, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]].
== What Are the Kujūku Ōji? == ''For more details, see {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}.''
The Kujūku Ōji (九十九王子) refers to a group of [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrines]] located along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]], particularly on the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahechi|qq=}} routes. These shrines were rapidly organized primarily during the 12th to 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shugendō|qq=}}, who served as guides for imperial family members and nobles on pilgrimages to Kumano, with prayers for the protection of pilgrims.
However, after the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū War]] in 1221, pilgrimages from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}} declined, leading to the deterioration and abandonment of the shrines along the Kiiji route. This trend accelerated after the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], as Kumano pilgrimages lost their prominence. Despite some restoration efforts during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] by the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], the shrines did not regain their former status. Further changes during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] due to the [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]] reforms, shrine mergers, and urbanization led to the loss of original shrine sites, with some locations now unknown.
This article covers the Ōji shrines with identified sites in Minabe Town, Wakayama Prefecture.
== Kujūku Ōji in Minabe Town == There are three Ōji shrines within Minabe Town:
=== Iwashiro Ōji === [[File:岩代王子２.jpg|thumb|Iwashiro Ōji]] Located near the mouth of the Nishi-Iwashiro River facing the coast, the '''Iwashiro Ōji''' (岩代王子) shrine site is identified. Some historical sources such as the ''{{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja|和歌山県聖蹟|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|qq=}}'' place it here, while others like the ''{{ill|Kii Fudoki Continued|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Fudoki Continued|qq=}}'' identify a nearby Tenjin Shrine as the original site. There is a monument nearby related to {{ill|Prince Arima|ja|有間皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Arima|qq=}}. It is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 1558 Nishi-Iwashiro, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Senri Ōji === [[File:Senri-Oji01s2040.jpg|thumb|220px|Senri Ōji]] The '''Senri Ōji''' (千里王子) is located along the coast of {{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|qq=}}. It has a monument commemorating [[:en:Emperor Kazan|Emperor Kazan]]’s poetry and is adjacent to Senri Kannon Temple. It was merged into {{ill|Suga Shrine (Minabe|ja|須賀神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine (Minabe|qq=}} in 1909 but remains well-preserved. The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Minabe Ōji === [[File:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|Minabe Ōji]] The '''Minabe Ōji''' (三鍋王子) is located near [[:en:Minabe Station|Minabe Station]]. Historical records mention it as a site for religious offerings during imperial pilgrimages. It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe|ja|鹿島神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Minabe|qq=}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
== Surrounding Information == * '''{{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|qq=}}''': A significant ritual site where pilgrims performed [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]] purification rites. * '''Senri Kannon''': A Buddhist hall originally associated with Senri Ōji, now a separate temple after the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|qq=}} during the Meiji era. * '''Ancient Pilgrimage Path (Senri Ōji to Minabe Ōji)''': A well-preserved ancient road, recently designated as a cultural property in 2022. * '''Nanbu Pass and Jizō Statue''': The pass was historically significant and features a [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]] statue believed to protect against fractures.
<gallery> File:千里海岸 02.jpg|Senri no Hama File:Kiiji - Senri Ōji.jpg|Senri Ōji with seashell offerings File:千里観音.jpg|Senri Kannon File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 01.jpg|Stone Buddhas along the ancient path File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 03.jpg|Pilgrimage path through private plum orchards File:南部峠.jpg|Old Nanbu Pass File:Minabe-touge01s2048.jpg|Jizō statue at Nanbu Pass </gallery>
== Further Reading == * Hasegawa, Yasutaka (2007). *Kumano Ōji Pilgrimage Guidebook*. Shinpu Shobo. ISBN 9784882696292 * Nishi, Ritsu (1987). *Kumano Kodo Guide: Current Survey of the Kujūku Ōji Shrines*. Arao Seibundō. * Heibonsha (1983). *Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture*. Heibonsha.
== See Also == {{Commonscat|Kujūku Ōji|ja|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji}} * {{ill|Kumano shrine|ja|熊野信仰|lt=Kumano faith|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji}}

This article covers the Ōji shrines with identified sites in Minabe Town, Wakayama Prefecture.
== Kujūku Ōji in Minabe Town == There are three Ōji shrines within Minabe Town:
=== Iwashiro Ōji === [[File:岩代王子２.jpg|thumb|Iwashiro Ōji]] Located near the mouth of the Nishi-Iwashiro River facing the coast, the '''Iwashiro Ōji''' (岩代王子) shrine site is identified. Some historical sources such as the ''{{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja|和歌山県聖蹟|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|qq=}}'' place it here, while others like the ''{{ill|Kii Fudoki Continued|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Fudoki Continued|qq=}}'' identify a nearby Tenjin Shrine as the original site. There is a monument nearby related to {{ill|Prince Arima|ja|有間皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Arima|qq=}}. It is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 1558 Nishi-Iwashiro, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Senri Ōji === [[File:Senri-Oji01s2040.jpg|thumb|220px|Senri Ōji]] The '''Senri Ōji''' (千里王子) is located along the coast of {{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|qq=}}. It has a monument commemorating [[:en:Emperor Kazan|Emperor Kazan]]’s poetry and is adjacent to Senri Kannon Temple. It was merged into {{ill|Suga Shrine (Minabe|ja|須賀神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine (Minabe|qq=}} in 1909 but remains well-preserved. The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Minabe Ōji === [[File:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|Minabe Ōji]] The '''Minabe Ōji''' (三鍋王子) is located near [[:en:Minabe Station|Minabe Station]]. Historical records mention it as a site for religious offerings during imperial pilgrimages. It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe|ja|鹿島神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Minabe|qq=}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
== Surrounding Information == * '''{{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|qq=}}''': A significant ritual site where pilgrims performed [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]] purification rites. * '''Senri Kannon''': A Buddhist hall originally associated with Senri Ōji, now a separate temple after the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|qq=}} during the Meiji era. * '''Ancient Pilgrimage Path (Senri Ōji to Minabe Ōji)''': A well-preserved ancient road, recently designated as a cultural property in 2022. * '''Nanbu Pass and Jizō Statue''': The pass was historically significant and features a [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]] statue believed to protect against fractures.
<gallery> File:千里海岸 02.jpg|Senri no Hama File:Kiiji - Senri Ōji.jpg|Senri Ōji with seashell offerings File:千里観音.jpg|Senri Kannon File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 01.jpg|Stone Buddhas along the ancient path File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 03.jpg|Pilgrimage path through private plum orchards File:南部峠.jpg|Old Nanbu Pass File:Minabe-touge01s2048.jpg|Jizō statue at Nanbu Pass </gallery>
== Further Reading == * Hasegawa, Yasutaka (2007). *Kumano Ōji Pilgrimage Guidebook*. Shinpu Shobo. ISBN 9784882696292 * Nishi, Ritsu (1987). *Kumano Kodo Guide: Current Survey of the Kujūku Ōji Shrines*. Arao Seibundō. * Heibonsha (1983). *Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture*. Heibonsha.
== See Also == {{Commonscat|Kujūku Ōji|ja|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji}} * {{ill|Kumano shrine|ja|熊野信仰|lt=Kumano faith|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji}}

This is a list of some of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the ōji Shrines in the region of [[:en:Minabe, Wakayama|Minabe Town]]
==Overview== This article discusses the '''{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}''' (九十九王子) located within [[:en:Minabe, Wakayama|Minabe, Wakayama]], [[:en:Hidaka District, Wakayama|Hidaka District, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]].
== What Are the Ninety-Nine Ōji? == ''For more details, see {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}.''
The Ninety-Nine Ōji (九十九王子) refers to a group of [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrines]] located along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]], particularly on the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahechi|qq=}} routes. These shrines were rapidly organized primarily during the 12th to 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shugendō|qq=}}, who served as guides for imperial family members and nobles on pilgrimages to Kumano, with prayers for the protection of pilgrims.
However, after the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū War]] in 1221, pilgrimages from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}} declined, leading to the deterioration and abandonment of the shrines along the Kiiji route. This trend accelerated after the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], as Kumano pilgrimages lost their prominence. Despite some restoration efforts during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] by the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], the shrines did not regain their former status. Further changes during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] due to the [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]] reforms, shrine mergers, and urbanization led to the loss of original shrine sites, with some locations now unknown.
This article covers the Ōji shrines with identified sites in Minabe Town, Wakayama Prefecture.
== Ninety-Nine Ōji in Minabe Town == There are three Ōji shrines within Minabe Town:
=== Iwashiro Ōji === [[File:岩代王子２.jpg|thumb|Iwashiro Ōji]] Located near the mouth of the Nishi-Iwashiro River facing the coast, the '''Iwashiro Ōji''' (岩代王子) shrine site is identified. Some historical sources such as the ''{{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja|和歌山県聖蹟|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|qq=}}'' place it here, while others like the ''{{ill|Kii Fudoki Continued|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Fudoki Continued|qq=}}'' identify a nearby Tenjin Shrine as the original site. There is a monument nearby related to {{ill|Prince Arima|ja|有間皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Arima|qq=}}. It is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 1558 Nishi-Iwashiro, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Senri Ōji === [[File:Senri-Oji01s2040.jpg|thumb|220px|Senri Ōji]] The '''Senri Ōji''' (千里王子) is located along the coast of {{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|qq=}}. It has a monument commemorating [[:en:Emperor Kazan|Emperor Kazan]]’s poetry and is adjacent to Senri Kannon Temple. It was merged into {{ill|Suga Shrine (Minabe|ja|須賀神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine (Minabe|qq=}} in 1909 but remains well-preserved. The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Minabe Ōji === [[File:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|Minabe Ōji]] The '''Minabe Ōji''' (三鍋王子) is located near [[:en:Minabe Station|Minabe Station]]. Historical records mention it as a site for religious offerings during imperial pilgrimages. It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe|ja|鹿島神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Minabe|qq=}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
== Surrounding Information == * '''{{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|qq=}}''': A significant ritual site where pilgrims performed [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]] purification rites. * '''Senri Kannon''': A Buddhist hall originally associated with Senri Ōji, now a separate temple after the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|qq=}} during the Meiji era. * '''Ancient Pilgrimage Path (Senri Ōji to Minabe Ōji)''': A well-preserved ancient road, recently designated as a cultural property in 2022. * '''Nanbu Pass and Jizō Statue''': The pass was historically significant and features a [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]] statue believed to protect against fractures.
<gallery> File:千里海岸 02.jpg|Senri no Hama File:Kiiji - Senri Ōji.jpg|Senri Ōji with seashell offerings File:千里観音.jpg|Senri Kannon File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 01.jpg|Stone Buddhas along the ancient path File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 03.jpg|Pilgrimage path through private plum orchards File:南部峠.jpg|Old Nanbu Pass File:Minabe-touge01s2048.jpg|Jizō statue at Nanbu Pass </gallery>
== Further Reading == * Hasegawa, Yasutaka (2007). *Kumano Ōji Pilgrimage Guidebook*. Shinpu Shobo. ISBN 9784882696292 * Nishi, Ritsu (1987). *Kumano Kodo Guide: Current Survey of the Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines*. Arao Seibundō. * Heibonsha (1983). *Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture*. Heibonsha.
== See Also == {{Commonscat|Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji}} * {{ill|Kumano shrine|ja|熊野信仰|lt=Kumano faith|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji}}

<!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--><!--みなべ町)--><!--みなべ町)--> [[Category:みなべ町の建築物]] [[Category:みなべ町の歴史]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Wakayama Prefecture]]

This is a list of some of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the ōji Shrines in the region of [[:en:Minabe, Wakayama|Minabe Town]]
==Overview== This article discusses the '''{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}''' (九十九王子) located within [[:en:Minabe, Wakayama|Minabe, Wakayama]], [[:en:Hidaka District, Wakayama|Hidaka District, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]].
== What Are the Ninety-Nine Ōji? == ''For more details, see {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}.''
The Ninety-Nine Ōji (九十九王子) refers to a group of [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrines]] located along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]], particularly on the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahechi|qq=}} routes. These shrines were rapidly organized primarily during the 12th to 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shugendō|qq=}}, who served as guides for imperial family members and nobles on pilgrimages to Kumano, with prayers for the protection of pilgrims.
However, after the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū War]] in 1221, pilgrimages from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}} declined, leading to the deterioration and abandonment of the shrines along the Kiiji route. This trend accelerated after the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], as Kumano pilgrimages lost their prominence. Despite some restoration efforts during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] by the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], the shrines did not regain their former status. Further changes during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] due to the [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]] reforms, shrine mergers, and urbanization led to the loss of original shrine sites, with some locations now unknown.
This article covers the Ōji shrines with identified sites in Minabe Town, Wakayama Prefecture.
== Ninety-Nine Ōji in Minabe Town == There are three Ōji shrines within Minabe Town:
=== Iwashiro Ōji === [[File:岩代王子２.jpg|thumb|Iwashiro Ōji]] Located near the mouth of the Nishi-Iwashiro River facing the coast, the '''Iwashiro Ōji''' (岩代王子) shrine site is identified. Some historical sources such as the ''{{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja|和歌山県聖蹟|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|qq=}}'' place it here, while others like the ''{{ill|Kii Fudoki Continued|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Fudoki Continued|qq=}}'' identify a nearby Tenjin Shrine as the original site. There is a monument nearby related to {{ill|Prince Arima|ja|有間皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Arima|qq=}}. It is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 1558 Nishi-Iwashiro, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Senri Ōji === [[File:Senri-Oji01s2040.jpg|thumb|220px|Senri Ōji]] The '''Senri Ōji''' (千里王子) is located along the coast of {{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|qq=}}. It has a monument commemorating [[:en:Emperor Kazan|Emperor Kazan]]’s poetry and is adjacent to Senri Kannon Temple. It was merged into {{ill|Suga Shrine (Minabe|ja|須賀神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine (Minabe|qq=}} in 1909 but remains well-preserved. The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Minabe Ōji === [[File:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|Minabe Ōji]] The '''Minabe Ōji''' (三鍋王子) is located near [[:en:Minabe Station|Minabe Station]]. Historical records mention it as a site for religious offerings during imperial pilgrimages. It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe|ja|鹿島神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Minabe|qq=}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
== Surrounding Information == * '''{{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|qq=}}''': A significant ritual site where pilgrims performed [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]] purification rites. * '''Senri Kannon''': A Buddhist hall originally associated with Senri Ōji, now a separate temple after the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|qq=}} during the Meiji era. * '''Ancient Pilgrimage Path (Senri Ōji to Minabe Ōji)''': A well-preserved ancient road, recently designated as a cultural property in 2022. * '''Nanbu Pass and Jizō Statue''': The pass was historically significant and features a [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]] statue believed to protect against fractures.
<gallery> File:千里海岸 02.jpg|Senri no Hama File:Kiiji - Senri Ōji.jpg|Senri Ōji with seashell offerings File:千里観音.jpg|Senri Kannon File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 01.jpg|Stone Buddhas along the ancient path File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 03.jpg|Pilgrimage path through private plum orchards File:南部峠.jpg|Old Nanbu Pass File:Minabe-touge01s2048.jpg|Jizō statue at Nanbu Pass </gallery>
== Further Reading == * Hasegawa, Yasutaka (2007). *Kumano Ōji Pilgrimage Guidebook*. Shinpu Shobo. ISBN 9784882696292 * Nishi, Ritsu (1987). *Kumano Kodo Guide: Current Survey of the Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines*. Arao Seibundō. * Heibonsha (1983). *Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture*. Heibonsha.
== See Also == {{Commonscat|Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji}} * {{ill|Kumano shrine|ja|熊野信仰|lt=Kumano faith|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji}}

This is a list of some of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the ōji Shrines in the region of [[:en:Minabe, Wakayama|Minabe Town]]
==Overview== This article discusses the '''{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}''' (九十九王子) located within [[:en:Minabe, Wakayama|Minabe, Wakayama]], [[:en:Hidaka District, Wakayama|Hidaka District, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]].
== What Are the Ninety-Nine Ōji? == ''For more details, see {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}.''
The Ninety-Nine Ōji (九十九王子) refers to a group of [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrines]] located along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]], particularly on the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahechi|qq=}} routes. These shrines were rapidly organized primarily during the 12th to 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shugendō|qq=}}, who served as guides for imperial family members and nobles on pilgrimages to Kumano, with prayers for the protection of pilgrims.
However, after the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū War]] in 1221, pilgrimages from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}} declined, leading to the deterioration and abandonment of the shrines along the Kiiji route. This trend accelerated after the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], as Kumano pilgrimages lost their prominence. Despite some restoration efforts during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] by the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], the shrines did not regain their former status. Further changes during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] due to the [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]] reforms, shrine mergers, and urbanization led to the loss of original shrine sites, with some locations now unknown.
This article covers the Ōji shrines with identified sites in Minabe Town, Wakayama Prefecture.
== Ninety-Nine Ōji in Minabe Town == There are three Ōji shrines within Minabe Town:
=== Iwashiro Ōji === [[File:岩代王子２.jpg|thumb|Iwashiro Ōji]] Located near the mouth of the Nishi-Iwashiro River facing the coast, the '''Iwashiro Ōji''' (岩代王子) shrine site is identified. Some historical sources such as the ''{{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja|和歌山県聖蹟|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|qq=}}'' place it here, while others like the ''{{ill|Kii Fudoki Continued|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Fudoki Continued|qq=}}'' identify a nearby Tenjin Shrine as the original site. There is a monument nearby related to {{ill|Prince Arima|ja|有間皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Arima|qq=}}. It is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 1558 Nishi-Iwashiro, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Senri Ōji === [[File:Senri-Oji01s2040.jpg|thumb|220px|Senri Ōji]] The '''Senri Ōji''' (千里王子) is located along the coast of {{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|qq=}}. It has a monument commemorating [[:en:Emperor Kazan|Emperor Kazan]]’s poetry and is adjacent to Senri Kannon Temple. It was merged into {{ill|Suga Shrine (Minabe|ja|須賀神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine (Minabe|qq=}} in 1909 but remains well-preserved. The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Minabe Ōji === [[File:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|Minabe Ōji]] The '''Minabe Ōji''' (三鍋王子) is located near [[:en:Minabe Station|Minabe Station]]. Historical records mention it as a site for religious offerings during imperial pilgrimages. It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe|ja|鹿島神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Minabe|qq=}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
== Surrounding Information == * '''{{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|qq=}}''': A significant ritual site where pilgrims performed [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]] purification rites. * '''Senri Kannon''': A Buddhist hall originally associated with Senri Ōji, now a separate temple after the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|qq=}} during the Meiji era. * '''Ancient Pilgrimage Path (Senri Ōji to Minabe Ōji)''': A well-preserved ancient road, recently designated as a cultural property in 2022. * '''Nanbu Pass and Jizō Statue''': The pass was historically significant and features a [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]] statue believed to protect against fractures.

This is a list of some of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the ōji Shrines in the region of [[:en:Minabe, Wakayama|Minabe Town]]
==Overview== This article discusses the '''{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}''' (九十九王子) located within [[:en:Minabe, Wakayama|Minabe, Wakayama]], [[:en:Hidaka District, Wakayama|Hidaka District, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]].
== What Are the Ninety-Nine Ōji? == ''For more details, see {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}.''
The Ninety-Nine Ōji (九十九王子) refers to a group of [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrines]] located along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]], particularly on the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahechi|qq=}} routes. These shrines were rapidly organized primarily during the 12th to 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shugendō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who served as guides for imperial family members and nobles on pilgrimages to Kumano, with prayers for the protection of pilgrims.
However, after the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū War]] in 1221, pilgrimages from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}} declined, leading to the deterioration and abandonment of the shrines along the Kiiji route. This trend accelerated after the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], as Kumano pilgrimages lost their prominence. Despite some restoration efforts during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] by the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], the shrines did not regain their former status. Further changes during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] due to the [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]] reforms, shrine mergers, and urbanization led to the loss of original shrine sites, with some locations now unknown.
This article covers the Ōji shrines with identified sites in Minabe Town, Wakayama Prefecture.
== Ninety-Nine Ōji in Minabe Town == There are three Ōji shrines within Minabe Town:
=== Iwashiro Ōji === [[File:岩代王子２.jpg|thumb|Iwashiro Ōji]] Located near the mouth of the Nishi-Iwashiro River facing the coast, the '''Iwashiro Ōji''' (岩代王子) shrine site is identified. Some historical sources such as the ''{{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja|和歌山県聖蹟|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' place it here, while others like the ''{{ill|Kii Fudoki Continued|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Fudoki Continued|qq=}}'' identify a nearby Tenjin Shrine as the original site. There is a monument nearby related to {{ill|Prince Arima|ja|有間皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Arima|qq=}}. It is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 1558 Nishi-Iwashiro, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Senri Ōji === [[File:Senri-Oji01s2040.jpg|thumb|220px|Senri Ōji]] The '''Senri Ōji''' (千里王子) is located along the coast of {{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|qq=}}. It has a monument commemorating [[:en:Emperor Kazan|Emperor Kazan]]’s poetry and is adjacent to Senri Kannon Temple. It was merged into {{ill|Suga Shrine (Minabe|ja|須賀神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine (Minabe|qq=}} in 1909 but remains well-preserved. The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Minabe Ōji === [[File:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|Minabe Ōji]] The '''Minabe Ōji''' (三鍋王子) is located near [[:en:Minabe Station|Minabe Station]]. Historical records mention it as a site for religious offerings during imperial pilgrimages. It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe|ja|鹿島神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Minabe|qq=}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
== Surrounding Information == * '''{{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|qq=}}''': A significant ritual site where pilgrims performed [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]] purification rites. * '''Senri Kannon''': A Buddhist hall originally associated with Senri Ōji, now a separate temple after the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} during the Meiji era. * '''Ancient Pilgrimage Path (Senri Ōji to Minabe Ōji)''': A well-preserved ancient road, recently designated as a cultural property in 2022. * '''Nanbu Pass and Jizō Statue''': The pass was historically significant and features a [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]] statue believed to protect against fractures.
<gallery> File:千里海岸 02.jpg|Senri no Hama File:Kiiji - Senri Ōji.jpg|Senri Ōji with seashell offerings File:千里観音.jpg|Senri Kannon File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 01.jpg|Stone Buddhas along the ancient path File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 03.jpg|Pilgrimage path through private plum orchards File:南部峠.jpg|Old Nanbu Pass File:Minabe-touge01s2048.jpg|Jizō statue at Nanbu Pass </gallery>
== Further Reading == * Hasegawa, Yasutaka (2007). *Kumano Ōji Pilgrimage Guidebook*. Shinpu Shobo. ISBN 9784882696292 * Nishi, Ritsu (1987). *Kumano Kodo Guide: Current Survey of the Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines*. Arao Seibundō. * Heibonsha (1983). *Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture*. Heibonsha.
== See Also == {{Commonscat|Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji}} * {{ill|Kumano shrine|ja|熊野信仰|lt=Kumano faith|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji}}

The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).

However, after the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū War]] in 1221, pilgrimages from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}} declined, leading to the deterioration and abandonment of the shrines along the Kiiji route. This trend accelerated after the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], as Kumano pilgrimages lost their prominence. Despite some restoration efforts during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] by the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], the shrines did not regain their former status. Further changes during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] due to the [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]] reforms, shrine mergers, and urbanization led to the loss of original shrine sites, with some locations now unknown.
This article covers the Ōji shrines with identified sites in Minabe Town, Wakayama Prefecture.
== Ninety-Nine Ōji in Minabe Town == There are three Ōji shrines within Minabe Town:
=== Iwashiro Ōji === [[File:岩代王子２.jpg|thumb|Iwashiro Ōji]] Located near the mouth of the Nishi-Iwashiro River facing the coast, the '''Iwashiro Ōji''' (岩代王子) shrine site is identified. Some historical sources such as the ''{{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja|和歌山県聖蹟|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' place it here, while others like the ''{{ill|Kii Fudoki Continued|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Fudoki Continued|qq=}}'' identify a nearby Tenjin Shrine as the original site. There is a monument nearby related to {{ill|Prince Arima|ja|有間皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Arima|qq=}}. It is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 1558 Nishi-Iwashiro, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Senri Ōji === [[File:Senri-Oji01s2040.jpg|thumb|220px|Senri Ōji]] The '''Senri Ōji''' (千里王子) is located along the coast of {{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|qq=}}. It has a monument commemorating [[:en:Emperor Kazan|Emperor Kazan]]’s poetry and is adjacent to Senri Kannon Temple. It was merged into {{ill|Suga Shrine (Minabe|ja|須賀神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine (Minabe|qq=}} in 1909 but remains well-preserved. The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Minabe Ōji === [[File:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|Minabe Ōji]] The '''Minabe Ōji''' (三鍋王子) is located near [[:en:Minabe Station|Minabe Station]]. Historical records mention it as a site for religious offerings during imperial pilgrimages. It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe|ja|鹿島神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Minabe|qq=}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
== Surrounding Information == * '''{{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|qq=}}''': A significant ritual site where pilgrims performed [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]] purification rites. * '''Senri Kannon''': A Buddhist hall originally associated with Senri Ōji, now a separate temple after the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} during the Meiji era. * '''Ancient Pilgrimage Path (Senri Ōji to Minabe Ōji)''': A well-preserved ancient road, recently designated as a cultural property in 2022. * '''Nanbu Pass and Jizō Statue''': The pass was historically significant and features a [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]] statue believed to protect against fractures.
<gallery> File:千里海岸 02.jpg|Senri no Hama File:Kiiji - Senri Ōji.jpg|Senri Ōji with seashell offerings File:千里観音.jpg|Senri Kannon File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 01.jpg|Stone Buddhas along the ancient path File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 03.jpg|Pilgrimage path through private plum orchards File:南部峠.jpg|Old Nanbu Pass File:Minabe-touge01s2048.jpg|Jizō statue at Nanbu Pass </gallery>
== Further Reading == * Hasegawa, Yasutaka (2007). *Kumano Ōji Pilgrimage Guidebook*. Shinpu Shobo. ISBN 9784882696292 * Nishi, Ritsu (1987). *Kumano Kodo Guide: Current Survey of the Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines*. Arao Seibundō. * Heibonsha (1983). *Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture*. Heibonsha.

This is a list of some of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the ōji Shrines in the region of [[:en:Minabe, Wakayama|Minabe Town]]
==Overview== This article discusses the '''{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}''' (九十九王子) located within [[:en:Minabe, Wakayama|Minabe, Wakayama]], [[:en:Hidaka District, Wakayama|Hidaka District, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]].
== What Are the Ninety-Nine Ōji? == ''For more details, see {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}.''
The Ninety-Nine Ōji (九十九王子) refers to a group of [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrines]] located along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]], particularly on the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahechi|qq=}} routes. These shrines were rapidly organized primarily during the 12th to 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shugendō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who served as guides for imperial family members and nobles on pilgrimages to Kumano, with prayers for the protection of pilgrims.
However, after the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū War]] in 1221, pilgrimages from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}} declined, leading to the deterioration and abandonment of the shrines along the Kiiji route. This trend accelerated after the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], as Kumano pilgrimages lost their prominence. Despite some restoration efforts during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] by the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], the shrines did not regain their former status. Further changes during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] due to the [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]] reforms, shrine mergers, and urbanization led to the loss of original shrine sites, with some locations now unknown.
This article covers the Ōji shrines with identified sites in Minabe Town, Wakayama Prefecture.
== Ninety-Nine Ōji in Minabe Town == There are three Ōji shrines within Minabe Town:
=== Iwashiro Ōji === [[File:岩代王子２.jpg|thumb|Iwashiro Ōji]] Located near the mouth of the Nishi-Iwashiro River facing the coast, the '''Iwashiro Ōji''' (岩代王子) shrine site is identified. Some historical sources such as the ''{{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja|和歌山県聖蹟|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' place it here, while others like the ''{{ill|Kii Fudoki Continued|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Fudoki Continued|qq=}}'' identify a nearby Tenjin Shrine as the original site. There is a monument nearby related to {{ill|Prince Arima|ja|有間皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Arima|qq=}}. It is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 1558 Nishi-Iwashiro, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Senri Ōji === [[File:Senri-Oji01s2040.jpg|thumb|220px|Senri Ōji]] The '''Senri Ōji''' (千里王子) is located along the coast of {{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|qq=}}. It has a monument commemorating [[:en:Emperor Kazan|Emperor Kazan]]’s poetry and is adjacent to Senri Kannon Temple. It was merged into {{ill|Suga Shrine (Minabe|ja|須賀神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine (Minabe|qq=}} in 1909 but remains well-preserved. The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Minabe Ōji === [[File:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|Minabe Ōji]] The '''Minabe Ōji''' (三鍋王子) is located near [[:en:Minabe Station|Minabe Station]]. Historical records mention it as a site for religious offerings during imperial pilgrimages. It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe|ja|鹿島神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Minabe|qq=}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
== Surrounding Information == * '''{{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|qq=}}''': A significant ritual site where pilgrims performed [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]] purification rites. * '''Senri Kannon''': A Buddhist hall originally associated with Senri Ōji, now a separate temple after the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} during the Meiji era. * '''Ancient Pilgrimage Path (Senri Ōji to Minabe Ōji)''': A well-preserved ancient road, recently designated as a cultural property in 2022. * '''Nanbu Pass and Jizō Statue''': The pass was historically significant and features a [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]] statue believed to protect against fractures.
<gallery> File:千里海岸 02.jpg|Senri no Hama File:Kiiji - Senri Ōji.jpg|Senri Ōji with seashell offerings File:千里観音.jpg|Senri Kannon File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 01.jpg|Stone Buddhas along the ancient path File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 03.jpg|Pilgrimage path through private plum orchards File:南部峠.jpg|Old Nanbu Pass File:Minabe-touge01s2048.jpg|Jizō statue at Nanbu Pass </gallery>
== Further Reading == * Hasegawa, Yasutaka (2007). *Kumano Ōji Pilgrimage Guidebook*. Shinpu Shobo. ISBN 9784882696292 * Nishi, Ritsu (1987). *Kumano Kodo Guide: Current Survey of the Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines*. Arao Seibundō. * Heibonsha (1983). *Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture*. Heibonsha.
== See Also == {{Commonscat|Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji}} * {{ill|Kumano shrine|ja|熊野信仰|lt=Kumano faith|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji}}

* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
== Surrounding Information == * '''{{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|qq=}}''': A significant ritual site where pilgrims performed [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]] purification rites. * '''Senri Kannon''': A Buddhist hall originally associated with Senri Ōji, now a separate temple after the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} during the Meiji era. * '''Ancient Pilgrimage Path (Senri Ōji to Minabe Ōji)''': A well-preserved ancient road, recently designated as a cultural property in 2022. * '''Nanbu Pass and Jizō Statue''': The pass was historically significant and features a [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]] statue believed to protect against fractures.
<gallery> File:千里海岸 02.jpg|Senri no Hama File:Kiiji - Senri Ōji.jpg|Senri Ōji with seashell offerings File:千里観音.jpg|Senri Kannon File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 01.jpg|Stone Buddhas along the ancient path File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 03.jpg|Pilgrimage path through private plum orchards File:南部峠.jpg|Old Nanbu Pass File:Minabe-touge01s2048.jpg|Jizō statue at Nanbu Pass </gallery>
== Further Reading == * Hasegawa, Yasutaka (2007). *Kumano Ōji Pilgrimage Guidebook*. Shinpu Shobo. ISBN 9784882696292 * Nishi, Ritsu (1987). *Kumano Kodo Guide: Current Survey of the Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines*. Arao Seibundō. * Heibonsha (1983). *Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture*. Heibonsha.

revision.text:
This is a list of some of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the ōji Shrines in the region of [[:en:Minabe, Wakayama|Minabe Town]]
==Overview== This article discusses the '''{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}''' (九十九王子) located within [[:en:Minabe, Wakayama|Minabe, Wakayama]], [[:en:Hidaka District, Wakayama|Hidaka District, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]].
== What Are the Ninety-Nine Ōji? == ''For more details, see {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}.''
The Ninety-Nine Ōji (九十九王子) refers to a group of [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrines]] located along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]], particularly on the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahechi|qq=}} routes. These shrines were rapidly organized primarily during the 12th to 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shugendō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who served as guides for imperial family members and nobles on pilgrimages to Kumano, with prayers for the protection of pilgrims.
However, after the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū War]] in 1221, pilgrimages from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}} declined, leading to the deterioration and abandonment of the shrines along the Kiiji route. This trend accelerated after the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], as Kumano pilgrimages lost their prominence. Despite some restoration efforts during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] by the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], the shrines did not regain their former status. Further changes during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] due to the [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]] reforms, shrine mergers, and urbanization led to the loss of original shrine sites, with some locations now unknown.
This article covers the Ōji shrines with identified sites in Minabe Town, Wakayama Prefecture.
== Ninety-Nine Ōji in Minabe Town == There are three Ōji shrines within Minabe Town:
=== Iwashiro Ōji === [[File:岩代王子２.jpg|thumb|Iwashiro Ōji]] Located near the mouth of the Nishi-Iwashiro River facing the coast, the '''Iwashiro Ōji''' (岩代王子) shrine site is identified. Some historical sources such as the ''{{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja|和歌山県聖蹟|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' place it here, while others like the ''{{ill|Kii Fudoki Continued|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Fudoki Continued|qq=}}'' identify a nearby Tenjin Shrine as the original site. There is a monument nearby related to {{ill|Prince Arima|ja|有間皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Arima|qq=}}. It is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 1558 Nishi-Iwashiro, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Senri Ōji === [[File:Senri-Oji01s2040.jpg|thumb|220px|Senri Ōji]] The '''Senri Ōji''' (千里王子) is located along the coast of {{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|qq=}}. It has a monument commemorating [[:en:Emperor Kazan|Emperor Kazan]]’s poetry and is adjacent to Senri Kannon Temple. It was merged into {{ill|Suga Shrine (Minabe|ja|須賀神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine (Minabe|qq=}} in 1909 but remains well-preserved. The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Minabe Ōji === [[File:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|Minabe Ōji]] The '''Minabe Ōji''' (三鍋王子) is located near [[:en:Minabe Station|Minabe Station]]. Historical records mention it as a site for religious offerings during imperial pilgrimages. It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe|ja|鹿島神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Minabe|qq=}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
== Surrounding Information == * '''{{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|qq=}}''': A significant ritual site where pilgrims performed [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]] purification rites. * '''Senri Kannon''': A Buddhist hall originally associated with Senri Ōji, now a separate temple after the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} during the Meiji era. * '''Ancient Pilgrimage Path (Senri Ōji to Minabe Ōji)''': A well-preserved ancient road, recently designated as a cultural property in 2022. * '''Nanbu Pass and Jizō Statue''': The pass was historically significant and features a [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]] statue believed to protect against fractures.
<gallery> File:千里海岸 02.jpg|Senri no Hama File:Kiiji - Senri Ōji.jpg|Senri Ōji with seashell offerings File:千里観音.jpg|Senri Kannon File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 01.jpg|Stone Buddhas along the ancient path File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 03.jpg|Pilgrimage path through private plum orchards File:南部峠.jpg|Old Nanbu Pass File:Minabe-touge01s2048.jpg|Jizō statue at Nanbu Pass </gallery>
== Further Reading == * Hasegawa, Yasutaka (2007). *Kumano Ōji Pilgrimage Guidebook*. Shinpu Shobo. ISBN 9784882696292 * Nishi, Ritsu (1987). *Kumano Kodo Guide: Current Survey of the Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines*. Arao Seibundō. * Heibonsha (1983). *Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture*. Heibonsha.
== See Also == {{Commonscat|Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji}} * {{ill|Kumano shrine|ja|熊野信仰|lt=Kumano faith|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji}}

== External Links == * [http://www.aikis.or.jp/~minabe/kumano.html Kumano Kodo] - Minabe Town Tourism Association * [http://www.town.minabe.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkzai.html Minabe Town Cultural Properties]

The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District

Historical records mention it as a site for religious offerings during imperial pilgrimages. It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe|ja|鹿島神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Minabe|qq=}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District

revision.text:
This is a list of some of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the ōji Shrines in the region of [[:en:Minabe, Wakayama|Minabe Town]]
==Overview== This article discusses the '''{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}''' (九十九王子) located within [[:en:Minabe, Wakayama|Minabe, Wakayama]], [[:en:Hidaka District, Wakayama|Hidaka District, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]].
== What Are the Ninety-Nine Ōji? == ''For more details, see {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}.''
The Ninety-Nine Ōji (九十九王子) refers to a group of [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrines]] located along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]], particularly on the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahechi|qq=}} routes. These shrines were rapidly organized primarily during the 12th to 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shugendō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who served as guides for imperial family members and nobles on pilgrimages to Kumano, with prayers for the protection of pilgrims.
However, after the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū War]] in 1221, pilgrimages from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}} declined, leading to the deterioration and abandonment of the shrines along the Kiiji route. This trend accelerated after the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], as Kumano pilgrimages lost their prominence. Despite some restoration efforts during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] by the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], the shrines did not regain their former status. Further changes during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] due to the [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]] reforms, shrine mergers, and urbanization led to the loss of original shrine sites, with some locations now unknown.
This article covers the Ōji shrines with identified sites in Minabe Town, Wakayama Prefecture.
== Ninety-Nine Ōji in Minabe Town == There are three Ōji shrines within Minabe Town:
=== Iwashiro Ōji === [[File:岩代王子２.jpg|thumb|Iwashiro Ōji]] Located near the mouth of the Nishi-Iwashiro River facing the coast, the '''Iwashiro Ōji''' (岩代王子) shrine site is identified. Some historical sources such as the ''{{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja|和歌山県聖蹟|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' place it here, while others like the ''{{ill|Kii Fudoki Continued|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Fudoki Continued|qq=}}'' identify a nearby Tenjin Shrine as the original site. There is a monument nearby related to {{ill|Prince Arima|ja|有間皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Arima|qq=}}. It is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 1558 Nishi-Iwashiro, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Senri Ōji === [[File:Senri-Oji01s2040.jpg|thumb|220px|Senri Ōji]] The '''Senri Ōji''' (千里王子) is located along the coast of {{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|qq=}}. It has a monument commemorating [[:en:Emperor Kazan|Emperor Kazan]]’s poetry and is adjacent to Senri Kannon Temple. It was merged into {{ill|Suga Shrine (Minabe|ja|須賀神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine (Minabe|qq=}} in 1909 but remains well-preserved. The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Minabe Ōji === [[File:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|Minabe Ōji]] The '''Minabe Ōji''' (三鍋王子) is located near [[:en:Minabe Station|Minabe Station]]. Historical records mention it as a site for religious offerings during imperial pilgrimages. It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe|ja|鹿島神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Minabe|qq=}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
== Surrounding Information == * '''{{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|qq=}}''': A significant ritual site where pilgrims performed [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]] purification rites. * '''Senri Kannon''': A Buddhist hall originally associated with Senri Ōji, now a separate temple after the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} during the Meiji era. * '''Ancient Pilgrimage Path (Senri Ōji to Minabe Ōji)''': A well-preserved ancient road, recently designated as a cultural property in 2022. * '''Nanbu Pass and Jizō Statue''': The pass was historically significant and features a [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]] statue believed to protect against fractures.
<gallery> File:千里海岸 02.jpg|Senri no Hama File:Kiiji - Senri Ōji.jpg|Senri Ōji with seashell offerings File:千里観音.jpg|Senri Kannon File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 01.jpg|Stone Buddhas along the ancient path File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 03.jpg|Pilgrimage path through private plum orchards File:南部峠.jpg|Old Nanbu Pass File:Minabe-touge01s2048.jpg|Jizō statue at Nanbu Pass </gallery>
== Further Reading == * Hasegawa, Yasutaka (2007). *Kumano Ōji Pilgrimage Guidebook*. Shinpu Shobo. ISBN 9784882696292 * Nishi, Ritsu (1987). *Kumano Kodo Guide: Current Survey of the Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines*. Arao Seibundō. * Heibonsha (1983). *Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture*. Heibonsha.
== See Also == {{Commonscat|Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji}} * {{ill|Kumano shrine|ja|熊野信仰|lt=Kumano faith|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji}}
== References == {{reflist|2}}
== External Links == * [http://www.aikis.or.jp/~minabe/kumano.html Kumano Kodo] - Minabe Town Tourism Association * [http://www.town.minabe.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkzai.html Minabe Town Cultural Properties]

Further changes during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] due to the [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]] reforms, shrine mergers, and urbanization led to the loss of original shrine sites, with some locations now unknown.

It has a monument commemorating [[:en:Emperor Kazan|Emperor Kazan]]’s poetry and is adjacent to Senri Kannon Temple. It was merged into {{ill|Suga Shrine (Minabe|ja|須賀神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine (Minabe|qq=}} in 1909 but remains well-preserved. The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Minabe Ōji === [[File:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|Minabe Ōji]] The '''Minabe Ōji''' (三鍋王子) is located near [[:en:Minabe Station|Minabe Station]]. Historical records mention it as a site for religious offerings during imperial pilgrimages. It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe|ja|鹿島神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Minabe|qq=}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District

The Ninety-Nine Ōji (九十九王子) refers to a group of [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrines]] located along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]], particularly on the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahechi|qq=}} routes. These shrines were rapidly organized primarily during the 12th to 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shugendō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who served as guides for imperial family members and nobles on pilgrimages to Kumano, with prayers for the protection of pilgrims.
However, after the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū War]] in 1221, pilgrimages from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}} declined, leading to the deterioration and abandonment of the shrines along the Kiiji route. This trend accelerated after the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], as Kumano pilgrimages lost their prominence. Despite some restoration efforts during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] by the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], the shrines did not regain their former status. Further changes during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] due to the [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]] reforms, shrine mergers, and urbanization led to the loss of original shrine sites, with some locations now unknown.
This article covers the Ōji shrines with identified sites in Minabe Town, Wakayama Prefecture.
== Ninety-Nine Ōji in Minabe Town == There are three Ōji shrines within Minabe Town:
=== Iwashiro Ōji === [[File:岩代王子２.jpg|thumb|Iwashiro Ōji]] Located near the mouth of the Nishi-Iwashiro River facing the coast, the '''Iwashiro Ōji''' (岩代王子) shrine site is identified. Some historical sources such as the ''{{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja|和歌山県聖蹟|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' place it here, while others like the ''{{ill|Kii Fudoki Continued|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Fudoki Continued|qq=}}'' identify a nearby Tenjin Shrine as the original site. There is a monument nearby related to {{ill|Prince Arima|ja|有間皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Arima|qq=}}. It is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 1558 Nishi-Iwashiro, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Senri Ōji === [[File:Senri-Oji01s2040.jpg|thumb|220px|Senri Ōji]] The '''Senri Ōji''' (千里王子) is located along the coast of {{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|qq=}}. It has a monument commemorating [[:en:Emperor Kazan|Emperor Kazan]]’s poetry and is adjacent to Senri Kannon Temple. It was merged into {{ill|Suga Shrine (Minabe|ja|須賀神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine (Minabe|qq=}} in 1909 but remains well-preserved. The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Minabe Ōji === [[File:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|Minabe Ōji]] The '''Minabe Ōji''' (三鍋王子) is located near [[:en:Minabe Station|Minabe Station]]. Historical records mention it as a site for religious offerings during imperial pilgrimages. It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe|ja|鹿島神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Minabe|qq=}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
== Surrounding Information == * '''{{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|qq=}}''': A significant ritual site where pilgrims performed [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]] purification rites. * '''Senri Kannon''': A Buddhist hall originally associated with Senri Ōji, now a separate temple after the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} during the Meiji era. * '''Ancient Pilgrimage Path (Senri Ōji to Minabe Ōji)''': A well-preserved ancient road, recently designated as a cultural property in 2022. * '''Nanbu Pass and Jizō Statue''': The pass was historically significant and features a [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]] statue believed to protect against fractures.
<gallery> File:千里海岸 02.jpg|Senri no Hama File:Kiiji - Senri Ōji.jpg|Senri Ōji with seashell offerings File:千里観音.jpg|Senri Kannon File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 01.jpg|Stone Buddhas along the ancient path File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 03.jpg|Pilgrimage path through private plum orchards File:南部峠.jpg|Old Nanbu Pass File:Minabe-touge01s2048.jpg|Jizō statue at Nanbu Pass </gallery>
== Further Reading == * Hasegawa, Yasutaka (2007). *Kumano Ōji Pilgrimage Guidebook*. Shinpu Shobo. ISBN 9784882696292 * Nishi, Ritsu (1987). *Kumano Kodo Guide: Current Survey of the Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines*. Arao Seibundō. * Heibonsha (1983). *Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture*. Heibonsha.
== See Also == {{Commonscat|Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji}} * {{ill|Kumano shrine|ja|熊野信仰|lt=Kumano faith|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji}}

However, after the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū War]] in 1221, pilgrimages from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}} declined, leading to the deterioration and abandonment of the shrines along the Kiiji route. This trend accelerated after the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], as Kumano pilgrimages lost their prominence. Despite some restoration efforts during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] by the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], the shrines did not regain their former status. Further changes during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] due to the [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]] reforms, shrine mergers, and urbanization led to the loss of original shrine sites, with some locations now unknown.

The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Minabe Ōji === [[File:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|Minabe Ōji]] The '''Minabe Ōji''' (三鍋王子) is located near [[:en:Minabe Station|Minabe Station]]. It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe|ja|鹿島神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Minabe|qq=}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.

The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Minabe Ōji === [[File:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|Minabe Ōji]] The '''Minabe Ōji''' (三鍋王子) is located near [[:en:Minabe Station|Minabe Station]]. Historical records mention it as a site for religious offerings during imperial pilgrimages. It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe|ja|鹿島神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Minabe|qq=}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
== Surrounding Information == * '''{{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|qq=}}''': A significant ritual site where pilgrims performed [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]] purification rites. * '''Senri Kannon''': A Buddhist hall originally associated with Senri Ōji, now a separate temple after the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} during the Meiji era. * '''Ancient Pilgrimage Path (Senri Ōji to Minabe Ōji)''': A well-preserved ancient road, recently designated as a cultural property in 2022. * '''Nanbu Pass and Jizō Statue''': The pass was historically significant and features a [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]] statue believed to protect against fractures.
