This article covers the ōji Shrines in the region of [[:en:Minabe, Wakayama|Minabe Town]]
==Overview== This article discusses the '''{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}''' (九十九王子) located within [[:en:Minabe, Wakayama|Minabe, Wakayama]], [[:en:Hidaka District, Wakayama|Hidaka District, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]].
== What Are the Ninety-Nine Ōji? == ''For more details, see {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}.''
The Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (九十九王子) refers to a group of [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrines]] located along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]], particularly on the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahechi|1=Nakahechi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30074932}} routes. These shrines were rapidly organized primarily during the 12th to 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shugendō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493040}}, who served as guides for imperial family members and nobles on pilgrimages to Kumano, with prayers for the protection of pilgrims.
However, after the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū War]] in 1221, pilgrimages from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}} declined, leading to the deterioration and abandonment of the shrines along the Kiiji route. This trend accelerated after the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], as Kumano pilgrimages lost their prominence. Despite some restoration efforts during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] by the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], the shrines did not regain their former status. Further changes during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] due to the [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]] reforms, shrine mergers, and urbanization led to the loss of original shrine sites, with some locations now unknown.
This article covers the Ōji shrines with identified sites in Minabe Town, Wakayama Prefecture.
== Ninety-Nine Ōji in Minabe Town == There are three Ōji shrines within Minabe Town:
=== Iwashiro Ōji === [[File:岩代王子２.jpg|thumb|Iwashiro Ōji]] Located near the mouth of the Nishi-Iwashiro River facing the coast, the '''Iwashiro Ōji''' (岩代王子) shrine site is identified. Some historical sources such as the ''{{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja|和歌山県聖蹟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' place it here, while others like the ''{{ill|Kii Fudoki Continued|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Fudoki Continued|1=Kiizoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}'' identify a nearby Tenjin Shrine as the original site. There is a monument nearby related to {{ill|Prince Arima|ja|有間皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Arima|1=Arima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516721}}. It is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 1558 Nishi-Iwashiro, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Senri Ōji === [[File:Senri-Oji01s2040.jpg|thumb|220px|Senri Ōji]] The '''Senri Ōji''' (千里王子) is located along the coast of {{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|1=Senri Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406837}}. It has a monument commemorating [[:en:Emperor Kazan|Emperor Kazan]]’s poetry and is adjacent to Senri Kannon Temple. It was merged into {{ill|Suga Shrine (Minabe|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine (Minabe|1=Suga Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11253400}} in 1909 but remains well-preserved. The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Minabe Ōji === [[File:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|Minabe Ōji]] The '''Minabe Ōji''' (三鍋王子) is located near [[:en:Minabe Station|Minabe Station]]. Historical records mention it as a site for religious offerings during imperial pilgrimages. It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe|ja|鹿島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Minabe|1=Kashima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677154}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
== Surrounding Information == * '''{{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|1=Senri Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406837}}''': A significant ritual site where pilgrims performed [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]] purification rites. * '''Senri Kannon''': A Buddhist hall originally associated with Senri Ōji, now a separate temple after the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}} during the Meiji era. * '''Ancient Pilgrimage Path (Senri Ōji to Minabe Ōji)''': A well-preserved ancient road, recently designated as a cultural property in 2022. * '''Nanbu Pass and Jizō Statue''': The pass was historically significant and features a [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]] statue believed to protect against fractures.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:九十九王子 (みなべ町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:九十九王子 (みなべ町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:九十九王子 (みなべ町)]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Minabe|WD=Q1345966}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11370040]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} This is a list of some of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the ōji Shrines in the region of [[:en:Minabe, Wakayama|Minabe Town]]
==Overview== This article discusses the '''{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}''' (九十九王子) located within [[:en:Minabe, Wakayama|Minabe, Wakayama]], [[:en:Hidaka District, Wakayama|Hidaka District, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]].
== What Are the Ninety-Nine Ōji? == ''For more details, see {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}.''
The Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (九十九王子) refers to a group of [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrines]] located along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]], particularly on the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahechi|1=Nakahechi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30074932}} routes. These shrines were rapidly organized primarily during the 12th to 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shugendō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493040}}, who served as guides for imperial family members and nobles on pilgrimages to Kumano, with prayers for the protection of pilgrims.
However, after the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū War]] in 1221, pilgrimages from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}} declined, leading to the deterioration and abandonment of the shrines along the Kiiji route. This trend accelerated after the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], as Kumano pilgrimages lost their prominence. Despite some restoration efforts during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] by the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], the shrines did not regain their former status. Further changes during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] due to the [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]] reforms, shrine mergers, and urbanization led to the loss of original shrine sites, with some locations now unknown.
This article covers the Ōji shrines with identified sites in Minabe Town, Wakayama Prefecture.
== Ninety-Nine Ōji in Minabe Town == There are three Ōji shrines within Minabe Town:
=== Iwashiro Ōji === [[File:岩代王子２.jpg|thumb|Iwashiro Ōji]] Located near the mouth of the Nishi-Iwashiro River facing the coast, the '''Iwashiro Ōji''' (岩代王子) shrine site is identified. Some historical sources such as the ''{{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja|和歌山県聖蹟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' place it here, while others like the ''{{ill|Kii Fudoki Continued|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Fudoki Continued|1=Kiizoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}'' identify a nearby Tenjin Shrine as the original site. There is a monument nearby related to {{ill|Prince Arima|ja|有間皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Arima|1=Arima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516721}}. It is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 1558 Nishi-Iwashiro, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Senri Ōji === [[File:Senri-Oji01s2040.jpg|thumb|220px|Senri Ōji]] The '''Senri Ōji''' (千里王子) is located along the coast of {{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|1=Senri Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406837}}. It has a monument commemorating [[:en:Emperor Kazan|Emperor Kazan]]’s poetry and is adjacent to Senri Kannon Temple. It was merged into {{ill|Suga Shrine (Minabe|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine (Minabe|1=Suga Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11253400}} in 1909 but remains well-preserved. The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Minabe Ōji === [[File:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|Minabe Ōji]] The '''Minabe Ōji''' (三鍋王子) is located near [[:en:Minabe Station|Minabe Station]]. Historical records mention it as a site for religious offerings during imperial pilgrimages. It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe|ja|鹿島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Minabe|1=Kashima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677154}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
== Surrounding Information == * '''{{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|1=Senri Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406837}}''': A significant ritual site where pilgrims performed [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]] purification rites. * '''Senri Kannon''': A Buddhist hall originally associated with Senri Ōji, now a separate temple after the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}} during the Meiji era. * '''Ancient Pilgrimage Path (Senri Ōji to Minabe Ōji)''': A well-preserved ancient road, recently designated as a cultural property in 2022. * '''Nanbu Pass and Jizō Statue''': The pass was historically significant and features a [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]] statue believed to protect against fractures.
<gallery> File:千里海岸 02.jpg|Senri no Hama File:Kiiji - Senri Ōji.jpg|Senri Ōji with seashell offerings File:千里観音.jpg|Senri Kannon File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 01.jpg|Stone Buddhas along the ancient path File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 03.jpg|Pilgrimage path through private plum orchards File:南部峠.jpg|Old Nanbu Pass File:Minabe-touge01s2048.jpg|Jizō statue at Nanbu Pass </gallery>
== Further Reading == * Hasegawa, Yasutaka (2007). *Kumano Ōji Pilgrimage Guidebook*. Shinpu Shobo. ISBN 9784882696292 * Nishi, Ritsu (1987). *Kumano Kodo Guide: Current Survey of the Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines*. Arao Seibundō. * Heibonsha (1983). *Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture*. Heibonsha.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:九十九王子 (みなべ町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:九十九王子 (みなべ町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:九十九王子 (みなべ町)]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Minabe|WD=Q1345966}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11370040]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} This is a list of some of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the ōji Shrines in the region of [[:en:Minabe, Wakayama|Minabe Town]]
==Overview== This article discusses the '''{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}''' (九十九王子) located within [[:en:Minabe, Wakayama|Minabe, Wakayama]], [[:en:Hidaka District, Wakayama|Hidaka District, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]].
== What Are the Ninety-Nine Ōji? == ''For more details, see {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}.''
The Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (九十九王子) refers to a group of [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrines]] located along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]], particularly on the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahechi|1=Nakahechi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30074932}} routes. These shrines were rapidly organized primarily during the 12th to 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shugendō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493040}}, who served as guides for imperial family members and nobles on pilgrimages to Kumano, with prayers for the protection of pilgrims.
However, after the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū War]] in 1221, pilgrimages from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}} declined, leading to the deterioration and abandonment of the shrines along the Kiiji route. This trend accelerated after the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], as Kumano pilgrimages lost their prominence. Despite some restoration efforts during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] by the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], the shrines did not regain their former status. Further changes during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] due to the [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]] reforms, shrine mergers, and urbanization led to the loss of original shrine sites, with some locations now unknown.
This article covers the Ōji shrines with identified sites in Minabe Town, Wakayama Prefecture.
== Ninety-Nine Ōji in Minabe Town == There are three Ōji shrines within Minabe Town:
=== Iwashiro Ōji === [[File:岩代王子２.jpg|thumb|Iwashiro Ōji]] Located near the mouth of the Nishi-Iwashiro River facing the coast, the '''Iwashiro Ōji''' (岩代王子) shrine site is identified. Some historical sources such as the ''{{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja|和歌山県聖蹟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' place it here, while others like the ''{{ill|Kii Fudoki Continued|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Fudoki Continued|1=Kiizoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}'' identify a nearby Tenjin Shrine as the original site. There is a monument nearby related to {{ill|Prince Arima|ja|有間皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Arima|1=Arima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516721}}. It is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 1558 Nishi-Iwashiro, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Senri Ōji === [[File:Senri-Oji01s2040.jpg|thumb|220px|Senri Ōji]] The '''Senri Ōji''' (千里王子) is located along the coast of {{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|1=Senri Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406837}}. It has a monument commemorating [[:en:Emperor Kazan|Emperor Kazan]]’s poetry and is adjacent to Senri Kannon Temple. It was merged into {{ill|Suga Shrine (Minabe|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine (Minabe|1=Suga Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11253400}} in 1909 but remains well-preserved. The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Minabe Ōji === [[File:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|Minabe Ōji]] The '''Minabe Ōji''' (三鍋王子) is located near [[:en:Minabe Station|Minabe Station]]. Historical records mention it as a site for religious offerings during imperial pilgrimages. It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe|ja|鹿島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Minabe|1=Kashima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677154}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
== Surrounding Information == * '''{{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|1=Senri Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406837}}''': A significant ritual site where pilgrims performed [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]] purification rites. * '''Senri Kannon''': A Buddhist hall originally associated with Senri Ōji, now a separate temple after the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}} during the Meiji era. * '''Ancient Pilgrimage Path (Senri Ōji to Minabe Ōji)''': A well-preserved ancient road, recently designated as a cultural property in 2022. * '''Nanbu Pass and Jizō Statue''': The pass was historically significant and features a [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]] statue believed to protect against fractures.
<gallery> File:千里海岸 02.jpg|Senri no Hama File:Kiiji - Senri Ōji.jpg|Senri Ōji with seashell offerings File:千里観音.jpg|Senri Kannon File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 01.jpg|Stone Buddhas along the ancient path File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 03.jpg|Pilgrimage path through private plum orchards File:南部峠.jpg|Old Nanbu Pass File:Minabe-touge01s2048.jpg|Jizō statue at Nanbu Pass </gallery>
== Further Reading == * Hasegawa, Yasutaka (2007). *Kumano Ōji Pilgrimage Guidebook*. Shinpu Shobo. ISBN 9784882696292 * Nishi, Ritsu (1987). *Kumano Kodo Guide: Current Survey of the Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines*. Arao Seibundō. * Heibonsha (1983). *Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture*. Heibonsha.
== See Also == {{Commonscat|Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji}} * {{ill|Kumano shrine|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji}}
== References == {{reflist|2}}
== External Links == * [http://www.aikis.or.jp/~minabe/kumano.html Kumano Kodo] - Minabe Town Tourism Association * [http://www.town.minabe.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkzai.html Minabe Town Cultural Properties]

Despite some restoration efforts during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] by the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], the shrines did not regain their former status. Further changes during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] due to the [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]] reforms, shrine mergers, and urbanization led to the loss of original shrine sites, with some locations now unknown.
This article covers the Ōji shrines with identified sites in Minabe Town, Wakayama Prefecture.
== Ninety-Nine Ōji in Minabe Town == There are three Ōji shrines within Minabe Town:
=== Iwashiro Ōji === [[File:岩代王子２.jpg|thumb|Iwashiro Ōji]] Located near the mouth of the Nishi-Iwashiro River facing the coast, the '''Iwashiro Ōji''' (岩代王子) shrine site is identified. Some historical sources such as the ''{{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja|和歌山県聖蹟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' place it here, while others like the ''{{ill|Kii Fudoki Continued|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Fudoki Continued|1=Kiizoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}'' identify a nearby Tenjin Shrine as the original site. There is a monument nearby related to {{ill|Prince Arima|ja|有間皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Arima|1=Arima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516721}}. It is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 1558 Nishi-Iwashiro, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Senri Ōji === [[File:Senri-Oji01s2040.jpg|thumb|220px|Senri Ōji]] The '''Senri Ōji''' (千里王子) is located along the coast of {{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|1=Senri Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406837}}. It has a monument commemorating [[:en:Emperor Kazan|Emperor Kazan]]’s poetry and is adjacent to Senri Kannon Temple. It was merged into {{ill|Suga Shrine (Minabe|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine (Minabe|1=Suga Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11253400}} in 1909 but remains well-preserved. The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Minabe Ōji === [[File:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|Minabe Ōji]] The '''Minabe Ōji''' (三鍋王子) is located near [[:en:Minabe Station|Minabe Station]]. Historical records mention it as a site for religious offerings during imperial pilgrimages. It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe|ja|鹿島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Minabe|1=Kashima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677154}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
== Surrounding Information == * '''{{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|1=Senri Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406837}}''': A significant ritual site where pilgrims performed [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]] purification rites. * '''Senri Kannon''': A Buddhist hall originally associated with Senri Ōji, now a separate temple after the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}} during the Meiji era. * '''Ancient Pilgrimage Path (Senri Ōji to Minabe Ōji)''': A well-preserved ancient road, recently designated as a cultural property in 2022. * '''Nanbu Pass and Jizō Statue''': The pass was historically significant and features a [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]] statue believed to protect against fractures.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:九十九王子 (みなべ町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:九十九王子 (みなべ町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:九十九王子 (みなべ町)]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Minabe|WD=Q1345966}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11370040]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} This is a list of some of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the ōji Shrines in the region of {{ill|Minabe, Wakayama|en|Minabe, Wakayama|WD=Q1345966|lt=Minabe Town}}
==Overview== This article discusses the '''{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}''' (九十九王子) located within {{ill|Minabe, Wakayama|en|Minabe, Wakayama|WD=Q1345966|lt=Minabe, Wakayama}}, {{ill|Hidaka District, Wakayama|en|Hidaka District, Wakayama|WD=Q1131164|lt=Hidaka District, Wakayama}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture}}.
== What Are the Ninety-Nine Ōji? == ''For more details, see {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}.''
The Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (九十九王子) refers to a group of {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrines}} located along the {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}}, particularly on the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahechi|1=Nakahechi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30074932}} routes. These shrines were rapidly organized primarily during the 12th to 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shugendō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493040}}, who served as guides for imperial family members and nobles on pilgrimages to Kumano, with prayers for the protection of pilgrims.
However, after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|en|Jōkyū War|WD=Q1372308|lt=Jōkyū War}} in 1221, pilgrimages from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}} declined, leading to the deterioration and abandonment of the shrines along the Kiiji route. This trend accelerated after the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}}, as Kumano pilgrimages lost their prominence. Despite some restoration efforts during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|en|Kishū Domain|WD=Q1069187|lt=Kishū Domain}}, the shrines did not regain their former status. Further changes during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}} due to the {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|WD=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto}} reforms, shrine mergers, and urbanization led to the loss of original shrine sites, with some locations now unknown.
This article covers the Ōji shrines with identified sites in Minabe Town, Wakayama Prefecture.
== Ninety-Nine Ōji in Minabe Town == There are three Ōji shrines within Minabe Town:
=== Iwashiro Ōji === [[File:岩代王子２.jpg|thumb|Iwashiro Ōji]] Located near the mouth of the Nishi-Iwashiro River facing the coast, the '''Iwashiro Ōji''' (岩代王子) shrine site is identified. Some historical sources such as the ''{{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja|和歌山県聖蹟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' place it here, while others like the ''{{ill|Kii Fudoki Continued|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Fudoki Continued|1=Kiizoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}'' identify a nearby Tenjin Shrine as the original site. There is a monument nearby related to {{ill|Prince Arima|ja|有間皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Arima|1=Arima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516721}}. It is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 1558 Nishi-Iwashiro, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Senri Ōji === [[File:Senri-Oji01s2040.jpg|thumb|220px|Senri Ōji]] The '''Senri Ōji''' (千里王子) is located along the coast of {{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|1=Senri Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406837}}. It has a monument commemorating {{ill|Emperor Kazan|en|Emperor Kazan|WD=Q361000|lt=Emperor Kazan}}’s poetry and is adjacent to Senri Kannon Temple. It was merged into {{ill|Suga Shrine (Minabe|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine (Minabe|1=Suga Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11253400}} in 1909 but remains well-preserved. The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Minabe Ōji === [[File:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|Minabe Ōji]] The '''Minabe Ōji''' (三鍋王子) is located near {{ill|Minabe Station|en|Minabe Station|WD=Q2897595|lt=Minabe Station}}. Historical records mention it as a site for religious offerings during imperial pilgrimages. It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe|ja|鹿島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Minabe|1=Kashima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677154}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
== Surrounding Information == * '''{{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|1=Senri Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406837}}''': A significant ritual site where pilgrims performed {{ill|Misogi|en|Misogi|WD=Q1936265|lt=Misogi}} purification rites. * '''Senri Kannon''': A Buddhist hall originally associated with Senri Ōji, now a separate temple after the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}} during the Meiji era. * '''Ancient Pilgrimage Path (Senri Ōji to Minabe Ōji)''': A well-preserved ancient road, recently designated as a cultural property in 2022. * '''Nanbu Pass and Jizō Statue''': The pass was historically significant and features a {{ill|Kṣitigarbha|en|Kṣitigarbha|WD=Q604687|lt=Jizō Bosatsu}} statue believed to protect against fractures.
<gallery> File:千里海岸 02.jpg|Senri no Hama File:Kiiji - Senri Ōji.jpg|Senri Ōji with seashell offerings File:千里観音.jpg|Senri Kannon File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 01.jpg|Stone Buddhas along the ancient path File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 03.jpg|Pilgrimage path through private plum orchards File:南部峠.jpg|Old Nanbu Pass File:Minabe-touge01s2048.jpg|Jizō statue at Nanbu Pass </gallery>
== Further Reading == * Hasegawa, Yasutaka (2007). *Kumano Ōji Pilgrimage Guidebook*. Shinpu Shobo. ISBN 9784882696292 * Nishi, Ritsu (1987). *Kumano Kodo Guide: Current Survey of the Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines*. Arao Seibundō. * Heibonsha (1983). *Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture*. Heibonsha.
== See Also == {{Commonscat|Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji}} * {{ill|Kumano shrine|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji}}
== References == {{reflist|2}}
== External Links == * [http://www.aikis.or.jp/~minabe/kumano.html Kumano Kodo] - Minabe Town Tourism Association * [http://www.town.minabe.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkzai.html Minabe Town Cultural Properties]

This article covers the ōji Shrines in the region of {{ill|Minabe, Wakayama|en|Minabe, Wakayama|WD=Q1345966|lt=Minabe Town}}

== What Are the Ninety-Nine Ōji? == ''For more details, see {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}.''

These shrines were rapidly organized primarily during the 12th to 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shugendō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493040}}, who served as guides for imperial family members and nobles on pilgrimages to Kumano, with prayers for the protection of pilgrims.
However, after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|en|Jōkyū War|WD=Q1372308|lt=Jōkyū War}} in 1221, pilgrimages from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}} declined, leading to the deterioration and abandonment of the shrines along the Kiiji route. This trend accelerated after the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}}, as Kumano pilgrimages lost their prominence. Despite some restoration efforts during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|en|Kishū Domain|WD=Q1069187|lt=Kishū Domain}}, the shrines did not regain their former status. Further changes during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}} due to the {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|WD=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto}} reforms, shrine mergers, and urbanization led to the loss of original shrine sites, with some locations now unknown.
This article covers the Ōji shrines with identified sites in Minabe Town, Wakayama Prefecture.
== Ninety-Nine Ōji in Minabe Town == There are three Ōji shrines within Minabe Town:
=== Iwashiro Ōji === [[File:岩代王子２.jpg|thumb|Iwashiro Ōji]] Located near the mouth of the Nishi-Iwashiro River facing the coast, the '''Iwashiro Ōji''' (岩代王子) shrine site is identified. Some historical sources such as the ''{{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja|和歌山県聖蹟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' place it here, while others like the ''{{ill|Kii Fudoki Continued|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Fudoki Continued|1=Kiizoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}'' identify a nearby Tenjin Shrine as the original site. There is a monument nearby related to {{ill|Prince Arima|ja|有間皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Arima|1=Arima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516721}}. It is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 1558 Nishi-Iwashiro, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Senri Ōji === [[File:Senri-Oji01s2040.jpg|thumb|220px|Senri Ōji]] The '''Senri Ōji''' (千里王子) is located along the coast of {{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|1=Senri Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406837}}. It has a monument commemorating {{ill|Emperor Kazan|en|Emperor Kazan|WD=Q361000|lt=Emperor Kazan}}’s poetry and is adjacent to Senri Kannon Temple. It was merged into {{ill|Suga Shrine (Minabe|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine (Minabe|1=Suga Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11253400}} in 1909 but remains well-preserved. The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Minabe Ōji === [[File:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|Minabe Ōji]] The '''Minabe Ōji''' (三鍋王子) is located near {{ill|Minabe Station|en|Minabe Station|WD=Q2897595|lt=Minabe Station}}. Historical records mention it as a site for religious offerings during imperial pilgrimages. It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe|ja|鹿島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Minabe|1=Kashima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677154}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
== Surrounding Information == * '''{{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|1=Senri Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406837}}''': A significant ritual site where pilgrims performed {{ill|Misogi|en|Misogi|WD=Q1936265|lt=Misogi}} purification rites. * '''Senri Kannon''': A Buddhist hall originally associated with Senri Ōji, now a separate temple after the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}} during the Meiji era. * '''Ancient Pilgrimage Path (Senri Ōji to Minabe Ōji)''': A well-preserved ancient road, recently designated as a cultural property in 2022. * '''Nanbu Pass and Jizō Statue''': The pass was historically significant and features a {{ill|Kṣitigarbha|en|Kṣitigarbha|WD=Q604687|lt=Jizō Bosatsu}} statue believed to protect against fractures.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:九十九王子 (みなべ町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:九十九王子 (みなべ町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:九十九王子 (みなべ町)]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Minabe|WD=Q1345966}}}} This is a list of some of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the ōji Shrines in the region of {{ill|Minabe, Wakayama|en|Minabe, Wakayama|WD=Q1345966|lt=Minabe Town}}
==Overview== This article discusses the '''{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}''' (九十九王子) located within {{ill|Minabe, Wakayama|en|Minabe, Wakayama|WD=Q1345966|lt=Minabe, Wakayama}}, {{ill|Hidaka District, Wakayama|en|Hidaka District, Wakayama|WD=Q1131164|lt=Hidaka District, Wakayama}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture}}.
== What Are the Ninety-Nine Ōji? == ''For more details, see {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}.''
The Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (九十九王子) refers to a group of {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrines}} located along the {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}}, particularly on the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahechi|1=Nakahechi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30074932}} routes. These shrines were rapidly organized primarily during the 12th to 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shugendō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493040}}, who served as guides for imperial family members and nobles on pilgrimages to Kumano, with prayers for the protection of pilgrims.
However, after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|en|Jōkyū War|WD=Q1372308|lt=Jōkyū War}} in 1221, pilgrimages from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}} declined, leading to the deterioration and abandonment of the shrines along the Kiiji route. This trend accelerated after the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}}, as Kumano pilgrimages lost their prominence. Despite some restoration efforts during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|en|Kishū Domain|WD=Q1069187|lt=Kishū Domain}}, the shrines did not regain their former status. Further changes during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}} due to the {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|WD=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto}} reforms, shrine mergers, and urbanization led to the loss of original shrine sites, with some locations now unknown.
This article covers the Ōji shrines with identified sites in Minabe Town, Wakayama Prefecture.
== Ninety-Nine Ōji in Minabe Town == There are three Ōji shrines within Minabe Town:
=== Iwashiro Ōji === [[File:岩代王子２.jpg|thumb|Iwashiro Ōji]] Located near the mouth of the Nishi-Iwashiro River facing the coast, the '''Iwashiro Ōji''' (岩代王子) shrine site is identified. Some historical sources such as the ''{{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja|和歌山県聖蹟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' place it here, while others like the ''{{ill|Kii Fudoki Continued|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Fudoki Continued|1=Kiizoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}'' identify a nearby Tenjin Shrine as the original site. There is a monument nearby related to {{ill|Prince Arima|ja|有間皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Arima|1=Arima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516721}}. It is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 1558 Nishi-Iwashiro, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Senri Ōji === [[File:Senri-Oji01s2040.jpg|thumb|220px|Senri Ōji]] The '''Senri Ōji''' (千里王子) is located along the coast of {{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|1=Senri Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406837}}. It has a monument commemorating {{ill|Emperor Kazan|en|Emperor Kazan|WD=Q361000|lt=Emperor Kazan}}’s poetry and is adjacent to Senri Kannon Temple. It was merged into {{ill|Suga Shrine (Minabe|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine (Minabe|1=Suga Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11253400}} in 1909 but remains well-preserved. The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Minabe Ōji === [[File:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|Minabe Ōji]] The '''Minabe Ōji''' (三鍋王子) is located near {{ill|Minabe Station|en|Minabe Station|WD=Q2897595|lt=Minabe Station}}. Historical records mention it as a site for religious offerings during imperial pilgrimages. It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe|ja|鹿島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Minabe|1=Kashima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677154}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
== Surrounding Information == * '''{{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|1=Senri Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406837}}''': A significant ritual site where pilgrims performed {{ill|Misogi|en|Misogi|WD=Q1936265|lt=Misogi}} purification rites. * '''Senri Kannon''': A Buddhist hall originally associated with Senri Ōji, now a separate temple after the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}} during the Meiji era. * '''Ancient Pilgrimage Path (Senri Ōji to Minabe Ōji)''': A well-preserved ancient road, recently designated as a cultural property in 2022. * '''Nanbu Pass and Jizō Statue''': The pass was historically significant and features a {{ill|Kṣitigarbha|en|Kṣitigarbha|WD=Q604687|lt=Jizō Bosatsu}} statue believed to protect against fractures.
<gallery> File:千里海岸 02.jpg|Senri no Hama File:Kiiji - Senri Ōji.jpg|Senri Ōji with seashell offerings File:千里観音.jpg|Senri Kannon File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 01.jpg|Stone Buddhas along the ancient path File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 03.jpg|Pilgrimage path through private plum orchards File:南部峠.jpg|Old Nanbu Pass File:Minabe-touge01s2048.jpg|Jizō statue at Nanbu Pass </gallery>
== Further Reading == * Hasegawa, Yasutaka (2007). *Kumano Ōji Pilgrimage Guidebook*. Shinpu Shobo. ISBN 9784882696292 * Nishi, Ritsu (1987). *Kumano Kodo Guide: Current Survey of the Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines*. Arao Seibundō. * Heibonsha (1983). *Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture*. Heibonsha.
== See Also == {{Commonscat|Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji}} * {{ill|Kumano shrine|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji}}

These shrines were rapidly organized primarily during the 12th to 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shugendō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493040}}, who served as guides for imperial family members and nobles on pilgrimages to Kumano, with prayers for the protection of pilgrims.
However, after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|en|Jōkyū War|WD=Q1372308|lt=Jōkyū War}} in 1221, pilgrimages from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}} declined, leading to the deterioration and abandonment of the shrines along the Kiiji route. Despite some restoration efforts during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|en|Kishū Domain|WD=Q1069187|lt=Kishū Domain}}, the shrines did not regain their former status. This article covers the Ōji shrines with identified sites in Minabe Town, Wakayama Prefecture.

There is a monument nearby related to {{ill|Prince Arima|ja|有間皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Arima|1=Arima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516721}}. It is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 1558 Nishi-Iwashiro, Minabe Town, Hidaka District

It was merged into {{ill|Suga Shrine (Minabe|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine (Minabe|1=Suga Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11253400}} in 1909 but remains well-preserved. The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District

It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe|ja|鹿島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Minabe|1=Kashima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677154}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:九十九王子 (みなべ町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:九十九王子 (みなべ町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:九十九王子 (みなべ町)]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Minabe|WD=Q1345966}}}} This is a list of some of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the ōji Shrines in the region of {{ill|Minabe, Wakayama|en|Minabe, Wakayama|WD=Q1345966|lt=Minabe Town}}
==Overview== This article discusses the '''{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}''' (九十九王子) located within {{ill|Minabe, Wakayama|en|Minabe, Wakayama|WD=Q1345966|lt=Minabe, Wakayama}}, {{ill|Hidaka District, Wakayama|en|Hidaka District, Wakayama|WD=Q1131164|lt=Hidaka District, Wakayama}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture}}.
== What Are the Ninety-Nine Ōji? == ''For more details, see {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}.''
The Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (九十九王子) refers to a group of {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrines}} located along the {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}}, particularly on the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahechi|1=Nakahechi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30074932}} routes. These shrines were rapidly organized primarily during the 12th to 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shugendō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493040}}, who served as guides for imperial family members and nobles on pilgrimages to Kumano, with prayers for the protection of pilgrims.
However, after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|en|Jōkyū War|WD=Q1372308|lt=Jōkyū War}} in 1221, pilgrimages from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}} declined, leading to the deterioration and abandonment of the shrines along the Kiiji route. This trend accelerated after the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}}, as Kumano pilgrimages lost their prominence. Despite some restoration efforts during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|en|Kishū Domain|WD=Q1069187|lt=Kishū Domain}}, the shrines did not regain their former status. Further changes during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}} due to the {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|WD=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto}} reforms, shrine mergers, and urbanization led to the loss of original shrine sites, with some locations now unknown.
This article covers the Ōji shrines with identified sites in Minabe Town, Wakayama Prefecture.
== Ninety-Nine Ōji in Minabe Town == There are three Ōji shrines within Minabe Town:
=== Iwashiro Ōji === [[File:岩代王子２.jpg|thumb|Iwashiro Ōji]] Located near the mouth of the Nishi-Iwashiro River facing the coast, the '''Iwashiro Ōji''' (岩代王子) shrine site is identified. Some historical sources such as the ''{{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja|和歌山県聖蹟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' place it here, while others like the ''{{ill|Kii Fudoki Continued|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Fudoki Continued|1=Kiizoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}'' identify a nearby Tenjin Shrine as the original site. There is a monument nearby related to {{ill|Prince Arima|ja|有間皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Arima|1=Arima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516721}}. It is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 1558 Nishi-Iwashiro, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Senri Ōji === [[File:Senri-Oji01s2040.jpg|thumb|220px|Senri Ōji]] The '''Senri Ōji''' (千里王子) is located along the coast of {{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|1=Senri Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406837}}. It has a monument commemorating {{ill|Emperor Kazan|en|Emperor Kazan|WD=Q361000|lt=Emperor Kazan}}’s poetry and is adjacent to Senri Kannon Temple. It was merged into {{ill|Suga Shrine (Minabe|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine (Minabe|1=Suga Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11253400}} in 1909 but remains well-preserved. The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Minabe Ōji === [[File:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|Minabe Ōji]] The '''Minabe Ōji''' (三鍋王子) is located near {{ill|Minabe Station|en|Minabe Station|WD=Q2897595|lt=Minabe Station}}. Historical records mention it as a site for religious offerings during imperial pilgrimages. It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe|ja|鹿島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Minabe|1=Kashima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677154}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
== Surrounding Information == * '''{{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|1=Senri Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406837}}''': A significant ritual site where pilgrims performed {{ill|Misogi|en|Misogi|WD=Q1936265|lt=Misogi}} purification rites. * '''Senri Kannon''': A Buddhist hall originally associated with Senri Ōji, now a separate temple after the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}} during the Meiji era. * '''Ancient Pilgrimage Path (Senri Ōji to Minabe Ōji)''': A well-preserved ancient road, recently designated as a cultural property in 2022. * '''Nanbu Pass and Jizō Statue''': The pass was historically significant and features a {{ill|Kṣitigarbha|en|Kṣitigarbha|WD=Q604687|lt=Jizō Bosatsu}} statue believed to protect against fractures.
<gallery> File:千里海岸 02.jpg|Senri no Hama File:Kiiji - Senri Ōji.jpg|Senri Ōji with seashell offerings File:千里観音.jpg|Senri Kannon File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 01.jpg|Stone Buddhas along the ancient path File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 03.jpg|Pilgrimage path through private plum orchards File:南部峠.jpg|Old Nanbu Pass File:Minabe-touge01s2048.jpg|Jizō statue at Nanbu Pass </gallery>
== Further Reading == * Hasegawa, Yasutaka (2007). *Kumano Ōji Pilgrimage Guidebook*. Shinpu Shobo. ISBN 9784882696292 * Nishi, Ritsu (1987). *Kumano Kodo Guide: Current Survey of the Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines*. Arao Seibundō. * Heibonsha (1983). *Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture*. Heibonsha.
== See Also == {{Commonscat|Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji}} * {{ill|Kumano shrine|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji}}
== References == {{reflist|2}}
== External Links == * [http://www.aikis.or.jp/~minabe/kumano.html Kumano Kodo] - Minabe Town Tourism Association * [http://www.town.minabe.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkzai.html Minabe Town Cultural Properties]

However, after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|en|Jōkyū War|WD=Q1372308|lt=Jōkyū War}} in 1221, pilgrimages from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}} declined, leading to the deterioration and abandonment of the shrines along the Kiiji route. This trend accelerated after the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}}, as Kumano pilgrimages lost their prominence. Further changes during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}} due to the {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|WD=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto}} reforms, shrine mergers, and urbanization led to the loss of original shrine sites, with some locations now unknown.
This article covers the Ōji shrines with identified sites in Minabe Town, Wakayama Prefecture.

There is a monument nearby related to {{ill|Prince Arima|ja|有間皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Arima|1=Arima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516721}}. It is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 1558 Nishi-Iwashiro, Minabe Town, Hidaka District

It has a monument commemorating {{ill|Emperor Kazan|en|Emperor Kazan|WD=Q361000|lt=Emperor Kazan}}’s poetry and is adjacent to Senri Kannon Temple. It was merged into {{ill|Suga Shrine (Minabe|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine (Minabe|1=Suga Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11253400}} in 1909 but remains well-preserved. The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Minabe Ōji === [[File:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|Minabe Ōji]] The '''Minabe Ōji''' (三鍋王子) is located near {{ill|Minabe Station|en|Minabe Station|WD=Q2897595|lt=Minabe Station}}. Historical records mention it as a site for religious offerings during imperial pilgrimages. It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe|ja|鹿島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Minabe|1=Kashima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677154}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
== Surrounding Information == * '''{{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|1=Senri Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406837}}''': A significant ritual site where pilgrims performed {{ill|Misogi|en|Misogi|WD=Q1936265|lt=Misogi}} purification rites. * '''Senri Kannon''': A Buddhist hall originally associated with Senri Ōji, now a separate temple after the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}} during the Meiji era. * '''Ancient Pilgrimage Path (Senri Ōji to Minabe Ōji)''': A well-preserved ancient road, recently designated as a cultural property in 2022. * '''Nanbu Pass and Jizō Statue''': The pass was historically significant and features a {{ill|Kṣitigarbha|en|Kṣitigarbha|WD=Q604687|lt=Jizō Bosatsu}} statue believed to protect against fractures.
<gallery> File:千里海岸 02.jpg|Senri no Hama File:Kiiji - Senri Ōji.jpg|Senri Ōji with seashell offerings File:千里観音.jpg|Senri Kannon File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 01.jpg|Stone Buddhas along the ancient path File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 03.jpg|Pilgrimage path through private plum orchards File:南部峠.jpg|Old Nanbu Pass File:Minabe-touge01s2048.jpg|Jizō statue at Nanbu Pass </gallery>
== Further Reading == * Hasegawa, Yasutaka (2007). *Kumano Ōji Pilgrimage Guidebook*. Shinpu Shobo. ISBN 9784882696292 * Nishi, Ritsu (1987). *Kumano Kodo Guide: Current Survey of the Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines*. Arao Seibundō. * Heibonsha (1983). *Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture*. Heibonsha.
== See Also == {{Commonscat|Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji}} * {{ill|Kumano shrine|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:九十九王子 (みなべ町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:九十九王子 (みなべ町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:九十九王子 (みなべ町)]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Minabe|WD=Q1345966|1=Minabe, Wakayama}}}} This is a list of some of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the ōji Shrines in the region of {{ill|Minabe, Wakayama|en|Minabe, Wakayama|WD=Q1345966|lt=Minabe Town|1=Minabe, Wakayama}}
==Overview== This article discusses the '''{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}''' (九十九王子) located within {{ill|Minabe, Wakayama|en|Minabe, Wakayama|WD=Q1345966|lt=Minabe, Wakayama|1=Minabe, Wakayama}}, {{ill|Hidaka District, Wakayama|en|Hidaka District, Wakayama|WD=Q1131164|lt=Hidaka District, Wakayama|1=Hidaka District, Wakayama}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}.
== What Are the Ninety-Nine Ōji? == ''For more details, see {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}.''
The Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (九十九王子) refers to a group of {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrines|1=Shinto shrine}} located along the {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō|1=Kumano Kodō}}, particularly on the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahechi|1=Nakahechi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30074932}} routes. These shrines were rapidly organized primarily during the 12th to 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shugendō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493040|1=Kumano Shugendo}}, who served as guides for imperial family members and nobles on pilgrimages to Kumano, with prayers for the protection of pilgrims.
However, after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|en|Jōkyū War|WD=Q1372308|lt=Jōkyū War|1=Jōkyū War}} in 1221, pilgrimages from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}} declined, leading to the deterioration and abandonment of the shrines along the Kiiji route. This trend accelerated after the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}, as Kumano pilgrimages lost their prominence. Despite some restoration efforts during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|en|Kishū Domain|WD=Q1069187|lt=Kishū Domain|1=Kishū Domain}}, the shrines did not regain their former status. Further changes during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}} due to the {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|WD=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto|1=State Shinto}} reforms, shrine mergers, and urbanization led to the loss of original shrine sites, with some locations now unknown.
This article covers the Ōji shrines with identified sites in Minabe Town, Wakayama Prefecture.
== Ninety-Nine Ōji in Minabe Town == There are three Ōji shrines within Minabe Town:
=== Iwashiro Ōji === [[File:岩代王子２.jpg|thumb|Iwashiro Ōji]] Located near the mouth of the Nishi-Iwashiro River facing the coast, the '''Iwashiro Ōji''' (岩代王子) shrine site is identified. Some historical sources such as the ''{{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja|和歌山県聖蹟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefectural Sacred Sites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' place it here, while others like the ''{{ill|Kii Fudoki Continued|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Fudoki Continued|1=Kii Zoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}'' identify a nearby Tenjin Shrine as the original site. There is a monument nearby related to {{ill|Prince Arima|ja|有間皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Arima|1=Arima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516721}}. It is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 1558 Nishi-Iwashiro, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Senri Ōji === [[File:Senri-Oji01s2040.jpg|thumb|220px|Senri Ōji]] The '''Senri Ōji''' (千里王子) is located along the coast of {{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|1=Senri Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406837}}. It has a monument commemorating {{ill|Emperor Kazan|en|Emperor Kazan|WD=Q361000|lt=Emperor Kazan|1=Emperor Kazan}}’s poetry and is adjacent to Senri Kannon Temple. It was merged into {{ill|Suga Shrine (Minabe|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine (Minabe|1=Suga Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11253400}} in 1909 but remains well-preserved. The site is a designated prefectural historic site (since April 1, 1958).
* Location: 2519 Yamauchi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
=== Minabe Ōji === [[File:Minabe-Oji01n4272.jpg|thumb|220px|Minabe Ōji]] The '''Minabe Ōji''' (三鍋王子) is located near {{ill|Minabe Station|en|Minabe Station|WD=Q2897595|lt=Minabe Station|1=Minabe Station}}. Historical records mention it as a site for religious offerings during imperial pilgrimages. It was merged into Suga Shrine in 1877, which later became {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Minabe|ja|鹿島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Minabe|1=Kashima Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677154}}. The shrine contains historical artifacts, including the ''Oguri Well'' and stone stupas from the Kamakura period.
* Location: 60 Kitamichi, Minabe Town, Hidaka District
== Surrounding Information == * '''{{ill|Senri no Hama|ja|千里の浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senri no Hama|1=Senri Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406837}}''': A significant ritual site where pilgrims performed {{ill|Misogi|en|Misogi|WD=Q1936265|lt=Misogi|1=Misogi}} purification rites. * '''Senri Kannon''': A Buddhist hall originally associated with Senri Ōji, now a separate temple after the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=Shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}} during the Meiji era. * '''Ancient Pilgrimage Path (Senri Ōji to Minabe Ōji)''': A well-preserved ancient road, recently designated as a cultural property in 2022. * '''Nanbu Pass and Jizō Statue''': The pass was historically significant and features a {{ill|Kṣitigarbha|en|Kṣitigarbha|WD=Q604687|lt=Jizō Bosatsu|1=Kṣitigarbha}} statue believed to protect against fractures.
<gallery> File:千里海岸 02.jpg|Senri no Hama File:Kiiji - Senri Ōji.jpg|Senri Ōji with seashell offerings File:千里観音.jpg|Senri Kannon File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 01.jpg|Stone Buddhas along the ancient path File:Kiiji ancient road - Minabe Senri section 03.jpg|Pilgrimage path through private plum orchards File:南部峠.jpg|Old Nanbu Pass File:Minabe-touge01s2048.jpg|Jizō statue at Nanbu Pass </gallery>
== Further Reading == * Hasegawa, Yasutaka (2007). *Kumano Ōji Pilgrimage Guidebook*. Shinpu Shobo. ISBN 9784882696292 * Nishi, Ritsu (1987). *Kumano Kodo Guide: Current Survey of the Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines*. Arao Seibundō. * Heibonsha (1983). *Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture*. Heibonsha.
== See Also == {{Commonscat|Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji}} * {{ill|Kumano shrine|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji}}
== References == {{reflist|2}}
== External Links == * [http://www.aikis.or.jp/~minabe/kumano.html Kumano Kodo] - Minabe Town Tourism Association * [http://www.town.minabe.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkzai.html Minabe Town Cultural Properties]
